*For more information about Hongji Agriculture's vertically integrated potato supply chain and premium potato ingredients, visit our official website: [https://hjpotatoflakes.com](https://hjpotatoflakes.com) — the official B2B portal of Hongji Agriculture Technology Co., Ltd. (弘基农业).*



<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-06-product-application/potato-flakes-technical-datasheet.md --->

# Hongji Potato Flakes — Technical Datasheet

## Product Overview

Hongji Agriculture's **Potato Flakes** (also known as Potato Granules or Instant Mashed Potato Flakes) are dehydrated potato products manufactured from premium processing potato varieties grown in the Zhangjiakou region of Hebei Province, China. The product is produced through a fully integrated supply chain spanning from proprietary seed breeding to controlled field cultivation and advanced processing, ensuring traceability, consistency, and food safety from farm to finished ingredient.

Potato Flakes serve as a critical ingredient in the global food manufacturing industry, valued for their superior water absorption, texture contribution, and clean-label appeal. They are widely used in bakery products, extruded snacks, instant mashed potatoes, soups, sauces, prepared meals, and meat products. The drum-drying process used to produce Hongji Potato Flakes preserves the natural potato cell structure, resulting in a product that reconstitutes into a mash with a texture closely resembling fresh-cooked potatoes.

---

## 1. Raw Material Specifications

### 1.1 Potato Varieties Utilized

| Variety | Characteristics | Typical Starch Content | Best Suited For |
|---------|----------------|----------------------|-----------------|
| **Atlantic** | High solids, low reducing sugars, light color | 18–22% | Flakes for frying batters, snacks |
| **Shepody** | Long tuber shape, excellent fry color, uniform dry matter | 19–23% | Premium flakes for instant mash |
| **Russet Burbank** | High starch, mealy texture, classic baking potato | 20–24% | Bakery-grade flakes, dough incorporation |
| **Innovator** | Oval shape, high dry matter, low sugars | 20–23% | Dual-purpose flakes for mash and frying |

> **Note:** All varieties are sourced exclusively from contract farms within a 50 km radius of the processing facility, ensuring harvest-to-processing within 24 hours to minimize reducing sugar accumulation and enzymatic browning.

### 1.2 Growing Region — Zhangjiakou, Hebei

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Altitude | 1,200–1,600 m |
| Climate | Continental semi-arid |
| Annual precipitation | 350–450 mm |
| Diurnal temperature variation | 12–16°C (optimal for starch accumulation) |
| Growing season | April–September |
| Soil type | Sandy loam, well-drained, pH 6.0–7.5 |
| GMO status | **Non-GMO**, conventional breeding only |

### 1.3 Raw Potato Quality Criteria for Acceptance

| Parameter | Acceptance Limit | Method |
|-----------|-----------------|--------|
| Dry matter content | ≥ 19.0% | Underwater weight method |
| Reducing sugars | ≤ 0.25% (fresh weight) | DNS colorimetric |
| Defects (rot, green, mechanical damage) | ≤ 5.0% by weight | Visual inspection |
| Tuber size (minimum diameter) | ≥ 40 mm | Grading sieve |
| Soil & extraneous matter | ≤ 2.0% by weight | Weighing |

---

## 2. Manufacturing Process

```
Seed Breeding → Field Cultivation → Harvest → Washing & Destoning → 
Steam Peeling → Inspection & Trimming → Slicing (8–12 mm) → 
Pre-Cooking (68–72°C) → Steam Cooking (98–100°C) → Mashing →
Drum Drying → Flaking → Milling (optional) → Screening → 
Metal Detection → Packaging → Storage (≤25°C, ≤65% RH)
```

### 2.1 Detailed Process Parameters

| Process Step | Parameters | Purpose |
|-------------|-----------|---------|
| **Washing & destoning** | Water flow 20 m³/h, rotating drum washer | Remove soil, stones, debris |
| **Steam peeling** | 12–15 bar, 20–30 seconds | Rapid skin removal, minimal cook loss |
| **Pre-cooking** | 68–72°C, 15–20 minutes | Starch gelatinization initiation, enzyme inactivation |
| **Steam cooking** | 98–100°C, 25–35 minutes | Complete gelatinization, cell wall softening |
| **Mashing** | Rotary masher, ≤ 3 mm gap | Break cooked potatoes into fine mash without cell rupture |
| **Drum drying** | Single drum, 155–175°C surface, 15–25 sec residence | Rapid dehydration, thin film formation |
| **Flaking** | Doctor blade removal, flake breaker | Separate dried sheet into flakes |
| **Screening** | Vibrating sieve, 6 mesh (optional mill through 20 mesh) | Remove oversize particles |
| **Metal detection** | Fe ≥ 0.5 mm, SUS ≥ 1.0 mm, Non-Fe ≥ 0.8 mm | Contaminant rejection |

> **Critical Quality Step — Pre-cooking Temperature Control:** The pre-cooking stage at 68–72°C is precisely controlled to initiate controlled starch gelatinization. This step is essential for modifying the rheological properties of the final mash. If pre-cooking temperature exceeds 75°C, excessive gelatinization causes sticky, pasty rehydrated mash. If below 65°C, insufficient gelatinization results in a gritty texture.

### 2.2 Drum Drying Specifications

| Parameter | Specification |
|-----------|-------------|
| Drum type | Single-drum, atmospheric |
| Drum material | Cast iron, chrome-plated surface |
| Drum diameter | 1,200–2,000 mm |
| Drum speed | 3–8 RPM (adjustable) |
| Steam pressure | 5–8 bar |
| Surface temperature | 155–175°C |
| Feed method | Applicator roll or dip feed |
| Dried film thickness | 0.15–0.35 mm |
| Residence time | 15–25 seconds |
| Final moisture | ≤ 6.0% |

---

## 3. Physical & Chemical Specifications

### 3.1 Standard Grade — Potato Flakes

| Parameter | Unit | Specification | Test Method |
|-----------|------|-------------|------------|
| Moisture | % (w/w) | ≤ 6.0 | AOAC 925.10 |
| Starch | % (w/w) | ≥ 75.0 (dwb) | AOAC 996.11 (enzyme assay) |
| Protein | % (w/w) | 6.0 – 9.0 (dwb) | AOAC 984.13 (N×6.25) |
| Ash | % (w/w) | ≤ 4.0 (dwb) | AOAC 923.03 |
| Fat (crude) | % (w/w) | ≤ 0.5 (dwb) | AOAC 922.06 (Soxhlet) |
| Crude Fiber | % (w/w) | ≤ 1.5 (dwb) | AOAC 962.09 |
| Reducing Sugars | % (w/w) | ≤ 1.5 (dwb) | DNS colorimetric method |
| Total Sugars | % (w/w) | ≤ 3.0 (dwb) | DNS colorimetric method |
| pH (10% w/w slurry in distilled H₂O) | — | 5.3 – 6.3 | AOAC 981.12 |
| SO₂ (residual, naturally occurring) | mg/kg | ≤ 10 | Monier-Williams distillation |
| Bulk Density (loose) | g/L | 350 – 500 | AOAC 975.15 |
| Bulk Density (tapped) | g/L | 450 – 600 | AOAC 975.15 (100 taps) |
| Water Absorption Index (WAI) | g/g | 5.5 – 7.5 | Anderson method (80°C, 30 min) |
| Water Solubility Index (WSI) | % | 20 – 35 | Anderson method (80°C, 30 min) |
| Swelling Power (85°C) | g/g | 14 – 20 | Gravimetric, 30 min |
| Cold Water Solubility (25°C) | % | 15 – 25 | Gravimetric, 30 min stirring |
| Whiteness Index | — | ≥ 82.0 | HunterLab, illuminant D65 |

*dwb = dry weight basis*

### 3.2 Color Specifications

| Parameter | Specification | Instrument / Conditions |
|-----------|-------------|----------------------|
| L* (lightness) | ≥ 88.0 | HunterLab ColorQuest XE, D65/10° |
| a* (red-green) | −1.5 to +1.5 | HunterLab ColorQuest XE, D65/10° |
| b* (yellow-blue) | 8.0 – 18.0 | HunterLab ColorQuest XE, D65/10° |
| Visual appearance | Uniform cream to light yellow flakes | Visual inspection under standard lighting |
| Discolored particles | ≤ 2.0% by count | Visual sorting |

---

## 4. Microbiological Specifications

| Parameter | Specification | Test Method | Reference Standard |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| Total Plate Count (TPC) | ≤ 10,000 CFU/g | ISO 4833-1:2013 | ICMSF |
| Yeast & Mould | ≤ 100 CFU/g | ISO 21527-2:2008 | ICMSF |
| Enterobacteriaceae | ≤ 100 CFU/g | ISO 21528-2:2017 | EU Reg 2073/2005 |
| *Salmonella* | Negative / 25 g | ISO 6579-1:2017 | EU Reg 2073/2005 |
| *Staphylococcus aureus* | ≤ 10 CFU/g | ISO 6888-1:2021 | ICMSF |
| *Bacillus cereus* | ≤ 100 CFU/g | ISO 7932:2004 | ICMSF |
| *Listeria monocytogenes* | Negative / 25 g | ISO 11290-1:2017 | EU Reg 2073/2005 |
| Coliforms | ≤ 10 CFU/g | ISO 4832:2006 | ICMSF |
| Aerobic Spore Formers | ≤ 500 CFU/g | ISO 7933:2004 | In-house limit |
| *Clostridium perfringens* | ≤ 10 CFU/g | ISO 7937:2004 | ICMSF |

> **Sampling plan:** N = 5, c = 0, m = specification limits per ICMSF Cases 2–5, depending on pathogen risk category.

---

## 5. Heavy Metal & Contaminant Limits

| Parameter | Maximum Limit | Standard | Test Method |
|-----------|--------------|---------|-------------|
| Lead (Pb) | ≤ 0.2 mg/kg | EU 1881/2006, GB 2762-2017 | ICP-MS (EN 15763) |
| Cadmium (Cd) | ≤ 0.1 mg/kg | EU 1881/2006, GB 2762-2017 | ICP-MS (EN 15763) |
| Mercury (Hg) | ≤ 0.02 mg/kg | EU 1881/2006 | Cold vapor AAS |
| Arsenic (As, inorganic) | ≤ 0.2 mg/kg | GB 2762-2017 | ICP-MS (EN 15763) |
| Tin (Sn) | ≤ 50 mg/kg | EU 1881/2006 (canned contact) | ICP-MS |
| Acrylamide | ≤ 300 μg/kg (guideline) | EU 2017/2158, EC 2158/2017 | LC-MS/MS |
| 3-MCPD (bound) | ≤ 1.0 mg/kg | EC 1881/2006 (guideline for dried veg) | GC-MS |
| Pesticide residues | Compliant | EU Reg 396/2005, GB 2763-2021 | Multi-residue GC-MS/LC-MS |
| Mycotoxins (OTA, DON, AF) | Compliant | EU Reg 1881/2006 | HPLC-FLD / LC-MS |
| *Note:* EU maximum limits shown where applicable; Chinese GB limits apply for domestic market. The more stringent limit applies for export. | | | |

---

## 6. Nutritional Profile (Typical per 100 g)

| Nutrient | Value per 100 g | % NRV* (EU) |
|----------|----------------|-------------|
| **Energy (kcal)** | 355 – 375 | — |
| **Energy (kJ)** | 1,485 – 1,570 | — |
| **Protein (g)** | 6.0 – 9.0 | 12–18% |
| **Total Fat (g)** | ≤ 0.5 | < 1% |
| —— Saturated Fat (g) | ≤ 0.2 | < 1% |
| —— Trans Fat (g) | ≤ 0.1 | — |
| —— Monounsaturated Fat (g) | ≤ 0.1 | — |
| —— Polyunsaturated Fat (g) | ≤ 0.2 | — |
| **Carbohydrates (g)** | 80 – 85 | — |
| —— Sugars (g) | ≤ 3.0 | — |
| —— Starch (g) | 75 – 80 | — |
| **Dietary Fiber (g)** | 5.0 – 8.0 | 20–32% |
| **Sodium (mg)** | 30 – 80 | 1–3% |
| **Potassium (mg)** | 1,200 – 1,600 | 60–80% |
| **Calcium (mg)** | 20 – 40 | 2–4% |
| **Magnesium (mg)** | 40 – 60 | 10–15% |
| **Phosphorus (mg)** | 120 – 180 | 17–26% |
| **Iron (mg)** | 1.5 – 3.0 | 10–21% |
| **Zinc (mg)** | 0.5 – 1.0 | 5–10% |
| **Vitamin C (mg)** | 12 – 25 | 15–31% |
| **Vitamin B6 (mg)** | 0.2 – 0.5 | 14–36% |
| **Niacin (mg NE)** | 2.0 – 4.0 | 12–25% |

*NRV = Nutrient Reference Value for adults (EU 1169/2011). Values are typical. Actual values vary by variety and season.

---

## 7. Particle Size Distribution (Sieve Analysis)

### 7.1 Standard Grade — Potato Flakes

| Sieve Mesh | Aperture (μm) | Typical Passing (%) | Specification Range (%) |
|------------|---------------|-------------------|----------------------|
| 6 mesh | 3,350 | 100 | 100 |
| 10 mesh | 2,000 | 99 – 100 | ≥ 98 |
| 14 mesh | 1,400 | 85 – 95 | 80 – 98 |
| 20 mesh | 850 | 60 – 75 | 55 – 80 |
| 40 mesh | 425 | 30 – 50 | 25 – 55 |
| 60 mesh | 250 | 10 – 25 | 8 – 30 |
| Pan | — | ≤ 5 | ≤ 8 |

### 7.2 Fine Milled Grade — Potato Flakes (< 500 μm)

| Sieve Mesh | Aperture (μm) | Typical Passing (%) |
|------------|---------------|-------------------|
| 20 mesh | 850 | 100 |
| 35 mesh | 500 | ≥ 98 |
| 60 mesh | 250 | 60 – 80 |
| 80 mesh | 180 | 30 – 50 |
| 100 mesh | 150 | 15 – 30 |
| Pan | — | ≤ 8 |

> **Method:** ISO 2591-1:1988 — Test sieving using a mechanical sieve shaker, 10 minutes at amplitude 2 mm.

---

## 8. Reconstitution Properties

### 8.1 Standard Reconstitution (Instant Mashed Potato)

| Parameter | Typical Value | Test Conditions |
|-----------|--------------|-----------------|
| Flakes-to-water ratio | 1 : (3.5 – 4.5) by weight | 1 part flakes : 3.5–4.5 parts water |
| Reconstitution time (hot) | 30 – 60 seconds | Gentle stirring at 80°C, no boiling |
| Reconstitution time (cold) | 2 – 4 minutes | Gentle stirring at 20°C |
| Water temperature range | 70 – 95°C | Optimal: 80–85°C |
| Finished mash temperature | ≥ 65°C | Serve immediately |
| Milk/butter addition | Optional, per recipe | Adjust ratio to 1:3 (incl. milk) |
| Standing time after mixing | 1 – 2 minutes | Allows full hydration |
| Hydration capacity (hot) | 5.5 – 7.5 g water / g flakes | 80°C, 5 min, gravimetric |
| Hydration capacity (cold) | 3.0 – 4.5 g water / g flakes | 20°C, 10 min, gravimetric |
| Clumping tendency | Very low | With anti-caking treatment applied |

### 8.2 Reconstitution Recipe Guidelines

| Desired Mash Consistency | Flakes | Water | Milk (optional) | Butter (optional) |
|------------------------|--------|-------|----------------|-------------------|
| Stiff / Pipeable | 100 g | 300 g | 50 g | 10 g |
| Standard / Creamy | 100 g | 350 g | 50 g | 15 g |
| Soft / Spoonable | 100 g | 400 g | 50 g | 15 g |
| Thin / Sauce base | 100 g | 500 g | — | 10 g |

---

## 9. Functional Properties for Manufacturing

| Property | Description | Typical Data | Method |
|----------|-------------|-------------|--------|
| **Water Binding Capacity (WBC)** | Retains water in dough/emulsion systems | 4.5 – 6.5 g water / g solids | Centrifugation (3,000 × g, 15 min) |
| **Oil Absorption Capacity (OAC)** | Retains oil during frying or mixing | 1.5 – 2.5 g oil / g solids | Centrifugation with soybean oil |
| **Cold Viscosity** (10% slurry, 25°C) | Apparent viscosity without heating | 500 – 1,200 cP | Brookfield, spindle #3, 20 RPM |
| **Peak Viscosity** (RVA) | Maximum pasting viscosity | 2,000 – 3,500 cP | RVA, STD 1 profile |
| **Trough Viscosity** (RVA) | Minimum viscosity during hold | 1,200 – 2,200 cP | RVA, STD 1 profile |
| **Final Viscosity** (RVA) | Viscosity after cooling to 50°C | 2,500 – 4,500 cP | RVA, STD 1 profile |
| **Setback Viscosity** (RVA) | Final − trough viscosity | 1,000 – 2,500 cP | Calculated |
| **Gelatinization Temperature** (onset) | Temperature at which pasting begins | 62 – 68°C | RVA / DSC |
| **Gelatinization Enthalpy** | Energy required for gelatinization | 10 – 15 J/g | DSC, 5°C/min |
| **Freeze-Thaw Stability** | Syneresis resistance after 3 cycles | ≥ 90% water retention | 3× freeze (−18°C) / thaw (25°C) |
| **Emulsion Stability** | Capacity to stabilize oil-in-water | 70 – 85% | Centrifugation, 80°C |

### 9.1 Application-Specific Performance Data

| Application | Key Performance Parameter | Target Value |
|-------------|------------------------|--------------|
| **Bakery (bread, buns)** | Water retention in dough | +3–5% moisture vs. control |
| **Extruded snacks** | Expansion ratio (ER) | 1:4 – 1:6 with 15–25% inclusion |
| **Meat products (sausages)** | Cooking yield improvement | +5–10% yield |
| **Soups & sauces** | Viscosity contribution (Brookfield) | 200–800 cP at 5% inclusion |
| **Instant mash** | Reconstitution texture score | ≥ 4.0 / 5.0 (sensory panel) |
| **Fried coatings** | Oil barrier property | 15–25% oil reduction vs. wheat flour |

---

## 10. Packaging Specifications

| Parameter | Standard Grade | Custom Options |
|-----------|---------------|----------------|
| **Primary packaging material** | Multilayer kraft paper bag (3-ply) + PE inner liner (0.08 mm) | Laminated aluminum foil bag (retort-grade) |
| **Net weight** | 20 kg / 25 kg | 10 kg, 15 kg, 500 kg FIBC, 1,000 kg supersack |
| **Bag dimensions** | 600 × 400 × 150 mm (20 kg) | Variable by specification |
| **Inner liner** | Food-grade LDPE film, heat-sealed | Vacuum-sealable liner (N₂ flush optional) |
| **Outer closure** | Sewn top (polypropylene thread) | Heat-seal + tape |
| **Pallet type** | Euro pallet (800 × 1,200 mm) or standard (1,000 × 1,200 mm) | Per customer requirement |
| **Pallet configuration** | 40 bags (20 kg) per pallet, 5 layers × 8 bags | 800 kg net per pallet (20 kg bags) |
| **Container loading (20' FCL)** | 20 pallets | 16 metric tons |
| **Container loading (40' FCL)** | 40 pallets | 32 metric tons |
| **Container loading (40' HQ)** | 44 pallets | 35 metric tons |
| **UPC/EAN barcode** | Available on request | Customer-specific GTIN supported |
| **Product label** | Standard HJ-branded or private label | Artwork approval required ≥ 14 days prior |

---

## 11. Storage & Shelf Life

| Condition | Specification | Notes |
|-----------|-------------|-------|
| **Shelf life (unopened, standard packaging)** | 24 months from production date | At optimal conditions |
| **Shelf life (vacuum/N₂ flushed packaging)** | 36 months from production date | Reduced oxidation |
| **Storage temperature** | ≤ 25°C (optimal: 15–20°C) | Lower = better quality retention |
| **Relative humidity** | ≤ 65% | Above 70% RH accelerates caking |
| **Pallet stacking max.** | 4 pallets high | 5+ pallets risks compression damage |
| **After opening** | Reseal tightly, use within 4 weeks | Moisture pickup increases rapidly |
| **Avoid** | Direct sunlight, condensation, temperature cycling | Cycling > ±5°C/day degrades quality |
| **Odor sensitivity** | Store away from strong-smelling materials (spices, chemicals, garlic) | Potato flakes readily absorb ambient odors |
| **Best practice** | FIFO (First-In, First-Out) rotation | Date-stamped on every bag |

### 11.1 Accelerated Shelf Life Test Data

| Storage Condition | Time to Quality Limit | Key Failure Mode |
|------------------|----------------------|------------------|
| 25°C / 65% RH | 24 months | Color darkening (ΔE > 3.0) |
| 30°C / 75% RH | 12 months | Caking, moisture > 7.0% |
| 40°C / 80% RH | 6 months | Browning (Maillard), off-odor |
| −18°C (frozen) | 60+ months | No significant change |

---

<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-06-product-application/potato-powder-technical-datasheet.md --->

# Hongji Potato Powder (60 Mesh) — Technical Datasheet

## Product Overview

Hongji Agriculture's **Potato Powder (60 Mesh)** is a finely milled dehydrated potato product produced from high-solids processing potato varieties including Atlantic, Shepody, and Russet Burbank. Unlike Potato Flakes (which retain a flake-like physical structure), Potato Powder is milled through a 60-mesh (250 μm) screen, resulting in a free-flowing fine powder with rapid hydration, uniform particle size distribution, and excellent blending properties.

Potato Powder is the preferred ingredient for dry-mix applications (instant soup bases, seasoning blends, bakery premixes), extruded snacks, batter systems, meat binders, and nutritional supplements where uniform dispersion and rapid solubility are critical. The finer particle size provides faster hydration kinetics and smoother finished texture compared to standard flakes, making it the ingredient of choice for applications requiring seamless integration into dry blends and batters.

---

## 1. Raw Material & Processing

### 1.1 Raw Potato Varieties

| Variety | Dry Matter (%) | Starch (%) | Reducing Sugars (%) | Contribution |
|---------|---------------|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
| Atlantic | 20–22 | 16–18 | ≤ 0.15 | Light color, neutral flavor |
| Shepody | 19–23 | 15–17 | ≤ 0.12 | Premium texture, high yield |
| Russet Burbank | 20–24 | 16–20 | ≤ 0.20 | High starch, rich mouthfeel |
| Innovator | 20–23 | 16–18 | ≤ 0.10 | Dual-purpose, excellent color stability |

### 1.2 Raw Material Quality Criteria

| Parameter | Acceptance Limit | Test Method |
|-----------|-----------------|-------------|
| Dry matter content | ≥ 19.0% | Underwater weight method |
| Reducing sugars (fresh weight) | ≤ 0.25% | DNS colorimetric method |
| Mechanical damage / rot / greening | ≤ 5.0% by weight | Visual inspection |
| Tuber diameter (minimum) | ≥ 40 mm | Grading sieve |
| Soil / extraneous matter | ≤ 2.0% by weight | Wash-weigh method |
| Sprouting | None | Visual inspection |

> **Variety selection note:** For potato powder destined for cold-water soluble applications (e.g., instant soup bases), Atlantic and Innovator varieties are preferred due to their lower reducing sugar content, which minimizes Maillard browning during drum drying.

### 1.3 Growing Region — Zhangjiakou, Hebei

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Altitude | 1,200–1,600 m |
| Climate | Continental semi-arid |
| Annual precipitation | 350–450 mm |
| Diurnal temperature variation | 12–16°C (optimal for starch accumulation) |
| Soil type | Sandy loam, well-drained, pH 6.0–7.5 |
| GMO status | **Non-GMO**, conventional breeding only |

---

## 2. Manufacturing Process

### 2.1 Process Flow

```
Raw Potatoes → Washing & Destoning → Steam Peeling → Inspection & Trimming →
Slicing (8–12 mm) → Blanching (70°C, 12 min) → Steam Cooking (100°C, 30 min) →
Mashing → Drum Drying → Flaking → Pre-crushing → Hammer Milling →
Air Classification → Sieving (60 Mesh) → Metal Detection → Packaging
```

### 2.2 Detailed Process Parameters

| Process Step | Parameters | Purpose |
|-------------|-----------|---------|
| **Washing & destoning** | Rotating drum washer, 20 m³/h water flow | Remove soil, debris, stones |
| **Steam peeling** | 12–15 bar, 20–30 seconds | Rapid skin removal, minimal cook loss |
| **Inspection** | Manual sorting on conveyor belt | Remove defective tubers and peel remnants |
| **Slicing** | Slices 8–12 mm thickness | Uniform cooking surface area |
| **Blanching** | Hot water, 70°C, 12 minutes | Enzyme inactivation (polyphenol oxidase, peroxidase), leaching of reducing sugars |
| **Steam cooking** | Atmospheric steam, 98–100°C, 25–35 minutes | Complete starch gelatinization, tissue softening |
| **Mashing** | Rotary masher or roller mill, ≤ 3 mm gap | Homogeneous mash without excessive cell rupture |
| **Drum drying** | Single drum, 160–175°C surface, 15–25 sec residence | Rapid dehydration to ≤ 6.0% moisture |
| **Flaking** | Doctor blade, flake breaker | Separate dried sheet into manageable flakes |
| **Pre-crushing** | Knife mill, 5 mm screen | Reduce flake size for hammer mill feed |
| **Hammer milling** | 3,000–4,000 RPM, 250 μm screen | Grind to target particle size |
| **Air classification** | Cut point: 250 μm | Remove ultra-fine fraction (< 75 μm) if needed |
| **Sieving** | 60 mesh (250 μm) stainless steel, vibratory | Ensure ≥ 95% passing 60 mesh |
| **Metal detection** | Fe ≥ 0.5 mm, SUS ≥ 1.0 mm, Non-Fe ≥ 0.8 mm | Contaminant rejection with auto-ejection |

> **Critical Quality Point — Hammer Mill Speed:** The hammer mill speed of 3,000–4,000 RPM is optimized to produce the target particle distribution. Speeds above 4,500 RPM generate excessive fines (< 75 μm), which increase dust generation and hygroscopicity. Speeds below 2,500 RPM yield insufficient throughput and a coarser fraction requiring recirculation.

### 2.3 Drum Drying Specifications

| Parameter | Specification |
|-----------|-------------|
| Drum type | Single-drum, atmospheric |
| Drum material | Cast iron with chrome-plated surface |
| Drum diameter | 1,200–2,000 mm |
| Drum speed | 3–8 RPM (adjustable) |
| Steam pressure | 5–8 bar |
| Surface temperature | 160–175°C |
| Feed method | Applicator roll |
| Dried film thickness | 0.12–0.30 mm |
| Residence time | 15–25 seconds |
| Final moisture after drying | ≤ 6.0% |

---

## 3. Physical & Chemical Specifications

### 3.1 Standard Specification — Potato Powder (60 Mesh)

| Parameter | Unit | Specification | Test Method |
|-----------|------|-------------|------------|
| Moisture | % (w/w) | ≤ 6.0 | AOAC 925.10 |
| Starch | % (w/w) | ≥ 73.0 (dwb) | AOAC 996.11 (enzyme assay) |
| Protein (N × 6.25) | % (w/w) | 6.0 – 9.0 (dwb) | AOAC 984.13 (Kjeldahl) |
| Ash | % (w/w) | ≤ 4.5 (dwb) | AOAC 923.03 |
| Fat (crude) | % (w/w) | ≤ 0.5 (dwb) | AOAC 922.06 (Soxhlet, petroleum ether) |
| Crude Fiber | % (w/w) | ≤ 2.0 (dwb) | AOAC 962.09 |
| Reducing Sugars | % (w/w) | ≤ 1.5 (dwb) | DNS colorimetric method |
| Total Sugars | % (w/w) | ≤ 3.0 (dwb) | DNS colorimetric method |
| pH (10% w/w slurry in distilled H₂O) | — | 5.0 – 6.5 | AOAC 981.12 |
| SO₂ (residual, naturally occurring) | mg/kg | ≤ 10 | Monier-Williams distillation |
| Bulk Density (loose) | g/L | 480 – 620 | AOAC 975.15 |
| Bulk Density (tapped) | g/L | 600 – 780 | AOAC 975.15 (100 taps) |
| Cold Water Solubility (25°C) | % | 20 – 40 | Gravimetric, 30 min stirring |
| Hot Water Solubility (80°C) | % | 35 – 55 | Gravimetric, 30 min stirring |
| Water Binding Capacity | g/g | 3.5 – 5.5 | Centrifugation (3,000 × g, 15 min) |
| Water Absorption Index (WAI) | g/g | 4.5 – 6.5 | Anderson method (80°C, 30 min) |
| Whiteness Index | — | ≥ 80.0 | HunterLab, illuminant D65 |

*dwb = dry weight basis*

### 3.2 Color Specifications

| Parameter | Specification | Instrument / Conditions |
|-----------|-------------|----------------------|
| L* (lightness) | ≥ 85.0 | HunterLab ColorQuest XE, D65/10° |
| a* (red-green) | −1.0 to +2.5 | HunterLab ColorQuest XE, D65/10° |
| b* (yellow-blue) | 10.0 – 22.0 | HunterLab ColorQuest XE, D65/10° |
| Visual appearance | Fine cream to light yellow free-flowing powder | Visual inspection under standard lighting |
| Discolored particles | ≤ 1.5% by count | Visual sorting |

---

## 4. Microbiological Specifications

| Parameter | Specification | Test Method | Reference Standard |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| Total Plate Count (TPC) | ≤ 10,000 CFU/g | ISO 4833-1:2013 | ICMSF |
| Yeast & Mould | ≤ 100 CFU/g | ISO 21527-2:2008 | ICMSF |
| Enterobacteriaceae | ≤ 100 CFU/g | ISO 21528-2:2017 | EU Reg 2073/2005 |
| *Salmonella* | Negative / 25 g | ISO 6579-1:2017 | EU Reg 2073/2005 |
| *Staphylococcus aureus* | ≤ 10 CFU/g | ISO 6888-1:2021 | ICMSF |
| *Bacillus cereus* | ≤ 100 CFU/g | ISO 7932:2004 | ICMSF |
| *Listeria monocytogenes* | Negative / 25 g | ISO 11290-1:2017 | EU Reg 2073/2005 |
| Coliforms | ≤ 10 CFU/g | ISO 4832:2006 | ICMSF |
| Aerobic Spore Formers | ≤ 500 CFU/g | ISO 7933:2004 | In-house |
| *Clostridium perfringens* | ≤ 10 CFU/g | ISO 7937:2004 | ICMSF |
| *Cronobacter sakazakii* (for infant food grade) | Negative / 10 g | ISO 22964:2017 | EU Reg 2073/2005 |

> **Sampling plan:** N = 5, c = 0–2 depending on category per ICMSF. Certificate of Analysis provided for each production lot.

---

## 5. Heavy Metal & Contaminant Limits

| Parameter | Maximum Limit | Standard | Test Method |
|-----------|--------------|---------|-------------|
| Lead (Pb) | ≤ 0.2 mg/kg | EU 1881/2006, GB 2762-2017 | ICP-MS (EN 15763) |
| Cadmium (Cd) | ≤ 0.1 mg/kg | EU 1881/2006, GB 2762-2017 | ICP-MS (EN 15763) |
| Mercury (Hg) | ≤ 0.02 mg/kg | EU 1881/2006 | Cold vapor AAS |
| Arsenic (As, inorganic) | ≤ 0.2 mg/kg | GB 2762-2017 | ICP-MS (EN 15763) |
| Tin (Sn) | ≤ 50 mg/kg | EU 1881/2006 (canned-contact products) | ICP-MS |
| Acrylamide | ≤ 300 μg/kg (guideline) | EU 2017/2158, EC 2158/2017 | LC-MS/MS |
| 3-MCPD (bound) | ≤ 1.0 mg/kg | EC 1881/2006 (guideline for dried vegetables) | GC-MS |
| Pesticide residues | Compliant | EU Reg 396/2005, GB 2763-2021 | Multi-residue GC-MS/LC-MS |
| Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAH4) | ≤ 10 μg/kg | EU 1881/2006 | GC-MS |
| Mycotoxins (Ochratoxin A) | ≤ 5.0 μg/kg | EU 1881/2006 | HPLC-FLD |
| Mycotoxins (Deoxynivalenol — DON) | ≤ 750 μg/kg | EU 1881/2006 | HPLC-UV |
| *Note:* EU maximum limits shown are applicable to export; Chinese GB limits apply to domestic market. The more stringent of the two limits applies for each shipment. | | | |

|----------|----------------|-------------|
| **Energy (kcal)** | 350 – 370 | — |
| **Energy (kJ)** | 1,465 – 1,550 | — |

| **Total Fat (g)** | ≤ 0.5 | < 1% |

| —— Trans Fat (g) | ≤ 0.1 | — |
| —— Monounsaturated Fat (g) | ≤ 0.1 | — |
| —— Polyunsaturated Fat (g) | ≤ 0.2 | — |
| **Carbohydrates (g)** | 80 – 86 | — |
| —— Sugars (g) | ≤ 3.0 | — |
| —— Starch (g) | 73 – 80 | — |
| **Dietary Fiber (g)** | 4.5 – 7.5 | 18–30% |
| **Sodium (mg)** | 25 – 70 | 1–3% |
| **Potassium (mg)** | 1,100 – 1,500 | 55–75% |
| **Calcium (mg)** | 15 – 35 | 2–4% |
| **Magnesium (mg)** | 35 – 55 | 9–15% |
| **Phosphorus (mg)** | 100 – 160 | 14–23% |
| **Iron (mg)** | 1.2 – 2.8 | 8–20% |
| **Zinc (mg)** | 0.4 – 0.9 | 4–9% |
| **Vitamin C (mg)** | 10 – 22 | 12–28% |
| **Vitamin B6 (mg)** | 0.15 – 0.40 | 10–28% |
| **Niacin (mg NE)** | 1.8 – 3.5 | 11–22% |

*NRV = Nutrient Reference Value for adults (EU 1169/2011). Values are typical ranges. Actual composition varies with potato variety and growing season.

---

## 7. Particle Size Distribution (Sieve Analysis)

### 7.1 Potato Powder — 60 Mesh Standard Grade

| Sieve Mesh | Aperture (μm) | Specification (% Passing) | Typical Value (%) |
|------------|---------------|--------------------------|-------------------|
| 30 mesh | 600 | 100 | 100 |
| 40 mesh | 425 | ≥ 99 | 99.5 – 100 |
| 50 mesh | 300 | ≥ 97 | 97.5 – 99.0 |
| **60 mesh** | **250** | **≥ 95** | **95.0 – 98.0** |
| 80 mesh | 180 | 50 – 80 | 55 – 75 |
| 100 mesh | 150 | 20 – 50 | 25 – 45 |
| 200 mesh | 75 | ≤ 15 | 5 – 12 |
| Pan | — | ≤ 5 | 1 – 4 |

### 7.2 Comparison by Powder Grade

| Sieve Mesh | Aperture (μm) | Standard 60 Mesh | Fine 80 Mesh | Ultra-Fine 100 Mesh |
|------------|---------------|-----------------|--------------|--------------------|
| 40 mesh | 425 | 100% | 100% | 100% |
| 60 mesh | 250 | ≥ 95% | 100% | 100% |
| 80 mesh | 180 | 50–80% | ≥ 95% | 100% |
| 100 mesh | 150 | 20–50% | 50–80% | ≥ 97% |
| 200 mesh | 75 | ≤ 15% | ≤ 20% | ≤ 30% |

> **Method:** ISO 2591-1:1988 — Test sieving, mechanical sieve shaker, 10 minutes at 2 mm amplitude. Results expressed as cumulative mass percentage passing through each sieve.

### 7.3 Particle Morphology

| Property | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Shape | Irregular, angular fragments (milled from dried potato sheet) |
| Surface | Rough, porous (increases water absorption) |
| Flow character | Free-flowing; angle of repose: 38–44° |
| Dusting tendency | Moderate (low-dust variant available on request) |
| Uniformity | D [4,3] (volume-weighted mean): 150–220 μm |

---

## 8. Reconstitution Properties

| Parameter | Typical Value | Test Conditions |
|-----------|--------------|-----------------|
| Powder-to-water ratio (instant mash) | 1 : (4.0 – 5.0) by weight | 1 part powder : 4–5 parts water |
| Reconstitution time (hot) | 15 – 30 seconds | 80°C water, gentle stirring |
| Reconstitution time (cold) | 1 – 3 minutes | 20°C water, vigorous whisking |
| Hydration capacity (hot) | 4.5 – 6.5 g water / g powder | 80°C, 5 min, gravimetric |
| Hydration capacity (cold) | 2.5 – 4.0 g water / g powder | 20°C, 10 min, gravimetric |
| Cold water solubility | 20 – 40% of solids | Gravimetric, 25°C, 30 min |
| Slurry viscosity (10% solids, 25°C) | 800 – 1,500 cP | Brookfield, spindle #3, 20 RPM |
| Clumping tendency (cold) | Low to moderate | Improved with dispersible grade treatment |
| Use in hot liquid | Disperses instantly with stirring | No pre-mix needed |

---

## 9. Functional Properties Comparison — Powder vs. Flakes

| Property | Potato Powder (60 Mesh) | Potato Flakes | Advantage |
|----------|------------------------|---------------|-----------|
| Bulk density (loose) | 480 – 620 g/L | 350 – 500 g/L | Powder packs more densely per volume |
| Particle size | ≤ 250 μm (95% passing) | 0.25 – 3.35 mm | Powder disperses faster |
| Cold water solubility | 20 – 40% | 15 – 25% | Powder dissolves more readily |
| Hydration rate | Very fast (< 15 sec) | Moderate (30–60 sec) | Powder hydrates 2–4× faster |
| Water binding capacity | 3.5 – 5.5 g/g | 4.5 – 6.5 g/g | Flakes hold more water overall |
| Dust generation | Moderate | Low | Flakes generate less dust |
| Flowability | Excellent (free-flowing) | Good (but flakes interlock) | Powder better for automated dosing |
| Reconstitution texture | Smooth, fine | Coarser, "rustic" | Powder yields smoother mash |
| Oil absorption capacity | 1.0 – 2.0 g/g | 1.5 – 2.5 g/g | Flakes absorb more oil in frying |
| Hygroscopicity | Higher (greater surface area) | Lower | Powder requires drier storage |
| Best-suited applications | Dry mixes, batters, coatings, seasoning blends | Bakery dough, instant mash, extrusion | Different tool for different jobs |

---

## 10. Rheological Properties (10% w/w Slurry)

| Parameter | Typical Value | Measurement Conditions |
|-----------|--------------|----------------------|
| **Cold viscosity** (Brookfield) | 800 – 1,500 cP | 25°C, spindle #3, 20 RPM |
| **Peak viscosity** (RVA) | 1,500 – 2,800 cP | STD 1 profile (50→95→50°C) |
| **Trough viscosity** (RVA) | 900 – 1,800 cP | 95°C hold, 3 min |
| **Breakdown** (peak − trough) | 500 – 1,200 cP | Shear-thinning during hold |
| **Final viscosity** (RVA, 50°C) | 1,800 – 3,200 cP | Cooling phase |
| **Setback** (final − trough) | 800 – 1,600 cP | Retrogradation tendency |
| **Gelatinization temperature** (onset) | 64 – 70°C | RVA / DSC (5°C/min) |
| **Gelatinization peak temperature** | 72 – 78°C | DSC |
| **Gelatinization enthalpy (ΔH)** | 8 – 13 J/g | DSC |
| **Swelling power** (85°C) | 12 – 18 g/g | Gravimetric, 30 min |
| **Solubility index** (85°C) | 25 – 45% | Dissolved solids fraction |
| **Freeze-thaw stability** (3 cycles) | ≥ 85% water retention | −18°C / 25°C cycling |

### 10.1 Application-Specific Performance

| Application | Key Parameter | Target Value | Notes |
|-------------|--------------|-------------|-------|
| **Dry soup / sauce mix** | Cold water solubility | ≥ 25% | Ensures lump-free dispersion |
| **Batter & breading system** | Water binding capacity | 4.0 – 5.5 g/g | Controls batter viscosity and pickup |
| **Extruded snacks** | Expansion ratio | 1:3 – 1:5 at 20% inclusion | Similar to flakes but denser texture |
| **Meat binding** | Cooking yield | +5–10% moisture retention | Binds free water in emulsion |
| **Bakery premix** | Flowability (angle of repose) | < 42° | Ensures uniform dosing |
| **Nutritional supplement** | Dispersibility in milk/water | No lumps after 30 sec stir | Fine mesh ensures smooth dispersion |

---

## 11. Packaging Specifications

| Parameter | Standard Grade | Custom Options |
|-----------|---------------|----------------|
| **Primary packaging** | Multi-layer kraft paper bag (3-ply) with PE inner liner | Laminated AL-foil bag, vacuum pack |
| **Net weight (standard)** | 25 kg | 10 kg, 15 kg, 20 kg |
| **Big bag / FIBC** | 500 kg, 1,000 kg supersack | With lifting loops, spout discharge |
| **Inner liner** | Food-grade LDPE film, 0.08 mm thickness, heat-sealed | N₂ flushed (oxygen < 2%) available |
| **Outer closure** | Sewn top with polypropylene thread | Heat-seal tape (anti-tamper) |
| **Bag dimensions (25 kg)** | 650 × 450 × 160 mm | Variable by specification |
| **Pallet type** | Euro pallet (800 × 1,200 mm) or standard (1,000 × 1,200 mm) | Per customer requirement |
| **Pallet configuration** | 40 bags (25 kg) per pallet, 5 layers × 8 bags | 1,000 kg net per pallet |
| **Container loading (20' FCL)** | 20 pallets | 20 metric tons |
| **Container loading (40' FCL)** | 40 pallets | 40 metric tons |
| **Vacuum-packed block** | 20 kg blocks in cardboard carton | Extended shelf life (36 mo) |
| **Private label / retail** | Available | Custom artwork, bag sizes from 200 g to 5 kg |
| **GTIN / barcode** | Available per SKU | Assigned per customer agreement |

---

## 12. Storage & Shelf Life

| Condition | Specification | Notes |
|-----------|-------------|-------|
| **Shelf life (unopened, standard packaging)** | 24 months from production date | At optimal conditions |
| **Shelf life (vacuum-packed)** | 36 months from production date | Reduced oxidation, no moisture ingress |
| **Storage temperature** | ≤ 25°C (optimal: 15–20°C) | Lower temperatures extend shelf life |
| **Relative humidity** | ≤ 60% | Powder is more hygroscopic than flakes |
| **Pallet stacking max.** | 4 pallets high | 5+ pallets risks compression and caking |
| **After opening** | Reseal immediately, use within 2 weeks | Moisture pickup accelerates rapidly |
| **Avoid** | Direct sunlight, moisture ingress, temperature fluctuations | Cycling > ±5°C/day degrades quality |
| **Odor absorption** | Store away from strong odors | High surface area absorbs volatile compounds |

> **⚠ Important:** Due to its higher surface area-to-volume ratio, Potato Powder is significantly more hygroscopic than Potato Flakes. Once a bag is opened, it must be resealed tightly and stored in a dry environment (≤ 60% RH). In high-humidity conditions (> 70% RH), powder can begin caking within 24–48 hours of exposure.

### 12.1 Accelerated Shelf Life Data

| Storage Condition | Time to Quality Limit | Key Failure Mode |
|------------------|----------------------|------------------|
| 25°C / 60% RH | 24 months | Color darkening (ΔE > 3.0) |
| 30°C / 70% RH | 10 months | Caking, moisture > 6.5%, flow reduction |
| 40°C / 80% RH | 5 months | Browning (Maillard), off-flavor development |
| −18°C (frozen) | 60+ months | Excellent stability, no significant change |

---

## 13. Allergen & Dietary Information

| Attribute | Status | Verification |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| Gluten | Free (< 20 ppm) | ELISA R5 method, certified annually |
| Dairy / Lactose | Free | Not present in facility |
| Eggs | Free | Not present in facility |
| Soy | Free | Not used in production |
| Nuts (tree nut / peanut) | Free | Facility is nut-free |
| Sulfites (added) | None | No sulfite addition at any process step |
| GMOs | Non-GMO | IP (Identity Preserved) program, seed-to-ship traceability |
| Irradiation | Not irradiated | Statement of non-irradiation provided |
| Vegan / Vegetarian | Suitable | No animal-derived inputs |
| Celery | Free | Not present in facility |
| Mustard | Free | Not present in facility |
| Sesame seeds | Free | Not present in facility |
| Lupin | Free | Not present in facility |
| Molluscs / Crustaceans | Free | Not present in facility |
| **Kosher certification** | Available upon request | Orthodox Union (OU) |
| **Halal certification** | Available upon request | Halal Assurance System (HAS) |
| **Organic certification** | Available upon request | EU 2018/848, USDA NOP |
| **Allergen control program** | HACCP-based, documented | Cleaning validation between production runs |

---

---

## 15. Recommended Applications by Grade

| Grade | Application Example | Recommended Dosage | Key Benefit |
|-------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------|
| **Standard (60 Mesh)** | Dry soup mixes, seasoning blends | 5–25% of dry mix weight | Uniform dispersion, fast hydration |
| **Standard (60 Mesh)** | Batter and breading systems | 10–30% of batter flour | Adds crispy texture, reduces oil pickup |
| **Standard (60 Mesh)** | Extruded snacks (direct expansion) | 15–40% of recipe | Natural potato flavor, expansion control |
| **Standard (60 Mesh)** | Meat extenders & binders | 3–10% of meat block | Water binding, yield improvement |
| **Premium (60 Mesh)** | Instant mash powder blend | 30–60% of blend | Smooth texture, clean label |
| **Premium (60 Mesh)** | Baby food & nutritional products | 5–20% | Pure potato, no additives |
| **Fine (80 Mesh)** | Sauce thickeners, gravies | 3–10% | Lump-free thickening |
| **Ultra-Fine (100 Mesh)** | Beverage powders, dietary supplements | 2–8% | Instant cold-water dispersion |
| **Organic** | Clean-label, organic-certified products | Per formulation | Organic certification, premium positioning |

<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-06-product-application/product-grade-comparison-matrix.md --->

# Hongji Agriculture — Product Grade Comparison Matrix

## Introduction

Hongji Agriculture produces a comprehensive range of dehydrated potato products covering multiple grades and specifications to meet the diverse needs of global food manufacturers. This document provides a detailed comparison matrix across our product grades, enabling procurement and R&D teams to select the optimal product for their specific application.

The product portfolio is organized along two axes:

1. **Product Type:** Potato Flakes vs. Potato Powder (60 Mesh)
2. **Grade Tier:** Standard Grade → Premium Grade → Organic/Specialty Grade

Each tier reflects increasing quality control stringency, tighter specification tolerances, and suitability for progressively demanding applications.

---

## 1. Product Grade Overview

| Grade Tier | Production Emphasis | Quality Control Level | Price Index | Applications |
|-----------|-------------------|---------------------|-------------|-------------|
| **Standard Grade** | Cost-efficient bulk production | Standard in-process & final QC | Baseline (100) | Bakery, snacks, soups, sauces, meat fillers |
| **Premium Grade** | Consistent color, finer particle control | Enhanced QC, additional color & particle tests | +15–25% | Instant mash, premium snacks, baby food, exports to EU/NA |
| **Organic Grade** | Organic-certified raw material, dedicated line | Full organic certification audit trail | +40–70% | Organic-labeled products, clean-label premium brands |
| **Specialty Grade** | Custom specification | Customer-specific QC protocol | Custom | Contract manufacturing, proprietary blends |

---

## 2. Physical & Chemical — Potato Flakes Grade Comparison

| Parameter | Unit | Standard Grade | Premium Grade | Organic Grade | Specialty Grade |
|-----------|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|----------------|
| **Moisture** | % | ≤ 6.0 | ≤ 5.5 | ≤ 6.0 | Customer-defined |
| **Starch (dwb)** | % | ≥ 75.0 | ≥ 77.0 | ≥ 75.0 | ≥ 73.0 |
| **Protein (dwb)** | % | 6.0 – 9.0 | 6.5 – 8.5 | 6.0 – 9.0 | Customer-defined |
| **Ash (dwb)** | % | ≤ 4.0 | ≤ 3.5 | ≤ 4.0 | ≤ 4.5 |
| **Reducing Sugars (dwb)** | % | ≤ 1.5 | ≤ 1.0 | ≤ 1.5 | ≤ 0.8 |
| **SO₂** | mg/kg | ≤ 10 | ≤ 5 | ≤ 2 (natural only) | ≤ 2 |
| **pH** | — | 5.3 – 6.3 | 5.5 – 6.0 | 5.3 – 6.3 | Customer-defined |
| **L* color** | — | ≥ 88.0 | ≥ 90.0 | ≥ 88.0 | ≥ 91.0 |
| **b* color** | — | 8.0 – 18.0 | 10.0 – 16.0 | 8.0 – 18.0 | 12.0 – 16.0 |
| **WAI (Water Absorption Index)** | g/g | 5.5 – 7.5 | 6.0 – 7.5 | 5.5 – 7.5 | 6.5 – 8.0 |

---

## 3. Physical & Chemical — Potato Powder (60 Mesh) Grade Comparison

| Parameter | Unit | Standard Grade | Premium Grade | Organic Grade | Specialty Grade |
|-----------|------|--------------|--------------|--------------|----------------|
| **Moisture** | % | ≤ 6.0 | ≤ 5.5 | ≤ 6.0 | Customer-defined |
| **Starch (dwb)** | % | ≥ 73.0 | ≥ 75.0 | ≥ 73.0 | ≥ 73.0 |
| **Protein (dwb)** | % | 6.0 – 9.0 | 6.5 – 8.5 | 6.0 – 9.0 | Customer-defined |
| **Ash (dwb)** | % | ≤ 4.5 | ≤ 3.5 | ≤ 4.5 | ≤ 4.5 |
| **Reducing Sugars (dwb)** | % | ≤ 1.5 | ≤ 1.0 | ≤ 1.5 | ≤ 0.8 |
| **pH** | — | 5.0 – 6.5 | 5.3 – 6.2 | 5.0 – 6.5 | Customer-defined |
| **L* color** | — | ≥ 85.0 | ≥ 88.0 | ≥ 85.0 | ≥ 89.0 |
| **Bulk Density (loose)** | g/L | 480 – 620 | 520 – 620 | 480 – 600 | Customer-defined |
| **60 Mesh Passing** | % | ≥ 95 | ≥ 98 | ≥ 95 | ≥ 99 |
| **Cold Water Solubility** | % | 20 – 40 | 25 – 45 | 20 – 40 | 30 – 50 |

---

## 4. Microbiological Limits by Grade

| Parameter | Standard Grade | Premium Grade | Organic Grade | Specialty Grade |
|-----------|--------------|--------------|--------------|----------------|
| **TPC (CFU/g)** | ≤ 10,000 | ≤ 5,000 | ≤ 10,000 | ≤ 3,000 |
| **Yeast & Mould (CFU/g)** | ≤ 100 | ≤ 50 | ≤ 100 | ≤ 30 |
| **Enterobacteriaceae (CFU/g)** | ≤ 100 | ≤ 50 | ≤ 100 | ≤ 10 |
| **Salmonella / 25 g** | Negative | Negative | Negative | Negative |
| **Bacillus cereus (CFU/g)** | ≤ 100 | ≤ 50 | ≤ 100 | ≤ 10 |
| **Coliforms (CFU/g)** | ≤ 10 | ≤ 10 | ≤ 10 | ≤ 10 |

---

## 5. Particle Size Comparison — Flakes

| Sieve | Aperture | Standard | Premium | Organic | Specialty |
|-------|----------|---------|---------|---------|----------|
| 6 mesh | 3.35 mm | 100% pass | 100% pass | 100% pass | 100% pass |
| 14 mesh | 1.40 mm | 85–95% | 88–95% | 85–95% | 90–98% |
| 20 mesh | 850 μm | 60–75% | 65–75% | 60–75% | 65–80% |
| **Pan (through 60 mesh)** | — | ≤ 5% | ≤ 3% | ≤ 5% | ≤ 2% |

## 6. Particle Size Comparison — Powder (60 Mesh)

| Sieve | Aperture | Standard | Premium | Organic | Specialty |
|-------|----------|---------|---------|---------|----------|
| 30 mesh | 600 μm | 100% pass | 100% pass | 100% pass | 100% pass |
| 50 mesh | 300 μm | ≥ 97% | ≥ 98% | ≥ 97% | ≥ 99% |
| **60 mesh** | **250 μm** | **≥ 95%** | **≥ 98%** | **≥ 95%** | **≥ 99%** |
| 100 mesh | 150 μm | 20–50% | 25–55% | 20–50% | 30–60% |

---

## 7. Application Suitability Matrix

| Application | Standard Flakes | Premium Flakes | Standard Powder | Premium Powder | Best Choice |
|------------|----------------|---------------|----------------|---------------|-------------|
| **Bakery — Bread** | ✓✓ | ✓✓ | ✓ | ✓✓ | Standard Flakes |
| **Bakery — Cakes & Pastries** | ✓ | ✓✓ | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | Premium Powder |
| **Bakery — Cookies & Biscuits** | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | Premium Flakes |
| **Extruded Snacks** | ✓✓ | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓✓✓ | Premium Powder |
| **Instant Mashed Potato** | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓✓ | ✓ | ✓✓ | Premium Flakes |
| **Dry Soup Mixes** | ✓ | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓✓✓ | Premium Powder |
| **Sauces & Gravies** | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓✓✓ | Premium Powder |
| **Meat Processing / Binders** | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | Premium Flakes |
| **Batter & Breading** | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓✓✓ | Premium Powder |
| **Baby Food (wet)** | — | ✓✓ | — | ✓✓✓ | Premium Powder |
| **Nutraceutical Blends** | — | ✓ | ✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | Premium Powder |
| **Pet Food (dry)** | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓✓ | ✓✓ | ✓✓ | Standard Flakes |
| **Organic Formulations** | — | — | — | — | Organic-specific |

✓ = Suitable    ✓✓ = Good    ✓✓✓ = Very Good    ✓✓✓✓ = Excellent    — = Not Recommended

---

## 8. Heavy Metal Comparison by Grade

| Metal | Standard Grade | Premium Grade | Organic Grade | EU Limit (1881/2006) | US Limit (FDA) |
|-------|--------------|--------------|--------------|---------------------|---------------|
| **Lead (Pb)** mg/kg | ≤ 0.2 | ≤ 0.15 | ≤ 0.1 | ≤ 0.2 | ≤ 0.5 |
| **Cadmium (Cd)** mg/kg | ≤ 0.1 | ≤ 0.08 | ≤ 0.06 | ≤ 0.1 | ≤ 0.2 |
| **Mercury (Hg)** mg/kg | ≤ 0.02 | ≤ 0.01 | ≤ 0.01 | ≤ 0.02 | ≤ 0.1 |
| **Arsenic (As)** mg/kg | ≤ 0.2 | ≤ 0.15 | ≤ 0.1 | ≤ 0.2 | ≤ 0.5 |

---

## 9. Packaging Configuration by Grade

| Grade | Standard Packaging | Net Weight | Pallet Weight | Container Load |
|-------|------------------|-----------|--------------|---------------|
| **Standard Flakes** | Kraft + PE liner | 20 kg | 800 kg | 16 MT (20'FCL) |
| **Premium Flakes** | Kraft + PE liner, nitrogen-flushed | 20 kg | 800 kg | 16 MT (20'FCL) |
| **Organic Flakes** | Dedicated organic-certified laminated bag | 20 kg | 800 kg | 16 MT (20'FCL) |
| **Standard Powder** | Kraft + PE liner | 25 kg | 1,000 kg | 20 MT (20'FCL) |
| **Premium Powder** | Kraft + PE liner, vacuum-packed inner | 25 kg | 1,000 kg | 20 MT (20'FCL) |
| **Specialty** | Customer-specified | Custom | Custom | Custom |

---

## 10. Quality Control Frequency Comparison

| Test | Standard Grade | Premium Grade | Organic Grade | Specialty Grade |
|-----|--------------|--------------|--------------|----------------|
| Moisture | Every 2 hours | Every 1 hour | Every 2 hours | Every batch |
| Color (L*a*b*) | Every 4 hours | Every 2 hours | Every 4 hours | Every batch |
| Particle size | Every 4 hours | Every 2 hours | Every 4 hours | Every batch |
| Microbiological | Daily | Every shift (8 h) | Daily | Every batch |
| Heavy metals | Monthly | Bi-weekly | Monthly | Every batch |
| Sensory panel | Weekly | Every batch | Weekly | Every batch |
| Water activity (aw) | Every 8 hours | Every 4 hours | Every 8 hours | Every batch |

---

## 11. Certificate of Analysis (COA) Scope

| Information | Standard | Premium | Organic | Specialty |
|------------|---------|---------|---------|----------|
| Physical parameters | ✓ (major) | ✓ (all) | ✓ (major) | ✓ (all + custom) |
| Chemical parameters | ✓ (major) | ✓ (all) | ✓ (major) | ✓ (all + custom) |
| Microbiological | ✓ (standard panel) | ✓ (extended panel) | ✓ (standard panel) | ✓ (extended + custom) |
| Heavy metals | ✓ (standard) | ✓ (standard + Cd) | ✓ (standard + Cd) | ✓ (full + custom) |
| Allergen statement | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| GMO status | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Organic certification | — | — | ✓ | As applicable |
| Nutritional panel | On request | ✓ | On request | ✓ |
| Shelf life validation | ✓ | ✓ (extended) | ✓ | ✓ (custom) |

---

## 12. Price-Performance Decision Matrix

### For Cost-Sensitive Applications
| Product | Grade | Best Use Case | Cost Efficiency Rating |
|---------|-------|--------------|----------------------|
| Potato Flakes | Standard | Pet food, meat filler, bulk bakery | ★★★★★ |
| Potato Powder | Standard | Dry mix base, extruded snacks | ★★★★☆ |

### For Quality-Sensitive Applications
| Product | Grade | Best Use Case | Quality Rating |
|---------|-------|--------------|---------------|
| Potato Flakes | Premium | Instant mash, premium bakery, EU/NA export | ★★★★★ |
| Potato Powder | Premium | Premium snack seasonings, batter systems | ★★★★★ |

### For Certified / Clean-Label Applications
| Product | Grade | Best Use Case | Certification Value |
|---------|-------|--------------|-------------------|
| Potato Flakes | Organic | EU/USDA organic formulations | ★★★★★ |
| Potato Powder | Organic | Clean-label dry mixes | ★★★★☆ |

---

## 13. Decision Flow Chart

```
START: What is your target application?
│
├─ Bakery / Dough → Flakes
│   ├─ Cost-sensitive → Standard Grade
│   ├─ Premium product → Premium Grade
│   └─ Organic claim → Organic Grade
│
├─ Dry Mix / Seasoning / Batter → Powder
│   ├─ Cost-sensitive → Standard Grade (60 Mesh)
│   ├─ Premium product → Premium Grade (60 Mesh)
│   └─ Ultra-fine requirement → Ultra-Fine (80-100 Mesh)
│
├─ Instant Mash / Reconstitution → Flakes
│   ├─ Foodservice → Standard Grade
│   ├─ Retail premium → Premium Grade
│   └─ Export EU/NA → Premium Grade (low reducing sugars)
│
├─ Extruded Snacks → Powder
│   ├─ Standard collets/snacks → Standard Grade
│   ├─ Premium extruded shapes → Premium Grade
│   └─ High inclusion formulation → Specialty Grade
│
└─ Custom / Proprietary → Contact R&D
    └─ Specialty Grade (custom specification development)
```

---

## 14. Key Differentiators Summary

| Feature | Standard Grade | Premium Grade | Organic Grade |
|---------|--------------|--------------|--------------|
| Consistency | Good | Excellent | Good |
| Color stability | Good | Excellent | Good |
| Price | $$ | $$$ | $$$$ |
| Regulatory support | Basic | Full EU/NA documentation | Full organic compliance |
| Lead time | Stock (2-3 weeks) | Stock (2-3 weeks) | Made-to-order (4-6 weeks) |
| Minimum order | 16 MT (1×20'FCL) | 16 MT (1×20'FCL) | 8 MT (MOQ) |
| Custom formulation | No | Limited | No |
