<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-06-product-application/application-guide-potato-flakes-bakery.md --->

# Application Guide: Potato Flakes in Bakery Products

## Scope & Objective

This technical application guide provides comprehensive formulation and processing parameters for incorporating Hongji Potato Flakes into bakery products including bread, cakes, pastries, cookies, and biscuits. The guide is intended for R&D teams and production managers at commercial bakeries and industrial bakery ingredient manufacturers seeking to optimize quality, texture, shelf life, and yield through potato flake incorporation.

Potato Flakes offer unique functional benefits in bakery applications: superior water absorption, natural emulsification, starch gelatinization enhancement, and a clean-label profile that meets growing consumer demand for recognizable ingredients.

---

## 1. Functional Benefits of Potato Flakes in Bakery

| Benefit | Mechanism | Impact |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| **Water absorption** | Pre-gelatinized starch absorbs 5.5–7.5× its weight in water | Increased dough yield, softer crumb |
| **Natural emulsification** | Potato proteins stabilize fat-water interfaces | Improved fat distribution, finer crumb structure |
| **Starch contribution** | Native potato starch swells at lower temperature (62–68°C) vs. wheat starch (52–58°C) | Delayed gelatinization, extended oven spring |
| **Humectancy** | Potato fiber retains moisture in finished product | Extended shelf life, reduced staling |
| **Clean label** | Simple ingredient declaration: "dried potato" | No E-numbers required |
| **Color enhancement** | Maillard reaction with reducing sugars | Golden crust color |
| **Flavor profile** | Subtle potato flavor, enhances savory notes | Rounds out overall flavor |

---

## 2. Recommended Inclusion Rates

### 2.1 By Bakery Category

| Product Category | Recommended Addition (% flour weight basis) | Optimal Range | Maximum Recommended |
|-----------------|-------------------------------------------|--------------|-------------------|
| White pan bread | 3 – 8% | 5% | 15% |
| Whole wheat / multigrain bread | 5 – 12% | 8% | 20% |
| Hamburger buns / hot dog buns | 3 – 6% | 5% | 12% |
| Dinner rolls | 5 – 10% | 7% | 15% |
| Croissants / laminated dough | 2 – 5% | 3% | 8% |
| Layer cakes | 5 – 15% | 10% | 25% |
| Pound cakes | 5 – 10% | 8% | 20% |
| Muffins | 8 – 15% | 12% | 25% |
| Cookies (drop) | 5 – 12% | 8% | 20% |
| Cookies (wire-cut) | 3 – 8% | 5% | 15% |
| Biscuits / crackers | 5 – 15% | 10% | 25% |
| Tortillas / flatbreads | 5 – 10% | 7% | 15% |
| Pancakes / waffles | 10 – 20% | 15% | 30% |

> **Note:** Percentages are based on substitution of baker's flour (wheat flour). For optimal results, do not exceed the maximum recommended without process adjustments.

### 2.2 Substitution Strategy

Potato Flakes should be added as a **partial flour replacement** rather than a simple addition. The general substitution equation:

```
Total flour = (100 - S)% wheat flour + (S)% potato flakes by weight
```

Where S = substitution rate (3–20% depending on category).

**Water adjustment:** For every 1% of flour replaced by Potato Flakes, total water should be increased by 0.8–1.2% due to the higher water absorption of pre-gelatinized potato starch.

---

## 3. Formulation Adjustments

### 3.1 Water Absorption Adjustment

Potato Flakes require significantly more water than wheat flour. Use the following guideline:

| Substitution Level (%) | Additional Water (% of total dough) | Dough Hydration Target (%) |
|----------------------|-----------------------------------|--------------------------|
| 0 (control) | 0 | 58 – 60 |
| 3% | 2.0 – 2.5 | 60 – 62 |
| 5% | 3.5 – 4.5 | 62 – 64 |
| 8% | 6.0 – 7.5 | 64 – 67 |
| 10% | 8.0 – 10.0 | 66 – 70 |
| 15% | 12.0 – 15.0 | 70 – 75 |

### 3.2 Fat & Sugar Adjustments

| Parameter | Adjustment | Rationale |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| **Shortening/fat** | Reduce by 5–10% | Potato flakes contribute mouthfeel and tenderness |
| **Sugar** | Reduce by 5–15% | Potato flakes add slight sweetness; Maillard browning increases sweetness perception |
| **Yeast** | Increase by 5–10% for fermented doughs | Potato solids stimulate yeast activity (additional minerals & sugars) |
| **Salt** | Reduce by 5% | Potato naturally contributes sodium (30–80 mg/100g) |

### 3.3 Emulsifier Adjustment

| Emulsifier | Standard Level | With Potato Flakes (5–10%) | Rationale |
|-----------|---------------|--------------------------|-----------|
| DATEM | 0.3 – 0.5% | 0.2 – 0.4% | Reduced need due to potato protein emulsification |
| SSL | 0.25 – 0.5% | 0.15 – 0.35% | Natural emulsification from potato proteins |
| Lecithin | 0.2 – 0.5% | Not required at ≤ 8% | Sufficient natural emulsifiers |
| Mono/diglycerides | 0.3 – 0.6% | 0.2 – 0.5% | Slight reduction possible |

---

## 4. Process Parameters & Adjustments

### 4.1 Dough Mixing — Straight Dough Method

| Parameter | Standard Dough | With Potato Flakes (5–10%) | Notes |
|-----------|---------------|---------------------------|-------|
| **Mixing time (spiral)** | 10–14 min | 8–12 min | Reduced gluten development time |
| **Dough temperature** | 26–28°C | 25–27°C | Potato addition requires slightly lower temp |
| **Water temperature** | 18–22°C | 15–18°C | Compensate for higher hydration |
| **Dough development** | Smooth, elastic | Smooth, slightly softer | Less elastic, more extensible |
| **Overmixing risk** | Moderate | Low | Pre-gelatinized starch resists overmixing |

### 4.2 Dough Mixing — Sponge & Dough Method

| Parameter | Standard | With Potato Flakes (5–10%) | Notes |
|-----------|---------|---------------------------|-------|
| **Sponge hydration** | 60–65% | 65–70% | Increase water in sponge stage |
| **Add potato flakes in** | — | Dough stage only | Avoid in sponge to prevent over-hydration |
| **Water temperature (dough stage)** | 4–8°C | 2–5°C | Compensate for potato flake addition |
| **Floor time** | 20–30 min | 15–25 min | Slightly shorter |

### 4.3 Fermentation

| Parameter | Standard | With Potato Flakes | Change |
|-----------|---------|-------------------|--------|
| **Bulk fermentation (straight dough)** | 60–90 min at 28°C | 50–75 min at 28°C | 10–15% reduction |
| **Proof time (final)** | 50–70 min | 45–60 min | 10–15% reduction |
| **Fermentation tolerance** | Good | Good to excellent | Better moisture retention |
| **Gas retention** | Standard | Slightly improved | Potato starch gels retain CO₂ efficiently |

### 4.4 Baking Parameters

| Product | Standard Temperature | With Potato Flakes | Standard Time | Adjusted Time |
|---------|-------------------|-------------------|--------------|--------------|
| White bread (450g loaf) | 210–220°C | 205–215°C | 30–35 min | 32–38 min (+5-10%) |
| Hamburger buns | 190–200°C | 185–195°C | 14–18 min | 15–20 min |
| Layer cake | 175–185°C | 170–180°C | 25–35 min | 28–38 min |
| Cookies | 180–190°C | 175–185°C | 10–14 min | 11–15 min |
| Biscuits/crackers | 200–220°C | 195–215°C | 8–12 min | 9–13 min |

> **Key adjustments:** Lower oven temperature by 5–10°C and extend baking time by 5–10%. The higher moisture content requires longer baking to achieve the same final moisture level.

---

## 5. Impact on Finished Product Quality

### 5.1 Bread Quality Attributes

| Parameter | 0% Control | 5% Potato Flakes | 10% Potato Flakes | 15% Potato Flakes |
|-----------|-----------|-----------------|------------------|------------------|
| **Loaf volume (mL/g)** | 4.5 | 4.7 (+4%) | 4.6 (+2%) | 4.3 (−4%) |
| **Crumb firmness (N, day 1)** | 1.8 | 1.5 (−17%) | 1.3 (−28%) | 1.1 (−39%) |
| **Crumb firmness (N, day 3)** | 3.5 | 2.8 (−20%) | 2.2 (−37%) | 1.8 (−49%) |
| **Crumb moisture (%)** | 36.0 | 38.5 (+7%) | 40.2 (+12%) | 42.0 (+17%) |
| **Water activity (aw)** | 0.945 | 0.950 | 0.955 | 0.962 |
| **Crust color** | Light golden | Golden | Deep golden | Brown |

### 5.2 Cookie & Biscuit Quality Attributes

| Parameter | 0% Control | 8% Potato Flakes | 15% Potato Flakes |
|-----------|-----------|-----------------|------------------|
| **Spread ratio** | 1.00 (baseline) | 0.93 (−7%) | 0.85 (−15%) |
| **Firmness (N)** | 12.5 | 10.2 (−18%) | 8.8 (−30%) |
| **Moisture (%)** | 3.2 | 4.5 (+41%) | 5.8 (+81%) |
| **Fracturability (mm)** | 3.8 | 4.5 (+18%) | 5.2 (+37%) |

---

## 6. Sample Formulations

### 6.1 Potato-Enriched White Bread

| Ingredient | Control (%) | 5% (%) | 10% (%) |
|-----------|-----------|--------|---------|
| Baker's flour (11.5% protein) | 100.0 | 95.0 | 90.0 |
| **Potato Flakes (HJ-PF-01)** | — | **5.0** | **10.0** |
| Water | 60.0 | 65.0 | 70.0 |
| Fresh yeast | 2.5 | 2.7 | 2.8 |
| Sugar | 5.0 | 4.5 | 4.0 |
| Shortening | 4.0 | 3.8 | 3.6 |
| Salt | 1.8 | 1.7 | 1.7 |
| DATEM | 0.3 | 0.25 | 0.2 |
| Ascorbic acid (ppm) | 100 | 80 | 70 |

**Process:** Straight dough. Mix 4 min low speed + 6 min high speed to full gluten development. Bulk ferment 60 min at 28°C. Punch, rest 15 min. Scale 450 g, mould, proof 55 min at 35°C / 85% RH. Bake 210°C for 32 min.

### 6.2 Potato-Enriched Sandwich Bread (Bun)

| Ingredient | Standard (%) | Optimized (%) |
|-----------|------------|-------------|
| Strong flour | 100.0 | 93.0 |
| **Potato Flakes** | — | **7.0** |
| Water | 56.0 | 63.0 |
| Yeast | 3.0 | 3.2 |
| Sugar | 6.0 | 5.0 |
| Shortening | 5.0 | 4.5 |
| Salt | 1.5 | 1.4 |
| Skim milk powder | 2.0 | 1.5 |
| Monoglyceride | 0.3 | 0.2 |

### 6.3 Potato Cookies

| Ingredient | Control (%) | 8% Potato Flakes |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|
| Soft wheat flour | 100.0 | 92.0 |
| **Potato Flakes (fine milled, HJ-PF-02)** | — | **8.0** |
| Powdered sugar | 45.0 | 40.0 |
| Shortening | 50.0 | 45.0 |
| Whole egg | 15.0 | 15.0 |
| Water | 5.0 | 10.0 |
| Salt | 0.5 | 0.4 |
| Baking powder | 0.3 | 0.3 |
| Vanillin | 0.1 | 0.1 |

### 6.4 Potato Biscuits (Savory Cracker)

| Ingredient | Control (%) | 12% Potato Flakes |
|-----------|-----------|-----------------|
| Crackermeal flour | 100.0 | 88.0 |
| **Potato Flakes (HJ-PF-01)** | — | **12.0** |
| Water | 32.0 | 42.0 |
| Shortening | 8.0 | 7.0 |
| Sugar | 1.5 | 1.0 |
| Salt | 1.2 | 1.1 |
| Yeast (cream) | 0.15 | 0.15 |
| Sodium metabisulfite (ppm) | 50 | 40 |

**Process:** Laminated dough. Mix to 34% moisture dough. Sheeting and lamination (5 layers). Rest 90 min. Cutting. Docking. Bake 220°C for 4 min, then 190°C for 6 min.

---

## 7. Effects on Shelf Life & Staling

### 7.1 Crumb Firming Rate (Bread stored at 20°C)

| Storage Day | Control (0%) | 5% Potato Flakes | 10% Potato Flakes |
|------------|-------------|-----------------|------------------|
| Day 0 | 1.8 N | 1.5 N | 1.3 N |
| Day 1 | 2.5 N | 2.0 N | 1.6 N |
| Day 3 | 3.5 N | 2.8 N | 2.2 N |
| Day 5 | 4.5 N | 3.5 N | 2.7 N |
| Day 7 | 5.2 N | 4.0 N | 3.1 N |

**Conclusion:** Potato Flakes at 5–10% reduce crumb firming by 20–40% over 7 days, extending perceived freshness significantly.

### 7.2 Moisture Retention

| Storage Day | Control (0%) | 5% | 10% |
|------------|-------------|-----|------|
| Day 0 | 36.0% | 38.5% | 40.2% |
| Day 3 | 32.5% | 36.0% | 38.5% |
| Day 7 | 28.0% | 32.5% | 35.8% |

---

## 8. Recommendations for Industrial Bakeries

| Aspect | Recommendation |
|--------|---------------|
| **Pre-hydration** | Pre-blend Potato Flakes with a portion of recipe water (1:3 ratio, 25°C water) and hold 10 min before adding to dough mixer. This ensures even hydration and prevents clumping. |
| **Dosing sequence** | Add pre-hydrated Potato Flake paste in the first minute of mixing (low speed), after flour and before fat. |
| **Gluten compensation** | For bread applications >8% substitution, consider using strong wheat flour (13–14% protein) to maintain dough strength. |
| **Enzyme support** | Consider adding xylanase (20–40 ppm) to counteract the slight increase in water activity. |
| **Freezing tolerance** | Potato flakes enhance freeze-thaw stability. For frozen dough, 5–8% inclusion recommended. |
| **Scale-up factor** | When scaling from bench to industrial mixer, ensure mixing time is reduced by 15–20% to account for faster gluten development. |

---

## 9. Troubleshooting Guide

| Problem | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| **Dough too sticky** | Excessive water; insufficient mixing | Reduce added water by 2–3%; increase mixing time by 1–2 min |
| **Loaf volume too low** | Over-substitution (>15%); weak flour | Reduce to ≤10%; increase flour protein to 13%+ |
| **Dark crust color** | High reducing sugars from potatoes | Reduce oven temp by 10°C; reduce sugar by 10–15%; cover loaf |
| **Gummy crumb** | Under-baked; excessive moisture | Extend baking time by 3–5 min; reduce hydration by 3% |
| **Poor cookie spread** | Too much moisture absorbed | Reduce potato level; increase fat by 5%; reduce water |
| **Off-flavor** | Potato flakes beyond shelf life | Use fresh stock; check storage conditions |
| **Mold growth (early)** | Increased water activity (aw) | Add calcium propionate (0.1–0.2%); reduce aw with humectant |

---

## 10. Potato Flake Selection for Bakery

| Bakery Application | Recommended Item Code | Optimal Particle Size | Key Parameter |
|-------------------|---------------------|---------------------|---------------|
| Bread & rolls | HJ-PF-01 (Standard) | Standard flake (0.25–3.35 mm) | WAI ≥ 6.0 |
| Premium bread | HJ-PF-02 (Fine milled) | < 500 μm | WAI ≥ 6.5, L* ≥ 88 |
| Cakes & pastries | HJ-PF-02 (Fine milled) | < 500 μm | Even dispersion |
| Cookies & biscuits | HJ-PF-02 (Fine milled) | < 500 μm | Low reducing sugars |
| Crackers | HJ-PF-01 (Standard) | Standard flake | Crunchy texture |

---

*Document version: 1.2 | Last updated: July 2026 | Prepared by: Hongji Agriculture R&D — Bakery Application Division*

*For further technical support or custom formulation development, contact: tech@hjpotatoflakes.com*

---

---

*This document is part of the Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) Technical Documentation Series. For more information about our vertically integrated potato supply chain — from seed breeding and cultivation to processing and global export — visit our official B2B website: [https://hjpotatoflakes.com](https://hjpotatoflakes.com)*



<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-06-product-application/application-guide-potato-flakes-snacks-extrusion.md --->

# Application Guide: Potato Flakes & Powder in Snacks & Extrusion

## Scope & Objective

This technical guide provides comprehensive formulation and processing parameters for using Hongji Potato Flakes and Potato Powder in snack food manufacturing, with a focus on direct-expanded (extruded) snacks, formed pellets, fabricated chips, and coated snack applications. The document is intended for R&D teams, process engineers, and production managers in the snack food industry who seek to optimize texture, expansion ratio, flavor delivery, and production efficiency through potato-based ingredient incorporation.

Potato-derived ingredients are fundamental to the snack industry. The pre-gelatinized starch in Potato Flakes and the fine, uniform particle size of Potato Powder (60 Mesh) offer distinct advantages in different snack processing platforms.

---

## 1. Snack Application Overview

| Snack Category | Processing Platform | Recommended Product | Inclusion Rate |
|---------------|-------------------|-------------------|---------------|
| **Direct-expanded collets / puffs** | Single/twin-screw extruder | Potato Powder (60 Mesh) | 15 – 40% |
| **Baked snack pellets (3G/4G)** | Extrusion + drying + frying | Potato Powder (60 Mesh) | 20 – 50% |
| **Fabricated potato chips** | Sheeting + cutting + frying | Potato Flakes (fine milled) | 20 – 45% |
| **Coated / seasoned snacks** | Tumble coating + drying | Potato Powder (60 Mesh) | 5 – 25% (as carrier) |
| **Stuffed snacks** | Co-extrusion | Potato Flakes (standard) | 10 – 30% |
| **Baked crackers / crispbreads** | Sheeting + baking | Potato Flakes + Powder blend | 10 – 35% |

---

## 2. Direct-Expanded Extruded Snacks

### 2.1 Functional Role of Potato Ingredients

In direct-expanded (direct expansion) extrusion, the potato ingredient serves three critical roles:
1. **Starch gelatinization matrix** — Provides the structural network for bubble formation and expansion
2. **Water binding** — Controls moisture delivery to the extrusion barrel
3. **Flavor base** — Delivers authentic potato flavor and browning potential

### 2.2 Recommended Formulations

#### 2.2.1 Potato Puff (High-Inclusion)

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) | Function |
|-----------|--------------|----------|
| **Potato Powder (60 Mesh, HJ-PP-60-01)** | **35.0** | Primary starch source, flavor |
| Corn grits (de-germed, 40 mesh) | 55.0 | Structural base, expansion carrier |
| Potato starch | 5.0 | Expansion booster |
| Vegetable oil | 3.0 | Texture, mouthfeel |
| Salt (fine) | 1.0 | Flavor |
| Sugar | 0.5 | Browning |
| Mono/diglyceride | 0.3 | Emulsification |
| Calcium carbonate | 0.2 | Cell structure refinement |

#### 2.2.2 Cheese-Flavored Potato Pillow

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) | Function |
|-----------|--------------|----------|
| **Potato Flakes (HJ-PF-01)** | **20.0** | Flavor, texture, water binding |
| Corn meal | 50.0 | Structure |
| Rice flour | 15.0 | Crispness |
| Cheddar cheese powder | 8.0 | Flavor |
| Vegetable oil | 5.0 | Texture |
| Salt | 1.5 | Flavor |
| Citric acid | 0.5 | Flavor enhancement |

### 2.3 Extrusion Parameters

| Parameter | Potato Powder (30–40%) | Potato Flakes (15–25%) |
|-----------|----------------------|----------------------|
| **Extruder type** | Twin-screw preferred | Twin-screw required |
| **Barrel temperature profile** | Zone 1: 80–100°C | Zone 1: 70–90°C |
| | Zone 2: 120–140°C | Zone 2: 110–130°C |
| | Zone 3: 140–165°C | Zone 3: 130–155°C |
| | Die: 150–170°C | Die: 140–160°C |
| **Feed moisture** | 14 – 18% | 16 – 22% |
| **Screw speed** | 300 – 500 RPM | 250 – 400 RPM |
| **Feed rate** | 20 – 40 kg/h (per 25 mm barrel) | 18 – 35 kg/h |
| **Die pressure** | 40 – 80 bar | 35 – 70 bar |
| **Die diameter** | 2 – 6 mm (round) | 2 – 8 mm |
| **Expansion ratio (SME dependent)** | 3:1 to 5:1 | 2.5:1 to 4:1 |

### 2.4 Effect of Potato Inclusion on Extrusion Performance

| Parameter | 0% Potato | 20% Potato Powder | 35% Potato Powder | 50% Potato Powder |
|-----------|----------|------------------|------------------|------------------|
| **Expansion ratio** | 4.8:1 | 4.5:1 (−6%) | 4.0:1 (−17%) | 3.2:1 (−33%) |
| **Bulk density (g/L)** | 55 | 65 (+18%) | 80 (+45%) | 110 (+100%) |
| **Piece hardness (N)** | 8.5 | 9.8 (+15%) | 11.5 (+35%) | 14.0 (+65%) |
| **Crispness (acoustic)** | Excellent | Very Good | Good | Moderate |
| **Oil absorption (%)** | 22 | 24 | 27 | 32 |

> **Note:** Higher potato inclusion increases bulk density and hardness while reducing expansion ratio. For maximum expansion (collets), keep potato inclusion ≤ 25%. For a dense, crunchy texture (snack pellets), 35–50% is preferred.

---

## 3. Fabricated (Formed) Potato Chips

### 3.1 Process Overview

```
Dry Ingredients (Potato Flakes + starch + seasonings) → Dry Blend →
Wet Mix (water + emulsifier) → Dough Formation → Sheeting →
Cutting → Frying → Seasoning → Packaging
```

### 3.2 Formulation for Fabricated Chips

| Ingredient | Typical Range (%) | Optimized Formula (%) |
|-----------|-----------------|---------------------|
| **Potato Flakes (fine milled, HJ-PF-02)** | **30 – 45** | **38.0** |
| Potato starch | 5 – 15 | 10.0 |
| Corn starch | 0 – 10 | 4.0 |
| Rice flour | 0 – 10 | 2.0 |
| Water (added) | 30 – 45 | 38.0 |
| Vegetable oil | 2 – 5 | 3.5 |
| Salt | 0.8 – 1.5 | 1.0 |
| Dextrose | 0.5 – 1.5 | 0.8 |
| Lecithin | 0.3 – 0.6 | 0.4 |
| Sodium metabisulfite (ppm) | 20 – 50 | 30 |

### 3.3 Process Parameters

| Parameter | Target Range |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Dough moisture** | 38 – 42% |
| **Dough temperature** | 25 – 30°C |
| **Rest time (dough)** | 10 – 20 min |
| **Sheet thickness** | 1.0 – 1.5 mm |
| **Sheeting pressure** | 5 – 15 kg/cm² |
| **Cutting shape** | Oval / ridged / round |
| **Frying temperature** | 170 – 185°C |
| **Frying time** | 90 – 150 seconds |
| **Final moisture** | ≤ 2.0% |
| **Final oil content** | 28 – 38% |

### 3.4 Chip Quality Parameters

| Parameter | Target | Acceptance Range |
|-----------|--------|-----------------|
| Color (Hunter L*) | ≥ 75 | 70 – 82 |
| Breaking force (N) | 3.5 | 2.5 – 4.5 |
| Oil content (%) | 33 | 28 – 38 |
| Moisture (%) | 1.5 | ≤ 2.0 |
| Soggy pieces (%) | — | ≤ 3% |
| Blisters (%) | — | ≤ 5% |
| Broken pieces (%) | — | ≤ 4% |

---

## 4. Snack Pellet (3G / Third Generation) Technology

### 4.1 Pellet Formulation

| Ingredient | Range (%) | Recommended (%) |
|-----------|----------|---------------|
| **Potato Powder (HJ-PP-60-01)** | **20 – 50** | **35.0** |
| Potato starch | 15 – 30 | 20.0 |
| Tapioca starch | 10 – 25 | 15.0 |
| Corn starch | 5 – 20 | 10.0 |
| Water | 25 – 35 | 30.0 |
| Salt | 0.5 – 1.5 | 1.0 |
| Sugar | 0 – 1.0 | 0.5 |
| Mono/diglyceride | 0.3 – 0.6 | 0.4 |
| Turmeric (color)/Paprika extract | Optional | 0.01 |

### 4.2 Pellet Process

| Stage | Parameter | Value |
|-------|-----------|-------|
| **Extrusion** | Barrel temp | 60 – 90°C (low shear) |
| | Feed moisture | 28 – 35% |
| | Die plate | 2 – 5 mm multi-hole |
| | Screw speed | 100 – 200 RPM |
| **Drying** | Temperature | 55 – 70°C |
| | Target moisture | 10 – 12% |
| | Drying time | 60 – 120 min |
| **Tempering** | Time | 8 – 24 hours |
| | Temperature | 20 – 25°C |
| **Frying (final)** | Temperature | 175 – 190°C |
| | Time | 20 – 40 seconds |
| | Final moisture | ≤ 3% |

---

## 5. Seasoning & Coating Applications

### 5.1 Potato Powder as Seasoning Carrier

Potato Powder (60 Mesh) is an excellent carrier for dry seasoning blends due to its:
- Neutral flavor profile
- High oil absorption (1.5–2.5 g/g)
- Fine particle size for even distribution
- Ability to carry both oil-soluble and water-soluble flavors

### 5.2 Recommended Seasoning Base Blends

#### Potato & Sour Cream Seasoning

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Powder (HJ-PP-60-01)** | **30.0** |
| Buttermilk powder | 25.0 |
| Sour cream powder | 20.0 |
| Salt | 12.0 |
| Onion powder | 5.0 |
| Garlic powder | 3.0 |
| Citric acid | 2.5 |
| Parsley flakes | 1.5 |
| Natural flavor | 1.0 |

#### BBQ Potato Seasoning

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Powder (HJ-PP-60-01)** | **25.0** |
| Sugar (brown) | 20.0 |
| Salt | 15.0 |
| Tomato powder | 12.0 |
| Smoked paprika | 8.0 |
| Onion powder | 6.0 |
| Garlic powder | 4.0 |
| Chili powder | 3.0 |
| Black pepper | 2.0 |
| Citric acid | 2.0 |
| Natural smoke flavor | 1.0 |
| Silicon dioxide (anti-caking) | 2.0 |

### 5.3 Coating Application Parameters

| Parameter | Tumble Drum | Continuous Coater |
|-----------|------------|------------------|
| **Seasoning dosage** | 3 – 8% of snack weight | 4 – 7% |
| **Oil spray level** | 8 – 15% (before seasoning) | 10 – 18% |
| **Drum speed** | 10 – 20 RPM | Variable |
| **Residence time** | 1 – 3 min | 30 – 60 sec |
| **Air flow (for drying)** | Optional | Recommended |

---

## 6. Equipment Considerations

### 6.1 Extruder Requirements

| Parameter | Twin-Screw (Recommended) | Single-Screw (Sufficient for) |
|-----------|--------------------------|------------------------------|
| High potato inclusion (>30%) | ✓ | × (Poor expansion) |
| Potato Flakes (>15%) | ✓ | × (Inconsistent feed) |
| Potato Powder (15–30%) | ✓ | ✓ (With preconditioner) |
| Potato Powder (<15%) | ✓ | ✓ |

### 6.2 Grinding / Particle Size for Snacks

| Application | Recommended Particle Size | Mill Type |
|------------|-------------------------|-----------|
| Extrusion (direct expansion) | 250 – 600 μm (30–60 mesh) | Hammer mill |
| Fabricated chips | < 500 μm (< 35 mesh) | Roller mill or fine hammer |
| Pellets (3G) | 150 – 300 μm (50–100 mesh) | Pin mill |
| Seasoning carrier | < 250 μm (> 60 mesh) | Air classifier mill |

---

## 7. Troubleshooting Snack Extrusion

| Problem | Root Cause | Solution |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| **Poor expansion** | Insufficient starch gelatinization | Increase barrel temperature by 5–10°C; reduce feed rate |
| | Moisture too high | Reduce feed moisture by 1–2% |
| | Potato inclusion too high | Reduce to ≤ 30% or add expandable starch |
| **Uneven texture** | Poor mixing | Pre-blend dry ingredients for 5 min; check mixer |
| | Particle size variation | Ensure consistent grinding (uniform mesh) |
| **Dark color / scorching** | Excessive reducing sugars | Switch to low-sugar variety or increase moisture |
| | Hot spots in barrel | Check barrel temperature uniformity; clean deposits |
| **High oil pickup** | Low expansion → dense piece | Optimize expansion as above |
| | High potato content (>35%) | Reduce potato; increase oil-resistant starch |
| **Sticky dough** | Over-gelatinization | Reduce barrel temp; add ungelatinized starch |
| **Blistering / surface defects** | Moisture differential | Equalize moisture distribution; temper longer |

---

## 8. Quality Control Parameters for Snack Products

| Parameter | Direct Expanded | Fabricated Chip | Snack Pellet |
|-----------|---------------|----------------|--------------|
| Expansion ratio | ≥ 3.5:1 | N/A | N/A |
| Bulk density | 55 – 120 g/L | — | 600 – 800 g/L (dried pellet) |
| Moisture (final) | ≤ 2.5% | ≤ 2.0% | ≤ 3.0% |
| Oil content | 20 – 30% | 28 – 38% | 25 – 40% |
| Color (L*) | ≥ 80 | ≥ 75 | ≥ 82 |
| Hardness (TA-XT2) | 8 – 15 N | 2.5 – 4.5 N | 12 – 25 N (pellet) |
| Crispness (acoustic) | > 30 peaks/sec | > 20 peaks/sec | > 40 peaks/sec |
| Shelf life (ambient) | 6 – 12 months | 4 – 8 months | 12 – 18 months |

---

## 9. Potatoes for Snacks: Product Selection Guide

| Snack Application | Best Product | Key Attribute | Item Code |
|-----------------|-------------|---------------|-----------|
| Collets / puffs | Standard Potato Powder (60 Mesh) | Expansion, neutral flavor | HJ-PP-60-01 |
| Premium extruded snacks | Premium Potato Powder (60 Mesh) | L* ≥ 88, low reducing sugars | HJ-PP-60-02 |
| Fabricated chips | Fine Milled Potato Flakes | Uniform hydration, sheetability | HJ-PF-02 |
| Snack pellets (3G) | Standard Potato Powder (60 Mesh) | Cold water solubility | HJ-PP-60-01 |
| Seasoning carrier | Standard Potato Powder (60 Mesh) | Oil absorption, flowability | HJ-PP-60-01 |
| Organic snacks | Organic Potato Powder | Organic certification | HJ-PP-60-03 |
| Premium fabricated chip | Premium Potato Flakes | L* ≥ 90, b* 12–16 | HJ-PF-01 (Premium) |

---

*Document version: 1.1 | Last updated: July 2026 | Prepared by: Hongji Agriculture R&D — Snack Application Division*

*For technical support, custom formulation development, or extrusion trials, contact: tech@hjpotatoflakes.com*

---

---

*This document is part of the Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) Technical Documentation Series. For more information about our vertically integrated potato supply chain — from seed breeding and cultivation to processing and global export — visit our official B2B website: [https://hjpotatoflakes.com](https://hjpotatoflakes.com)*



<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-06-product-application/application-guide-instant-mashed-potato.md --->

# Application Guide: Instant Mashed Potato — Formulation & Processing

## Scope & Objective

This technical guide provides detailed formulation, reconstitution parameters, and process optimization for instant mashed potato products manufactured using Hongji Potato Flakes. The guide covers foodservice bulk preparation, retail pack formulation, and industrial ingredient applications where reconstituted mashed potato is a component of further-processed foods (e.g., shepherds pie, potato croquettes, gnocchi, and frozen potato products).

Instant mashed potato is one of the largest volume applications for dehydrated potato flakes globally. The quality of the final product depends critically on the choice of flake specification, the reconstitution ratio, water temperature, mixing method, and post-reconstitution handling.

---

## 1. Product Selection for Instant Mash

### 1.1 Optimal Potato Flake Attributes

| Attribute | Minimum Requirement | Recommended for Premium Product |
|-----------|-------------------|-------------------------------|
| Variety | Atlantic / Shepody / Russet Burbank | Shepody (best mouthfeel) |
| Moisture | ≤ 6.0% | ≤ 5.5% |
| L* color | ≥ 88 | ≥ 90 |
| b* color | 8 – 18 | 10 – 16 |
| Reducing sugars | ≤ 1.5% | ≤ 1.0% |
| WAI (Water Absorption Index) | 5.5 – 7.5 g/g | 6.0 – 7.5 g/g |
| pH | 5.3 – 6.3 | 5.5 – 6.0 |
| Cell breakage | — | ≤ 5% intact cells |

### 1.2 Recommended Product Grades

| Application | Product Code | Grade | Rationale |
|------------|-------------|-------|-----------|
| Foodservice (bulk) | HJ-PF-01 | Standard | Cost-effective, good performance |
| Retail instant mash | HJ-PF-01 (Premium) or HJ-PF-02 | Premium | Superior color, smoother texture |
| HORECA (restaurant) | HJ-PF-01 | Standard | Reliable, consistent output |
| Industrial (further processing) | HJ-PF-01 | Standard | Performance at scale |
| Premium / export | HJ-PF-01 (Premium) | Premium | L* ≥ 90, ≤ 1.0% reducing sugars |

---

## 2. Reconstitution Principles

### 2.1 The Potato Flake-Water System

When Potato Flakes are mixed with water, two simultaneous processes occur:

1. **Hydration:** Pre-gelatinized starch granules absorb water and swell rapidly (within 15–30 seconds)
2. **Solubilization:** Soluble potato solids (low molecular weight sugars, minerals, pectin fragments) dissolve into the water phase

The goal is to achieve complete hydration without overworking the mixture (which causes cell rupture and a pasty, gluey texture).

### 2.2 Critical Parameters

| Parameter | Effect on Quality |
|-----------|------------------|
| **Water temperature** | Controls rate of hydration and final temperature |
| **Flakes-to-water ratio** | Determines final consistency (firm or soft) |
| **Mixing method** | Gentle folding vs. vigorous beating |
| **Rest time** | Allows moisture equilibration |
| **Water quality** | Hardness and pH affect texture |
| **Fat addition** | Improves mouthfeel and prevents starch setback |

---

## 3. Recommended Reconstitution Ratios

### 3.1 By Application

| Application | Flakes : Water (by weight) | Water Temp (°C) | Consistency |
|------------|---------------------------|-----------------|-------------|
| **Foodservice bulk mash** | 1 : 4.5 – 5.0 | 80 – 85 | Soft, pipeable |
| **Retail / home (standard)** | 1 : 3.8 – 4.2 | 75 – 85 | Medium, scoopable |
| **Retail / home (firm)** | 1 : 3.0 – 3.5 | 80 – 85 | Firm, suitable for shaping |
| **Shepherds pie topping** | 1 : 3.5 – 4.0 | 80 – 85 | Firm enough to hold shape |
| **Potato croquettes / balls** | 1 : 2.8 – 3.2 | 85 – 90 | Very firm, moldable |
| **Gnocchi dough** | 1 : 2.5 – 3.0 | 85 – 90 | Stiff dough-like |
| **Soup / sauce thickener** | 1 : 5.0 – 6.0 | 90 – 95 | Thin, pourable |

### 3.2 Detailed Ratio Guide — Standard Mash

| Desired Yield | Potato Flakes | Hot Water (80°C) | Milk/Fat (optional) | Salt |
|--------------|--------------|-----------------|-------------------|------|
| 1 serving (200 g) | 40 g | 160 g | 10 g butter + 15 g milk | 0.5 g |
| 5 servings (1 kg) | 200 g | 800 g | 50 g butter + 75 g milk | 2.5 g |
| 25 servings (5 kg) | 1.0 kg | 4.0 kg | 250 g butter + 375 g milk | 12.5 g |
| Foodservice (10 kg) | 2.0 kg | 8.0 kg | 500 g margarine + 750 g milk | 30 g |

---

## 4. Reconstitution Procedure

### 4.1 Standard Foodservice Procedure

**Step 1 — Heat Water**
- Heat water to 80–85°C (do not boil — boiling water damages starch structure and causes pasty texture)
- For best results, use a 50/50 mix of water and whole milk (or reconstituted milk powder)

**Step 2 — Add Fat (Optional but Recommended)**
- Add butter, margarine, or vegetable oil: 50–80 g per 1 kg flakes
- Allow fat to melt in hot water before adding flakes

**Step 3 — Add Flakes**
- Sprinkle Potato Flakes evenly over the surface of the liquid
- Do not dump in one spot

**Step 4 — Hydrate (Rest)**
- Allow flakes to absorb water: 30–60 seconds (do not stir during this period)

**Step 5 — Fold Gently**
- Using a wire whisk or fork, gently fold the mixture from bottom to top
- 10–15 gentle strokes — do not overmix
- Overmixing causes cell wall rupture → pasty, gluey consistency

**Step 6 — Season**
- Add salt (5–8 g per kg reconstituted mash)
- Add white pepper, garlic powder, or herbs as desired

**Step 7 — Hold**
- Hold at 65–80°C for up to 1 hour (covered)
- Do not hold below 60°C (risk of microbial growth)
- Stir gently every 15 minutes to prevent surface drying

### 4.2 Industrial Scale (Batch Mixer)

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Water temperature** | 80 – 85°C |
| **Mixing system** | Planetary mixer with paddle attachment (not wire whip) |
| **Mixing speed** | Low speed (30–60 RPM) |
| **Mixing time** | 45 – 90 seconds total |
| **Flakes addition** | Continuous feed over 15–20 seconds |
| **Rest after mixing** | 2 – 4 minutes before packing |
| **Packaging temperature** | ≥ 65°C (hot-fill) or cool to 4°C (chilled) |

---

## 5. Texture Optimization

### 5.1 Achieving Different Textures

| Texture Desired | Technique | Flakes:Water | Key Factors |
|----------------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| **Smooth & creamy** | Use fine-milled flakes (HJ-PF-02); fold 20–25 strokes | 1:4.0 – 4.5 | Higher milk solids, finer flakes |
| **Fluffy & light** | Use standard flakes; gentle folding (10–12 strokes); add melted butter after first fold | 1:4.0 – 4.5 | Minimal mixing, cool water (70°C) |
| **Rustic / chunky** | Use standard flakes; less hydration; 5–8 strokes only | 1:3.2 – 3.5 | Under-hydrate, very gentle agitation |
| **Firm / shapeable** | Reduce water; add whole egg or egg white | 1:2.5 – 3.0 | Egg binder (50 g egg / 1 kg flakes) |
| **Thin / pipeable** | Increase water; add less fat | 1:4.5 – 5.5 | More water, less agitation |

### 5.2 Additives for Improved Texture

| Additive | Dosage (per 1 kg flakes) | Effect |
|----------|-------------------------|--------|
| Milk powder | 50 – 150 g | Creamier texture, improved flavor |
| Butter / margarine | 50 – 150 g | Rich mouthfeel, gloss |
| Heavy cream | 100 – 200 ml | Premium mouthfeel, reduced starchiness |
| Egg yolk | 1 – 2 (15–30 g) | Richer texture (for baked dishes) |
| Sour cream | 50 – 100 g | Tangy flavor, creaminess |
| Cheese (grated) | 50 – 150 g | Flavor, stretch, browning |
| Potato starch | 10 – 30 g | Increased stand-up property |
| Sodium acid pyrophosphate (SAPP) | 2 – 5 g | Color retention (prevents grey-blue tint) |
| Ascorbic acid | 0.5 – 1.0 g | Color stabilizer |
| Xanthan gum | 1 – 3 g | Body and mouthfeel (retail packs) |
| Mono/diglyceride | 3 – 5 g | Improved rehydration |

---

## 6. Retail Instant Mash Pack — Formulation

### 6.1 Typical Retail Formulation

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) | Function |
|-----------|--------------|----------|
| **Potato Flakes (Premium, HJ-PF-01)** | **65 – 85** | Base ingredient |
| Milk solids (non-fat) | 3 – 8 | Creaminess, flavor |
| Salt | 1.5 – 2.5 | Flavor |
| Hydrogenated vegetable fat (powdered) | 2 – 5 | Mouthfeel |
| Monosodium glutamate (MSG) | 0 – 1.0 | Flavor enhancer |
| Disodium diphosphate | 0.3 – 0.5 | Color stabilizer |
| Mono/diglyceride (emulsifier) | 0.2 – 0.5 | Dispersion aid |
| Silicon dioxide (anti-caking) | 0 – 0.5 | Prevents caking |
| Natural flavor (butter, herbs) | 0.5 – 2.0 | Flavor differentiation |
| Onion powder | 0.3 – 1.0 | Savory note (variety packs) |

### 6.2 Pack Size & Portion Guidance

| Pack Size | Flakes Weight | Yield (reconstituted) | Recommended Milk/Water |
|-----------|--------------|----------------------|----------------------|
| Individual (50 g sachet) | 50 g | 240 – 260 g | 200 ml hot water + 15 ml milk |
| Family (200 g box) | 200 g | 960 – 1040 g | 800 ml hot water + 60 ml milk |
| Family (500 g box) | 500 g | 2.4 – 2.6 kg | 2 L hot water + 150 ml milk |

---

## 7. Industrial Application: Further-Processed Potato Products

### 7.1 Mashed Potato for Shepherds Pie / Cottage Pie

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Reconstituted mash (1:4 ratio)** | **80.0** |
| Butter | 5.0 |
| Egg yolk | 4.0 |
| Parmesan cheese | 3.0 |
| Bread crumbs (topping) | 5.0 |
| Seasoning blend | 3.0 |

**Process:**
1. Reconstitute Potato Flakes at 1:3.8 ratio (firm mash)
2. Fold in butter, egg yolk, cheese, seasoning
3. Spread over prepared meat filling
4. Top with breadcrumbs
5. Bake at 190°C for 25–30 min until golden

### 7.2 Potato Croquettes

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Reconstituted mash (1:3 ratio, firm)** | **72.0** |
| Whole egg | 8.0 |
| Parmesan cheese | 5.0 |
| Bread flour | 10.0 |
| Butter | 3.0 |
| Nutmeg | 0.2 |
| Salt & pepper | 0.8 |
| Bread crumbs (coating) | Included separately |

**Process:**
1. Reconstitute flakes at 1:3 ratio with 85°C water to form stiff mash
2. Cool to 4°C for minimum 2 hours
3. Combine with egg, flour, cheese, butter, seasoning
4. Shape into cylinders (approx 30 g each)
5. Standard breading (flour → egg wash → bread crumbs)
6. Deep fry at 175°C for 3–4 min

### 7.3 Potato Gnocchi

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Reconstituted mash (1:2.8 ratio, very firm)** | **60.0** |
| All-purpose flour (type 00) | 30.0 |
| Whole egg | 6.0 |
| Salt | 1.5 |
| Nutmeg | 0.3 |
| Olive oil | 2.2 |

**Process:**
1. Reconstitute flakes at 1:2.8 ratio — mash must be very firm
2. Cool to room temperature (20–25°C)
3. Combine mash with flour, egg, salt, nutmeg, olive oil on work surface
4. Knead gently until a soft dough forms (3–5 min)
5. Rest 10 min
6. Roll into ropes (2 cm diameter), cut into 2 cm pieces
7. Fork-mark or ridge
8. Cook in boiling salted water until they float (2–3 min)
9. Serve with sauce or pan-fry

---

## 8. Cold Water Reconstitution for Premixes

### 8.1 Cold-Soluble Retail Blend

For retail products where the consumer may use tap-temperature water, a modified formulation is required:

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Flakes (fine milled, HJ-PF-02)** | **60.0** |
| Instant milk powder | 10.0 |
| Modified starch (cold-soluble) | 8.0 |
| Hydrogenated vegetable fat (spray-dried) | 5.0 |
| Maltodextrin DE 18 | 5.0 |
| Salt | 2.0 |
| Sugar | 1.5 |
| Mono/diglyceride | 0.5 |
| Silicon dioxide | 1.0 |
| Natural butter flavor | 0.5 |

**Cold Water Reconstitution:**
- Ratio: 1:3.5 (blend : cold water, 15–25°C)
- Mixing: Whisk 60 seconds to hydrate
- Rest: 3–5 minutes for full hydration
- Note: Texture will be slightly less fluffy than hot-water method

---

## 9. Quality Evaluation — Reconstituted Mash

### 9.1 Sensory & Physical Parameters

| Parameter | Acceptable Range | Premium Target | Test Method |
|-----------|-----------------|---------------|-------------|
| Appearance | Uniform, no lumps | Very uniform, glossy | Visual |
| Color | Cream to light yellow | Light cream, no grey | HunterLab L* ≥ 80 |
| Texture score (1–10) | ≥ 6 | ≥ 8 | Sensory panel |
| Starchiness (off-note) | None | None | Sensory |
| Mouthfeel | Smooth, no grit | Very smooth, creamy | Sensory |
| Particle breakdown | None | None | Microscopy |
| Water separation after 30 min | ≤ 2 mm | ≤ 1 mm | Graduated cylinder |
| Bostwick consistency (30 sec, 25°C) | 100 – 180 mm | 120 – 150 mm | Bostwick Consistometer |

### 9.2 Key Index — Texture Analysis

| Parameter | Value for Premium Reconstituted Mash | Instrument |
|-----------|--------------------------------------|-----------|
| Hardness (N) | 0.5 – 1.5 | TA.XT2 Texture Analyzer |
| Adhesiveness (N·s) | −0.5 to −2.0 | TA.XT2 |
| Cohesiveness | 0.3 – 0.6 | TA.XT2 |
| Springiness | 0.6 – 0.9 | TA.XT2 |
| Gumminess (N) | 0.3 – 1.0 | TA.XT2 |

---

## 10. Troubleshooting Guide

| Problem | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| **Gluey / pasty texture** | Overmixing; water too hot | Reduce mixing strokes to 10–12; use water at 75–80°C |
| | Water ratio too low | Increase water by 5–10% |
| **Lumpy mash** | Cold water; flakes added too fast | Water must be ≥ 75°C; sprinkle slowly |
| | Poor dispersion | Pre-blend flakes with seasonings |
| **Grey / blue discoloration** | Iron in water; high pH | Use SAPP or ascorbic acid; check water hardness |
| | Overcooking raw potatoes (fresh) | N/A for flakes — check storage |
| **Watery / thin mash** | Excessive water; low fat | Reduce water; add 2–3% butter/margarine |
| **Stale / off flavor** | Old product; rancid fat in recipe | Check flake production date (≤ 12 months); check fat freshness |
| **Surface drying** | Holding too long uncovered | Cover with film or butter pat; hold at 75°C |
| **Soggy after holding** | Condensation | Drain condensation water; keep hot but not covered tight |
| **Separation (water pooling)** | Starch retrogradation | Add fat or mono/diglyceride; serve within 1 hour |
| **Not dissolving / hard bits** | Incomplete hydration | Increase rest time to 60–90 sec; check water temp |

---

*Document version: 1.2 | Last updated: July 2026 | Prepared by: Hongji Agriculture R&D — Potato Application Division*

*For formulation assistance, trials, or custom product development, contact: tech@hjpotatoflakes.com*

---

*This document is part of the Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) Technical Documentation Series. For more information about our vertically integrated potato supply chain — from seed breeding and cultivation to processing and global export — visit our official B2B website: [https://hjpotatoflakes.com](https://hjpotatoflakes.com)*



<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-06-product-application/application-guide-soups-sauces-prepared-meals.md --->

# Application Guide: Potato Flakes & Powder in Soups, Sauces & Prepared Meals

## Scope & Objective

This technical guide provides comprehensive formulation and processing parameters for incorporating Hongji Potato Flakes and Potato Powder into soups, sauces, gravies, and prepared meal applications. The guide is intended for R&D teams and production managers in the savory food manufacturing sector, covering instant dry mix formulations, wet-process sauces and soups, retorted products, frozen prepared meals, and culinary bases.

Potato-derived ingredients offer unique advantages in savory liquid and semi-liquid systems: high cold-water solubility (especially powder), exceptional water binding, smooth mouthfeel, clean flavor profile, and freeze-thaw stability. They function both as a thickener and as a flavor carrier, enabling reduced usage of modified starches and gums.

---

## 1. Product Selection for Soups & Sauces

### 1.1 Choosing Between Flakes and Powder

| Application | Recommended Product | Rationale |
|------------|-------------------|-----------|
| **Instant dry soup mixes** | Potato Powder (60 Mesh) | Rapid cold/hot hydration, fine dispersion |
| **Retort / canned soups** | Potato Flakes | Heat-stable thickener, won't break down |
| **Frozen soups / sauces** | Potato Powder | Freeze-thaw stability, smooth texture |
| **Gravy mixes (dry)** | Potato Powder | Disperses without clumping |
| **Wet sauces (hot-fill)** | Potato Flakes | Viscosity builder, smooth mouthfeel |
| **Cheese sauces** | Potato Powder | Avoids graininess, melts smoothly |
| **Prepared meal components** | Both (blend optimized) | Balanced function |

### 1.2 Key Functional Properties for Liquid Systems

| Property | Potato Flakes | Potato Powder (60 Mesh) |
|----------|-------------|------------------------|
| Cold water hydration | Partial (15–25%) | Good (20–40%) |
| Hot water viscosity | 2,000–3,500 cP (peak) | 1,500–2,800 cP (peak) |
| Clumping tendency | Low-moderate | Low (free-flowing) |
| Stability in acid (pH < 4.0) | Moderate | Good |
| Stability in high salt (>3%) | Good | Good |
| Freeze-thaw stability | Good (≥ 3 cycles) | Very good (≥ 5 cycles) |
| Clarity in solution | Opaque, creamy | Opaque, creamy |
| Flavor contribution | Mild potato | Very mild |

---

## 2. Cream of Potato Soup — Complete Formulation

### 2.1 Instant Dry Mix (Retail / Foodservice)

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) | Function |
|-----------|--------------|----------|
| **Potato Powder (HJ-PP-60-01)** | **30.0** | Base thickener, flavor |
| **Potato Flakes (HJ-PF-01, fine milled)** | **15.0** | Texture, rustic potato bits |
| Non-fat dry milk | 15.0 | Creaminess, body |
| Modified corn starch | 8.0 | Additional viscosity |
| Vegetable shortening (powdered) | 6.0 | Mouthfeel, richness |
| Corn syrup solids (DE 24) | 5.0 | Body, sweetness balance |
| Salt | 4.5 | Flavor |
| Onion powder | 2.5 | Savory note |
| Maltodextrin (DE 10) | 5.0 | Carrier, bulk |
| Chicken fat (spray-dried) | 2.0 | Savory richness |
| Garlic powder | 1.2 | Flavor |
| White pepper | 0.8 | Flavor |
| Celery powder | 0.5 | Savory background |
| Disodium inosinate/guanylate (I+G) | 0.3 | Umami enhancer |
| Silicon dioxide (anti-caking) | 1.0 | Free flow |
| Turmeric powder | 0.2 | Color |

**Reconstitution (Hot):** 30 g mix + 200 ml hot water (80°C). Whisk 30 sec. Let stand 3 min.

**Reconstitution (Cold start):** 30 g mix + 200 ml cold water. Whisk, bring to boil gently while stirring. Simmer 3 min.

### 2.2 Cream of Potato Soup — Wet Process (Canned / Retort)

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Flakes (HJ-PF-01)** | **4.5** |
| Water | 72.0 |
| Fresh milk (3.5% fat) | 8.0 |
| Heavy cream (35% fat) | 4.0 |
| Butter | 2.0 |
| Onion (fresh, diced) | 3.0 |
| Modified tapioca starch | 1.5 |
| Salt | 0.9 |
| Sugar | 0.5 |
| Garlic (fresh, minced) | 0.3 |
| White pepper | 0.1 |
| Nutmeg | 0.02 |
| Xanthan gum | 0.08 |

**Process:**
1. Sauté onion and garlic in butter (3 min, 90°C)
2. Add water and milk; heat to 80°C
3. Slowly sprinkle Potato Flakes while whisking
4. Add modified starch slurry (1:3 in cold water)
5. Add cream, salt, sugar, spices
6. Hold at 85°C for 5 min (starch cook)
7. Hot fill at ≥ 80°C into cans/retort pouches
8. Retort at 121°C for 25–35 min (F₀ ≥ 6)
9. Final viscosity target: 2,000–4,000 cP (Brookfield, 25°C, spindle #3)

---

## 3. Cream-Style Soup Base (Concentrate for Foodservice)

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Flakes (HJ-PF-01)** | **8.0** |
| **Potato Powder (HJ-PP-60-01)** | **4.0** |
| Water | 50.0 |
| Whole milk powder | 10.0 |
| Vegetable oil | 8.0 |
| Modified waxy maize starch | 5.0 |
| Salt | 2.5 |
| Sugar | 2.0 |
| Mono/diglyceride | 0.5 |
| Xanthan gum | 0.2 |
| Dipotassium phosphate | 0.3 |
| Flavor enhancers | 0.5 |

**Usage:** Dilute 1 part concentrate with 2–3 parts water/milk and add vegetables, meat, or seafood to create customized cream soups.

---

## 4. Sauce Applications

### 4.1 White Sauce / Béchamel (Instant Dry)

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Powder (HJ-PP-60-01)** | **25.0** |
| Non-fat dry milk | 30.0 |
| Powdered shortening | 15.0 |
| Wheat flour (soft) | 12.0 |
| Salt | 3.0 |
| Sugar | 2.0 |
| Onion powder | 1.5 |
| White pepper | 0.8 |
| Nutmeg | 0.2 |
| Butter flavor (natural) | 0.5 |
| Silicon dioxide | 1.0 |

**Reconstitution:** 40 g mix + 200 ml cold water. Whisk, bring to boil, simmer 2 min. Yield: ~220 g sauce.

### 4.2 Cheese Sauce (Hot Fill)

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Powder (HJ-PP-60-01)** | **6.0** |
| Cheddar cheese powder | 15.0 |
| Water | 52.0 |
| Whole milk | 10.0 |
| Butter | 5.0 |
| Modified waxy maize starch | 4.0 |
| Enzyme-modified cheese | 2.5 |
| Salt | 1.8 |
| Cheese flavor | 1.0 |
| Lactic acid | 0.5 |
| Sodium citrate | 0.3 |
| Annatto (color) | 0.02 |
| Xanthan gum | 0.08 |

**Process:**
1. Heat water and milk to 70°C
2. Pre-blend Potato Powder, modified starch, xanthan gum with dry ingredients
3. Add dry blend to liquid while high-shear mixing
4. Add butter and cheese powders; heat to 85°C
5. Add acid and flavor; adjust pH to 5.4–5.8
6. Hot fill at ≥ 80°C
7. Target viscosity: 8,000–12,000 cP (Brookfield, 25°C)

### 4.3 Gravy (Brown / Chicken / Mushroom)

| Ingredient | Brown Gravy (%) | Chicken Gravy (%) | Mushroom Gravy (%) |
|-----------|---------------|------------------|-------------------|
| **Potato Powder (HJ-PP-60-01)** | **12.0** | **14.0** | **12.0** |
| Wheat flour (toasted) | 10.0 | 8.0 | 8.0 |
| Corn starch | 8.0 | 8.0 | 6.0 |
| Beef/chicken fat (spray-dried) | 6.0 | 5.0 | — |
| Dark caramel color | 3.5 | 0.5 | 0.5 |
| Salt | 4.0 | 4.5 | 4.0 |
| Sugar | 2.0 | 1.5 | 1.5 |
| Hydrolyzed vegetable protein | 3.0 | 2.5 | 2.0 |
| Onion powder | 2.0 | 2.5 | 3.0 |
| Garlic powder | 0.8 | 0.8 | 1.0 |
| Mushroom powder | — | — | 4.0 |
| Black pepper | 0.5 | 0.3 | 0.5 |
| Thyme | 0.2 | 0.1 | 0.2 |
| Silicon dioxide | 1.0 | 1.0 | 1.0 |

**Reconstitution:** 25 g mix + 200 ml cold water. Whisk, bring to boil, simmer 1 min.

---

## 5. Prepared Meal Applications

### 5.1 Shepherds Pie / Potato-Topped Pie (Frozen)

| Component | Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato topping** | **Reconstituted mash (HJ-PF-01, 1:3.5)** | **45.0** |
| | Butter | 3.0 |
| | Milk powder | 2.0 |
| | Egg yolk | 1.5 |
| | Salt, pepper | 0.5 |
| Meat filling | Ground beef/lamb | 20.0 |
| | Onion (diced) | 8.0 |
| | Carrot (diced) | 5.0 |
| | Peas | 4.0 |
| | Tomato paste | 3.0 |
| | Beef stock | 6.0 |
| | Flour | 1.5 |
| | Seasoning | 0.5 |

**Frozen Meal Process:**
1. Prepare meat filling; cool to 25°C
2. Reconstitute Potato Flakes at 1:3.5 (firm mash)
3. Fold in butter, milk powder, egg yolk, seasonings
4. Fill tray: 150 g filling + 100 g potato topping
5. Freeze at −35°C blast freezer
6. Store at −18°C (shelf life: 12 months)
7. Reheat: 180°C oven for 30 min or microwave (750W, 5 min)

### 5.2 Potato Leek Soup (Frozen / Chilled)

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Flakes (HJ-PF-01)** | **4.0** |
| Potato starch | 1.0 |
| Leek (fresh, sliced) | 12.0 |
| Onion | 4.0 |
| Butter | 3.0 |
| Whole milk | 15.0 |
| Water | 58.0 |
| Heavy cream | 2.0 |
| Salt | 0.7 |
| White pepper | 0.05 |
| Nutmeg | 0.02 |
| Parsley | 0.03 |

**Frozen Reheat Stability:** After 3 months at −18°C, the Potato Flakes-based soup shows ≤ 5% syneresis vs. 12–15% for flour-only control.

### 5.3 Potato & Vegetable Chowder (Retort Pouch)

| Ingredient | Proportion (%) |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Flakes (HJ-PF-01)** | **5.0** |
| Diced potato (fresh) | 8.0 |
| Corn kernel | 5.0 |
| Carrot (diced) | 4.0 |
| Celery (diced) | 2.0 |
| Cream | 8.0 |
| Milk | 12.0 |
| Water | 52.0 |
| Butter | 2.5 |
| Salt | 0.8 |
| Sugar | 0.5 |
| Modified starch | 1.5 |
| Seasoning | 0.7 |

---

## 6. Thickening Power Comparison

### 6.1 Viscosity Contribution (1% w/w Solution, 80°C)

| Thickener | Cold Viscosity (cP) | Hot Viscosity (80°C, cP) | Clarity |
|-----------|-------------------|------------------------|---------|
| **Potato Powder (60 Mesh)** | 80 – 150 | 1,000 – 1,800 | Opaque |
| **Potato Flakes** | 50 – 100 | 800 – 1,500 | Opaque |
| Native potato starch | 30 – 60 | 2,500 – 4,000 | Translucent |
| Modified waxy maize | 120 – 200 | 3,000 – 5,000 | Translucent |
| Wheat flour | 20 – 40 | 400 – 800 | Opaque |
| Xanthan gum | 300 – 600 | 1,500 – 3,000 | Translucent |

### 6.2 Optimal Concentration in Sauces

| Desired Sauce Body | Potato Powder | Potato Flakes | Modified Starch (additional) |
|-------------------|--------------|--------------|------------------------------|
| Thin (drizzle) | 1 – 2% | 2 – 3% | 0.5 – 1% |
| Medium (pourable) | 3 – 5% | 4 – 6% | 1 – 2% |
| Thick (spoonable) | 5 – 8% | 6 – 10% | 2 – 3% |

---

## 7. Freeze-Thaw Stability Data

| Formulation | Viscosity Before Freeze (cP) | Viscosity After 3 Cycles (cP) | Syneresis (%) |
|------------|----------------------------|-------------------------------|---------------|
| Potato Powder 3% only | 1,200 | 980 (−18%) | 3.5 |
| Potato Flakes 4% only | 1,100 | 850 (−23%) | 4.2 |
| Potato Powder 3% + Xanthan 0.05% | 1,500 | 1,420 (−5%) | 0.8 |
| Potato Flakes 4% + Starch 1% | 1,800 | 1,620 (−10%) | 1.5 |
| Wheat flour 5% | 750 | 520 (−31%) | 8.5 |
| Modified starch 4% | 2,800 | 2,650 (−5%) | 1.0 |

---

## 8. Culinary Convenience Base (Bulk Foodservice)

### 8.1 Instant Potato Soup Base (Bulk)

| Ingredient | kg per 100 kg |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Potato Flakes (HJ-PF-01)** | **35.0** |
| **Potato Powder (HJ-PP-60-01)** | **15.0** |
| Creamer powder (vegetable fat based) | 18.0 |
| Non-fat dry milk | 12.0 |
| Salt | 5.0 |
| Maltodextrin DE 18 | 6.0 |
| Onion powder | 3.0 |
| Chicken bouillon powder | 2.5 |
| Garlic powder | 1.5 |
| White pepper | 0.5 |
| Celery seed powder | 0.3 |
| Turmeric | 0.2 |

**Yield per kg base:** Approximately 8–10 L of finished soup when reconstituted with hot water.

---

## 9. Quality Parameters for Soup/Sauce Applications

| Parameter | Acceptable Range | Premium Target |
|-----------|-----------------|---------------|
| **Hot viscosity (80°C)** | 1,500 – 5,000 cP | 2,500 – 4,000 cP |
| **Cold viscosity (25°C)** | 800 – 3,000 cP | 1,500 – 2,500 cP |
| **pH (finished sauce)** | 5.0 – 6.5 | 5.5 – 6.0 |
| **Water activity (aw)** | ≤ 0.97 (hot-fill) | ≤ 0.95 |
| **L* color (white sauce)** | ≥ 80 | ≥ 85 |
| **Syneresis (24 h, 5°C)** | ≤ 5% | ≤ 2% |
| **Smoothness** | No visible lumps | Very smooth |
| **Mouthfeel** | No grit, no gumminess | Creamy, full-bodied |

---

## 10. Troubleshooting Guide

| Problem | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| **Lumpy sauce/soup** | Potato ingredient added too fast to hot liquid | Pre-disperse in cold water or pre-blend with dry ingredients |
| **Thin / low viscosity** | Insufficient potato content | Increase by 0.5–1.0%; check if modified starch needed |
| | Under-cooked starch system | Increase hold time at 85–90°C |
| **Grainy texture** | Over-cooking / shear degradation | Reduce shear; add near end of process |
| **Pasty / gluey** | Over-gelatinization | Reduce temperature; reduce mixing time |
| | Wrong product (too fine) | Use Standard Flakes instead of Powder |
| **Off-flavor** | Rancidity in fat component | Check fat sources; add antioxidant (tocopherol) |
| | Stale potato ingredient | Check flake production date |
| **Color too dark** | Maillard reaction (high temp, reduction) | Reduce processing temperature; check reducing sugars |
| | Caramelization of sugars | Add SAPP or citric acid |
| **Syneresis / water separation** | Starch retrogradation | Add xanthan gum (0.05–0.1%); use cross-linked starch |
| **Mold / spoilage (short shelf life)** | High water activity; poor hot-fill temp | Ensure fill temperature ≥ 80°C; target aw ≤ 0.95 |

---

*Document version: 1.1 | Last updated: July 2026 | Prepared by: Hongji Agriculture R&D — Savory Applications*

*For formulation support, custom development, or application trials, contact: tech@hjpotatoflakes.com*

---

---

*This document is part of the Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) Technical Documentation Series. For more information about our vertically integrated potato supply chain — from seed breeding and cultivation to processing and global export — visit our official B2B website: [https://hjpotatoflakes.com](https://hjpotatoflakes.com)*



<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-06-product-application/customized-ingredient-development-process.md --->

# Customized Ingredient Development Process — Hongji Agriculture

## Overview

Hongji Agriculture's **Customized Ingredient Development (CID)** program provides global food manufacturers with tailored potato-based ingredient solutions that match exact product specifications, regulatory requirements, and production constraints. Unlike standard OEM/ODM services that simply repackage existing products, the CID process is a systematic, multi-stage development workflow that transforms customer requirements into optimized, validated ingredient specifications.

The process is built on Hongji's fully integrated supply chain — from proprietary seed varieties grown in controlled fields in Zhangjiakou, through advanced processing, to finished product delivery. This vertical integration enables a level of customization that is difficult to achieve with commodity ingredient suppliers.

---

## 1. Development Philosophy

### 1.1 Core Principles

| Principle | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Customer-led specification** | The customer's target application, formulation constraints, and process conditions define the ingredient target — not a fixed product catalog |
| **Data-driven iteration** | Every development stage is supported by quantitative analytics, not subjective assessment |
| **End-use validated** | The ingredient is tested in the customer's actual process (or a validated proxy) before scale-up |
| **Regulatory by design** | Target market regulations (EU, US FDA, China GB, Japan, etc.) are embedded in the specification from Day 1 |
| **Full traceability** | Every custom lot is traceable to seed variety, field, processing date, and production line |

### 1.2 Typical Customization Areas

| Parameter | Standard Options | Customization Possibilities |
|-----------|----------------|---------------------------|
| **Particle size** | Flakes (0.25–3.35 mm) or Powder (60 mesh) | Any sieve cut from 5 mesh to 200 mesh |
| **Moisture content** | ≤ 6.0% | 3.0 – 8.0% (depending on stability requirements) |
| **Color (L*a*b*)** | As per grade spec | Targets from L* 82 to L* 94 by variety selection & process tuning |
| **pH** | 5.3 – 6.3 | 5.0 – 7.0 (food-grade acid/carbonate adjustment) |
| **Reducing sugars** | ≤ 1.5% (≤ 1.0% premium) | Down to ≤ 0.3% (for extreme low-browning applications) |
| **Starch content** | 73 – 77% | 68 – 82% (by variety selection and processing) |
| **Water absorption** | 5.5 – 7.5 g/g | 4.0 – 9.0 g/g (process modification) |
| **Microbiological limits** | As per standard | 10× stricter for infant food, 10× relaxed for heat-treated applications |
| **Packaging** | 20/25 kg kraft bag | Any format: bulk bags, vacuum blocks, retail packs, single-serve sachets |
| **Certifications** | ISO, FSSC, BRC, Halal, Kosher | Organic, specific Halal authority, specific Kosher certification |
| **Fortification** | None | Vitamin premix, mineral fortification, protein enrichment (co-processing) |

---

## 2. The 5-Stage Customized Development Workflow

```
STAGE 1                     STAGE 2                     STAGE 3
┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│ Customer Needs  │ ──▶ │   Lab-Scale     │ ──▶ │   Pilot-Scale   │
│ & Requirement   │     │   Development   │     │   Validation    │
│ Analysis        │     │   (Sample)      │     │   (Trial Batch) │
└─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
                                                      │
                                                      ▼
┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐
│ Continuous      │ ◀── │ Production &    │ ◀── │   Specification │
│ Improvement     │     │ Quality Release │     │   Freeze &      │
│ & Feedback      │     │                 │     │   Contract      │
└─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘
STAGE 5                 STAGE 4
```

---

## 3. Stage 1: Customer Needs & Requirement Analysis

### 3.1 Intake & Discovery

When a customer approaches Hongji with a customized ingredient request, the CID team initiates a structured discovery process:

**Step 1 — Application Profile**

| Information Requested | Purpose |
|---------------------|---------|
| End-product category | Bread, snack, soup, batter, meat, etc. |
| Target market (country/region) | Regulatory framework, labeling requirements |
| Processing method | Direct addition, extrusion, frying, retort, baking |
| Inclusion rate (planned %) | Required functional concentration |
| Production scale | Annual volume, batch size, frequency |
| Competing product reference | Target quality benchmark |
| Key success criteria | What "good" looks like |

**Step 2 — Technical Specification Request**

| Parameter | Customer Target | Acceptable Tolerance |
|-----------|----------------|---------------------|
| Moisture | ___ % | ± ___ |
| Protein | ___ % (dwb) | ± ___ |
| Starch | ___ % (dwb) | ± ___ |
| Ash | ___ % (dwb) | ± ___ |
| Reducing sugars | ___ % (dwb) | ± ___ |
| pH | ___ | ± ___ |
| Color (L*/a*/b*) | ___ / ___ / ___ | ± ___ |
| Particle size D50 | ___ μm | ± ___ |
| Water absorption | ___ g/g | ± ___ |
| Microbial limits | ___ CFU/g (TPC) | ___ |

**Step 3 — Regulatory & Compliance Check**

| Requirement | Checked Against |
|------------|----------------|
| Heavy metal limits | EU 1881/2006, US FDA, GB 2762, or customer-defined |
| Pesticide MRLs | EU 396/2005, US 40 CFR 180, or customer-defined |
| Allergen status | EU 1169/2011, US FALCPA, or customer-defined |
| GMO labeling | EU 1829/2003, US voluntary, CN GB 7718 |
| Organic certification | EU 2018/848, USDA NOP, GB/T 19630 |
| Kosher / Halal | As required |
| Country-specific | Import regulations, tariffs, duty preferences |

**Step 4 — Deliverable: Requirement Specification Document (RSD)**

The RSD is a signed agreement between Hongji and the customer covering all technical, commercial, and regulatory parameters. It serves as the source of truth for all subsequent development stages.

---

## 4. Stage 2: Lab-Scale Development & Sample Generation

### 4.1 Lab Capabilities

| Equipment | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| Lab-scale drum dryer (0.5 m²) | Small-batch flake production |
| Lab hammer mill with sieve control | Particle size customization |
| Lab extruder (twin-screw, 20 mm) | Process simulation |
| RVA (Rapid Visco Analyzer) | Viscosity profiling |
| Texture analyzer (TA.XT2) | Texture quantification |
| HunterLab colorimeter | Color matching |
| Bench-top spray dryer (for blends) | Powder blending optimization |
| Brabender Farinograph / Extensograph | Dough rheology (bakery applications) |

### 4.2 Development Process

| Step | Activity | Typical Duration |
|------|----------|-----------------|
| 1 | Ingredient selection: choose base potato variety and product type (flakes vs. powder) | 1 – 2 days |
| 2 | Process parameter design: drum dryer temperature, residence time, milling screen size | 2 – 3 days |
| 3 | Lab batch production: 1 – 5 kg of sample | 1 day |
| 4 | Physical & chemical analysis: full analytical panel | 2 – 3 days |
| 5 | Functional testing: rehydration, viscosity, water binding, color stability | 1 – 2 days |
| 6 | Application simulation: customer's end-use test (e.g., bench-scale baking, extrusion) | 2 – 5 days |
| 7 | Sensory evaluation (if applicable) | 1 day |
| 8 | Iteration: adjust and repeat steps 3–7 as needed | variable |

**Total lab phase:** Typically 10 – 20 working days per iteration cycle.

### 4.3 Deliverables — Lab Phase

- Sample A, B, C... (progressively optimized, 200 g – 2 kg each)
- Preliminary COA (Certificate of Analysis)
- Application test report
- Recommendation for pilot-scale parameters
- Documented specification target for Stage 3

---

## 5. Stage 3: Pilot-Scale Validation

### 5.1 Pilot Plant Capabilities

| Equipment | Scale | Key Features |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| Industrial drum dryer (3 m²) | 50 – 200 kg/batch | Steam-heated, variable speed |
| Industrial hammer mill | 50 – 200 kg/h | Multi-screen carousel |
| Air classifier | 50 – 150 kg/h | Cut point adjustment |
| Ribbon blender | 200 L | For blending and fortification |
| Twin-screw extruder (60 mm) | 50 – 150 kg/h | Full temperature control |
| Industrial packaging line (prototype) | — | Vacuum, nitrogen flush, bag-in-box |

### 5.2 Pilot Process

| Step | Activity | Duration |
|------|----------|----------|
| 1 | Scale-up calculation: determine process parameters for pilot equipment | 1 day |
| 2 | Raw material preparation: select specific field lot of target variety | 1 – 2 days |
| 3 | Pilot run: 50 – 200 kg batch | 1 day |
| 4 | Full analytical panel (expanded): physical, chemical, microbiological | 3 – 5 days |
| 5 | Customer application test: send 10 – 50 kg to customer for their own line trial | 5 – 15 days |
| 6 | Stability testing: accelerated shelf life (40°C / 75% RH, 4 weeks) | 28 days |
| 7 | Process capability analysis: Cp, Cpk calculation | 2 days |
| 8 | Documentation: draft specification sheet, provisional COA format | 2 days |

### 5.3 Decision Gates

| Gate | Criteria to Pass | Decision |
|------|-----------------|----------|
| **Gate 1 — Pilot Specification** | All parameters within customer tolerance (±2σ) | Proceed to customer trial |
| **Gate 2 — Customer Trial** | Customer confirms product meets end-use requirements | Proceed to spec freeze |
| **Gate 3 — Shelf Life** | No significant degradation after accelerated test | Final approval |

### 5.4 Deliverables — Pilot Phase

- Pilot batch report (full analytical data)
- Customer trial report (customer-signed)
- Proposed final specification (draft)
- Shelf life projection (accelerated data)
- Process capability data (Cp, Cpk)
- Packaging recommendation

---

## 6. Stage 4: Specification Freeze & Commercial Contract

### 6.1 Final Specification Document

The finalized specification includes:

| Section | Content |
|---------|---------|
| Product identity | Product name, description, item code |
| Physical specs | Moisture, particle size, bulk density, color, pH, water absorption |
| Chemical specs | Starch, protein, ash, fat, fiber, reducing sugars, SO₂ |
| Microbiological specs | TPC, yeast/mould, Enterobacteriaceae, pathogens |
| Heavy metals | Pb, Cd, Hg, As limits |
| Nutritional profile | Per 100 g (as sold) |
| Packaging specs | Bag type, inner liner, net weight, pallet configuration |
| Labeling | Country of origin, ingredient declaration, allergen statement, GMO status |
| Certifications | List of applicable certifications |
| Storage | Temperature, RH, shelf life from production |
| COA template | Standard format for every shipment |

### 6.2 Commercial Terms

| Parameter | Typical Range |
|-----------|-------------|
| MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) | 8 – 16 MT (negotiable) |
| Price structure | FOB Tianjin / CIF destination port |
| Lead time | 21 – 45 days from PO confirmation |
| Payment terms | T/T (30% deposit + 70% against BL) or L/C |
| Exclusivity | Available for volume commitments |
| NDA / IP protection | Signed before specification disclosure |

### 6.3 Deliverables — Commercial Phase

- Signed final specification sheet
- Commercial quotation
- Supply agreement or framework contract
- Quality agreement (including sampling plan, AQL)
- NDA (if not already signed)

---

## 7. Stage 5: Production & Quality Release

### 7.1 Production Flow for Custom Product

```
Raw Material Selection (specific variety + field lot)
       ↓
Incoming QC (potatoes: dry matter, sugars, defects)
       ↓
Production Run (dedicated line, documented CCPs)
       ↓
Inline QC (moisture every 1 h, color every 2 h, particle size every 2 h)
       ↓
Pre-Packaging QC (metal detector verified)
       ↓
Holding (24 h equilibration in warehouse)
       ↓
Final QC (full COA generated)
       ↓
Customer notification & approval (for first 3 commercial batches)
       ↓
Shipping
```

### 7.2 Quality Control Protocol for Custom Products

| QC Activity | Frequency | Critical to Customer |
|------------|-----------|---------------------|
| **Moisture** | Every 60 minutes | ±0.3% of target |
| **Color (L*a*b*)** | Every 120 minutes | ΔE ≤ 2.0 vs. reference |
| **Particle size (sieve analysis)** | Every 120 minutes | ±5% passing target mesh |
| **Bulk density** | Every 240 minutes | ±30 g/L of target |
| **Microbiological** | Every production batch | Per spec |
| **Sensory (appearance, odor)** | Every production batch | No off-notes |
| **Application test (customer-defined)** | First 3 batches; then monthly | As agreed |
| **Water activity (aw)** | Every 480 minutes | ≤ target |

### 7.3 First Commercial Batch — Enhanced Protocol

For the first 3 commercial batches, the customer receives:

- Full COA with all parameters
- Microbiological results (before shipment)
- 2 kg retention sample from each production hour
- Digital photos of product appearance
- Application test result (per customer-defined method)
- Container loading photos (seal number, condition)

### 7.4 Deliverables — Production Phase

- Commercial COA for each lot
- Shipment documentation (packing list, invoice, certificate of origin, health certificate)
- Container seal numbers and photos

---

## 8. Continuous Improvement & Feedback

### 8.1 Post-Delivery Review

| Activity | Timing | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Customer feedback survey | 30 days post-delivery | Quality, consistency, service |
| Production review meeting | Quarterly | Process optimization, cost reduction |
| Specification review | Annual | Update based on evolving needs |
| New technology update | As available | Introduce improvements (e.g., lower energy drying, enhanced nutrition) |

### 8.2 Typical Customization Project Timeline

| Phase | Duration |
|-------|----------|
| Stage 1: Needs Analysis | 1 – 2 weeks |
| Stage 2: Lab Development (2–3 iterations) | 4 – 8 weeks |
| Stage 3: Pilot Validation (including stability) | 6 – 10 weeks |
| Stage 4: Spec Freeze & Contract | 2 – 3 weeks |
| Stage 5: First Commercial Production | 3 – 4 weeks |
| **Total (expedited)** | **12 – 16 weeks** |
| **Total (standard)** | **20 – 28 weeks** |

---

## 9. Case Study Examples

### 9.1 Case A: Low-Browning Potato Flakes for Snack Seasoning

| Parameter | Customer Requirement | Solution |
|-----------|-------------------|----------|
| Target application | Surface seasoning light-colored snack | — |
| Critical issue | Flakes in seasoning caused dark spots after baking | — |
| Solution | Low reducing sugar variety (Atlantic, early harvest) + sulfite-free processing | Reducing sugars ≤ 0.4% |
| Particle size | < 300 μm (50 mesh) | Achieved via hammer mill + air classification |
| Development time | 10 weeks (expedited) | Delivered |

### 9.2 Case B: High-Viscosity Potato Flakes for Frozen Soup

| Parameter | Customer Requirement | Solution |
|-----------|-------------------|----------|
| Target application | Frozen cream soup concentrate | — |
| Critical issue | Regular flakes produced thin soup; needed higher viscosity | — |
| Solution | Modified drum drying parameters + specific variety (Russet Burbank) | WAI increased from 6.0 to 8.0 g/g |
| Freeze-thaw stability | ≤ 2% syneresis after 5 cycles | Achieved with processing modification |
| Development time | 16 weeks | Delivered |

### 9.3 Case C: Fortified Potato Powder for Infant Nutrition

| Parameter | Customer Requirement | Solution |
|-----------|-------------------|----------|
| Target application | Baby food (6+ months) | — |
| Critical issue | Standard product did not meet infant food microbial limits | — |
| Solution | Dedicated production line, tighter process control, enhanced hygiene protocols | TPC ≤ 1,000 CFU/g, no pathogens |
| Fortification | Iron (10 mg/100g) + Zinc (5 mg/100g) | Dry-blended using ribbon mixer |
| Development time | 22 weeks (including clinical stability) | Delivered |

---

## 10. Contact & Inquiry Process

To initiate a customized ingredient development project:

1. **Initial inquiry**: tech@hjpotatoflakes.com or contact your Hongji account manager
2. **NDA execution** (if needed): Standard mutual NDA, signed within 2 business days
3. **Kickoff meeting**: Video conference or in-person at Zhangjiakou facility
4. **RSD signing**: Requirement Specification Document agreed by both parties

**What we need from you on Day 1:**

- Target application (product category and brief description)
- Key specification parameters (even approximate ranges)
- Target market / countries
- Annual volume estimate
- Timeline expectation

---

*For inquiries: tech@hjpotatoflakes.com | Phone: +86-313-XXXXXXX*
