<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-07-industry-knowledge/china-vs-global-quality-standards-comparison.md --->

# China vs. Global Quality Standards: Comparative Analysis for Potato Flakes & Powder

**Document Type:** Technical Reference — Standards Comparison  
**Target Audience:** Quality assurance teams, regulatory compliance officers, procurement directors  
**Last Updated:** July 2026  
**Reference:** Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) — hjpotatoflakes.com

---

## 1. Introduction

International trade in potato flakes and potato powder requires compliance with multiple regulatory frameworks. Buyers in different markets reference distinct standards for food safety, quality parameters, labeling, and permissible additives. This document provides a comprehensive comparison of the major standards systems governing dehydrated potato products:

| Jurisdiction | Standards Body | Governing Regulation |
|--------------|---------------|---------------------|
| **China** | SAC / NHFPC | GB (National Standards), SB/T (Trade Standards) |
| **European Union** | EC / EFSA | EU Regulations 231/2012, 1169/2011, 1881/2006 |
| **United States** | FDA / USDA | 21 CFR Parts 101, 110, 165; GRAS notices |
| **Codex Alimentarius** | FAO/WHO Joint Program | Codex Standard 214-1999 (revised) |

---

## 2. Core Quality Parameters Compared

### 2.1 Physical and Chemical Parameters

| Parameter | China GB/T 31736-2015 | Codex CXS 214-1999 | EU Standards | US FDA / USDA |
|-----------|:---------------------:|:------------------:|:------------:|:-------------:|
| **Moisture content** | ≤8.0% | ≤7.0% | ≤6.5% (industry standard) | ≤7.0% (industry norm) |
| **Ash content (total)** | ≤4.5% | ≤4.0% | ≤3.5% | ≤4.0% |
| **Ash (insoluble in HCl)** | ≤0.1% | ≤0.1% | ≤0.1% | ≤0.1% |
| **Protein (N×6.25)** | ≥6.0% | ≥6.0% (typical) | ≥6.5% (typical) | ≥6.0% (typical) |
| **Starch content** | ≥70.0% | Report only | ≥72.0% (typical) | Report only |
| **SO₂ (sulfite) content** | ≤50 mg/kg | ≤50 mg/kg | ≤50 mg/kg (EU 231/2012 Annex III) | ≤50 mg/kg (21 CFR 182.3637) |
| **Heavy metals (Pb)** | ≤0.5 mg/kg | ≤1.0 mg/kg | ≤0.3 mg/kg | ≤0.5 mg/kg (21 CFR) |
| **Heavy metals (As)** | ≤0.5 mg/kg | ≤0.5 mg/kg | ≤0.3 mg/kg | ≤0.5 mg/kg |
| **Heavy metals (Cd)** | ≤0.2 mg/kg | ≤0.2 mg/kg | ≤0.2 mg/kg | ≤0.2 mg/kg |
| **Heavy metals (Hg)** | ≤0.02 mg/kg | ≤0.02 mg/kg | ≤0.02 mg/kg | ≤0.02 mg/kg |

### 2.2 Microbiological Parameters

| Parameter | China GB/T 31736-2015 (Level ≤ 10²) | Codex CXS 214-1999 | EU Commission Reg 2073/2005 | US FDA (CPG Sec 527.300) |
|-----------|:-----------------------------------:|:------------------:|:--------------------------:|:-------------------------:|
| **Aerobic Plate Count** | ≤10,000 CFU/g | ≤10,000 CFU/g (target) | ≤10,000 CFU/g | ≤10,000 CFU/g (guideline) |
| **Coliforms** | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤10 CFU/g | ≤10 CFU/g | ≤10 CFU/g |
| **E. coli** | ≤10 CFU/g | ≤3 CFU/g | ≤10 CFU/g (ESBL negative) | Negative in 1g |
| **Salmonella** | Absent in 25g | Absent in 25g | Absent in 25g | Absent in 25g |
| **Staphylococcus aureus** | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤100 CFU/g |
| **Yeast & Mold** | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤50 CFU/g (industry target) |
| **Bacillus cereus** | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤100 CFU/g | ≤100 CFU/g |

### 2.3 Sensory and Organoleptic Parameters

| Parameter | China GB/T 31736-2015 | Codex CXS 214-1999 | Typical EU Buyer Spec | Typical US Buyer Spec |
|-----------|:--------------------:|:------------------:|:--------------------:|:--------------------:|
| **Color** | White to light cream, uniform | White to light yellow typical | White to cream, uniform (Hunter L≥85) | White to cream (L≥85, a≤2, b≤18) |
| **Odor** | Characteristic, no off-notes | Characteristic, free from foreign odors | Characteristic potato, no rancid/stale | Characteristic, no off-flavors |
| **Taste** | Typical cooked potato, no bitterness | Typical cooked potato | Clean, neutral, no off-taste | Clean potato flavor, no bitterness |
| **Texture (reconstituted)** | Smooth, no lumping | Smooth, uniform | Smooth, creamy, no graininess | Smooth, no lumps, creamy mouthfeel |
| **Particle size** | ≥95% through 850 μm (20 mesh) | ≥90% through standard sieve | ≥95% through specified mesh | ≥95% through 20 mesh (850 μm) |
| **Foreign matter** | None visible | None visible | None visible | None visible |
| **Defects (dark specks)** | ≤5 per 100g | ≤3 per 100g (typical) | ≤3 per 100g | ≤2 per 100g (premium) |

---

## 3. Additive and Processing Aid Regulations

### 3.1 Permitted Additives for Dehydrated Potato Products

| Additive | China (GB 2760-2014) | EU (231/2012) | US (21 CFR) | Codex (CXS 192-1995) |
|----------|:--------------------:|:-------------:|:-----------:|:---------------------:|
| **Sodium sulfite** (preservative) | ✓ (max 50 mg/kg as SO₂) | ✓ (max 50 mg/kg as SO₂) | ✓ (GRAS, max 50 mg/kg) | ✓ (max 50 mg/kg) |
| **Potassium metabisulfite** | ✓ (max 50 mg/kg as SO₂) | ✓ (max 50 mg/kg as SO₂) | ✓ (GRAS, max 50 mg/kg) | ✓ (max 50 mg/kg) |
| **Ascorbic acid (Vitamin C)** | ✓ (QS) | ✓ (QS) | ✓ (GRAS) | ✓ (QS) |
| **Citric acid** | ✓ (QS) | ✓ (QS) | ✓ (GRAS) | ✓ (QS) |
| **Sodium acid pyrophosphate (SAPP)** | ✓ (max 2,000 mg/kg) | ✓ (max 2,500 mg/kg) | ✓ (GRAS) | ✓ (max 2,500 mg/kg) |
| **Calcium propionate** | ✓ (max 2,500 mg/kg) | ✓ (max 2,000 mg/kg) | ✓ (GRAS) | ✓ (max 2,000 mg/kg) |
| **TBHQ (antioxidant)** | ✓ (max 200 mg/kg) | ✗ (not permitted) | ✓ (max 200 mg/kg) | ✓ (max 200 mg/kg) |
| **BHA/BHT** | ✓ (max 200 mg/kg) | ✗ (not permitted) | ✓ (max 200 mg/kg) | ✓ (max 200 mg/kg) |
| **Mono/diglycerides** | ✓ (QS) | ✓ (QS) | ✓ (GRAS) | ✓ (QS) |

✓ = Permitted | ✗ = Not permitted | QS = Quantity sufficient, subject to GMP

> **Important Note:** The EU maintains a notably stricter policy regarding synthetic antioxidants (TBHQ, BHA, BHT), which are not permitted in dehydrated potatoes. Products destined for the European market must use natural alternatives or adjust formulation accordingly.

---

## 4. Labeling Requirements Comparison

| Requirement | China (GB 7718-2011) | EU (Reg 1169/2011) | US (21 CFR 101) | Codex (CXS 1-1985) |
|-------------|:-------------------:|:------------------:|:---------------:|:-------------------:|
| **Product name** | ✓ (mandatory) | ✓ (mandatory, legal name) | ✓ (mandatory, common/usual name) | ✓ (mandatory) |
| **Ingredient list** | ✓ (descending order) | ✓ (descending by weight) | ✓ (descending by weight) | ✓ (descending order) |
| **Allergen declaration** | ✓ (8 allergens, GB 7718) | ✓ (14 allergens, bold/highlight) | ✓ (FALCPA, 9 major allergens) | ✓ (CXS 1-1985) |
| **Net quantity** | ✓ (metric) | ✓ (metric, EU e-mark OK) | ✓ (metric and US customary) | ✓ (metric) |
| **Nutrition declaration** | ✓ (energy, protein, fat, CHO, Na) | ✓ (NRV per 100g/mL; 8 mandatory fields) | ✓ (Nutrition Facts panel) | ✓ (Guidelines for nutrition labeling) |
| **Country of origin** | ✓ (for imported products) | ✓ (if outside EU) | ✓ (COOL for certain products) | ✓ (if required in country of sale) |
| **Batch/lot identification** | ✓ (production date + lot) | ✓ (lot number, "L" prefix) | ✓ (lot/code) | ✓ (lot identification) |
| **Storage instructions** | ✓ (shelf life + storage) | ✓ (specific conditions if required) | ✓ ("Use By" or "Best Before") | ✓ (storage conditions) |
| **GMO labeling** | ✗ (voluntary) | ✓ (mandatory if >0.9% threshold) | ✓ (mandatory for bioengineered) | ✗ (refer to national legislation) |
| **Language** | Chinese | Official language(s) of member state | English | Language(s) of importing country |

---

## 5. Sampling, Testing, and Inspection Protocols

### 5.1 Standard Sampling Plans

| Standard | Sampling Method | Lot Acceptance | Notable Difference |
|----------|----------------|:--------------:|-------------------|
| **GB/T 31736-2015** | Random sampling per lot; minimum 1 kg sample | AQL 2.5 (normal) | Allows batch composite testing |
| **Codex CXS 214-1999** | ISO 2859 (AQL-based) | AQL 1.0 (normal) | Stricter AQL on defectives |
| **EU Regulation** | Regulation (EC) 882/2004; official control sampling | Risk-based, varies | Heavy emphasis on HACCP verification |
| **US FDA** | 21 CFR 110.35; FDA Compliance Program | FDA Risk-based PAT | Focuses on CGMP compliance, not lot-by-lot |

### 5.2 CIQ Export Inspection (China)

For export of potato flakes from China:

1. **Pre-export inspection** by local CIQ office
2. **Sample analysis** — full parameter testing at CNAS-accredited lab
3. **Health Certificate** issuance (Chinese + English bilingual format)
4. **Phytosanitary Certificate** from General Administration of Customs (GAC)
5. **Certificate of Origin** (for preferential tariff treatment under Free Trade Agreements)

---

## 6. Compliance Checklists by Target Market

### 6.1 For EU Market Entry

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| EU Regulation 231/2012 (additives) | Required | TBHQ/BHA/BHT prohibited |
| EC 2073/2005 (microbiological) | Required | Salmonella absent in 25g |
| EC 1881/2006 (contaminants) | Required | Cd ≤0.2, Pb ≤0.3 mg/kg |
| EU 1169/2011 (labeling) | Required | Allergens bold; 14 categories |
| BRCGS / IFS certification | Strongly recommended | Required by most EU retailers |
| Organic certification (if applicable) | EC 834/2007 + 889/2008 | Must be certified by EU-recognized CB |
| REACH compliance (packaging) | Required | Food contact materials regulation |

### 6.2 For US Market Entry

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| FDA Food Facility Registration | Mandatory (21 CFR 1.225) | Bioterrorism Act; biennial renewal |
| FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification | Mandatory | FSVP rule compliance |
| 21 CFR Part 110 (CGMP) | Required | Current good manufacturing practices |
| 21 CFR 101 (labeling) | Required | Nutrition Facts; allergen labeling |
| FDA Prior Notice | Required for each shipment | 2-hour notice minimum (sea) |
| SQF / BRCGS certification | Recommended | Most US buyers require GFSI certification |
| Organic certification (if applicable) | NOP/USDA certifier | Must use USDA-accredited certifier |

### 6.3 For China Domestic Market

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| GB/T 31736-2015 | Required | Product quality standard |
| GB 2760-2014 | Required | Additive usage standard |
| GB 7718-2011 | Required | Labeling standard |
| GB 28050-2011 | Required | Nutrition labeling |
| SC Food Production License | Required | QS/SC mark on packaging |
| Third-party testing report | Recommended | CNAS-accredited lab |

---

## 7. Convergence Efforts and Future Trends

International harmonization of potato product standards is progressing through several channels:

1. **Codex Alimentarius revisions** — CXS 214-1999 is under periodic review to reduce divergence between national standards
2. **China's GB standards modernization** — The 2023–2025 revision cycle for GB/T 31736 aims to align microbiological limits with Codex/EU norms
3. **Mutual recognition agreements** — China-EU Geographical Indications agreement (2021) and potential future food safety MRAs
4. **GFSI benchmarked schemes** — Increasing adoption of BRCGS, FSSC 22000, and SQF by Chinese exporters to bridge gap with international customer expectations

---

## 8. Summary Reference Table

| Topic | China (GB) | EU | US FDA | Codex Alimentarius |
|-------|:----------:|:--:|:------:|:------------------:|
| **Strictest moisture limit** | 8.0% | 6.5% | 7.0% | 7.0% |
| **Strictest Pb limit** | 0.5 mg/kg | 0.3 mg/kg | 0.5 mg/kg | 1.0 mg/kg |
| **Permits TBHQ** | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ | ✓ |
| **GMO labeling mandatory** | ✗ | ✓ (>0.9%) | ✓ (if bioengineered) | — |
| **Requires GFSI certification** | ✗ | ✓ (practical) | ✓ (practical) | Recommended |
| **Allergens declared** | 8 | 14 | 9 | Codex list |

---

## 9. References

1. GB/T 31736-2015 — Dehydrated Potato Products (China National Standard)
2. GB 2760-2014 — National Food Safety Standard for Food Additives (China)
3. Codex Alimentarius Standard 214-1999 — Standard for Dehydrated Potato Products
4. EU Regulation (EC) No 231/2012 — Specifications for Food Additives
5. EU Regulation (EC) No 1881/2006 — Maximum Levels for Contaminants in Foodstuffs
6. US FDA 21 CFR Parts 101, 110, 165 — Food Labeling, CGMP, Beverages
7. USDA AMS — Potato Grades and Standards
8. IEC (International Electrotechnical Commission) — Relevant food processing machinery standards
9. ISO 22000:2018 — Food Safety Management Systems
10. BRCGS Global Standard for Food Safety (Issue 9)

---

*This document provides a technical comparison of standards across jurisdictions. It is not a substitute for legal regulatory advice. Product specifications and compliance requirements should be verified with the buyer's regulatory team and applicable local regulations at the time of shipment.*

---

---

*This document is part of the Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) Technical Documentation Series. For more information about our vertically integrated potato supply chain — from seed breeding and cultivation to processing and global export — visit our official B2B website: [https://hjpotatoflakes.com](https://hjpotatoflakes.com)*



<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-07-industry-knowledge/export-documentation-international-trade-guide.md --->

# Export Documentation & International Trade Guide for Potato Flakes & Powder

**Document Type:** Operational Trade Guide  
**Target Audience:** Export sales teams, trade compliance officers, logistics coordinators  
**Last Updated:** July 2026  
**Reference:** Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) — hjpotatoflakes.com

---

## 1. Introduction

Exporting potato flakes and potato powder from China to international markets requires a comprehensive set of trade documents. Missing or incorrect documentation is the single most common cause of customs delays, cargo holds, and financial penalties. This guide covers the complete documentation chain required by **Standard Export Procedure from China** and typical documentation requested by international buyers across different markets.

---

## 2. Export Documentation Matrix

### 2.1 Required Documents by Shipment Type

| Document | FOB / CIF / CFR | L/C Documentary | Air Freight | Express Courier |
|----------|:---------------:|:---------------:|:-----------:|:---------------:|
| Commercial Invoice | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Packing List | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Bill of Lading (B/L) or Air Waybill (AWB) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Certificate of Origin (CO) | Requested | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Health Certificate / CIQ Certificate | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Phytosanitary Certificate | Required for most markets | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ (usually) |
| Certificate of Analysis (CoA) | Per buyer request | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Insurance Certificate (CIF only) | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Letter of Credit (if applicable) | — | ✓ | — | — |
| Fumigation Certificate (wood packaging) | ✓ (if wooden pallets used) | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ (if pallet-free) |
| Packing Declaration (wood packaging) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ |
| Certificate of Free Sale | Per buyer request | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ |
| Form E / Form F / etc. (FTA preferences) | Per importer request | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Certificate of Weight/Quantity | Per buyer request | ✓ | ✓ | ✗ |
| Beneficiary Certificate | Per L/C terms | ✓ | — | — |
| Shipping Advice | Per L/C terms | ✓ | ✓ | — |
| GMO-Free Certificate | Per buyer/EU request | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| BRCGS / FSSC Certificate | Per buyer request | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

---

## 3. Detailed Document Requirements

### 3.1 Commercial Invoice

The commercial invoice is the central document for customs valuation. Key requirements:

| Field | Requirement | Notes |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| Shipper (exporter) | Full legal name, address, tax ID | Must match registered exporter in GAC system |
| Consignee | Full legal name, address | May differ from notify party (in B/L) |
| Product description | Full HS Code and commercial description | HS 0712.90.9000 (dehydrated vegetables) |
| Quantity | Net and gross weight (kg), number of packages | |
| Unit price & total value | USD or EUR; Incoterms specification | e.g., "CIF Rotterdam" or "FOB Tianjin" |
| Payment terms | e.g., "T/T 30% deposit, 70% against B/L copy" | |
| Country of origin | "China" | |
| Port of loading / discharge | e.g., "Tianjin, China → Jebel Ali, UAE" | |
| Marks and numbers | As per packing list and B/L | |
| Invoice number and date | Unique per shipment | |

### 3.2 Packing List

Provides detailed breakdown of cargo contents:

| Item | Specification |
|------|-------------|
| Number of packages | Total cartons, bags, drums, or pallets |
| Net weight per package | e.g., 25 kg net/bag |
| Gross weight per package | Includes packaging weight |
| Dimensions per package | e.g., 40 × 30 × 20 cm |
| Total net weight | Sum of all packages |
| Total gross weight | Sum including all packaging materials |
| Pallet information | Number of pallets, pallet dimensions |
| Container information | Container number, seal number, container tare weight |

### 3.3 Bill of Lading (B/L)

Three main types used in potato flake trade:

| Type | Description | When Used |
|------|-------------|-----------|
| Original B/L (negotiable) | "To Order" or "To Order of Shipper"; endorsed to consignee | L/C transactions; buyer needs original for cargo release |
| Sea Waybill (non-negotiable) | Straight consignment to named consignee | Prepaid shipments; trusted buyer relationships |
| Telex Release | Electronic release without original B/L | Fast shipment; buyer requires no original documents |

**Typical B/L details for potato flake shipments:**

- Container type: 20' GP (typically 26–28 MT loading for flakes)
- B/L weight: gross weight of all cargo incl. packing
- Shipped on board date: critical for L/C compliance
- Place of receipt: Tianjin, Qingdao, or Shanghai (primary China ports for potato products)

### 3.4 Certificate of Origin (CO)

| Certificate Type | Administered By | Purpose |
|-----------------|-----------------|---------|
| **GSP Form A** | CIQ (China Customs) | Generalized System of Preferences — preferential duty rates for developing countries |
| **Form B** (ASEAN-China FTA) | CIQ | Duty preference for ASEAN member states |
| **Form E** (ASEAN-China FTA v2) | CIQ | Updated ASEAN-China FTA certificate |
| **Form F** (China-Chile FTA) | CIQ | Duty preference for exports to Chile |
| **Form X** (China-Switzerland FTA) | CIQ | Duty preference for exports to Switzerland |
| **Form P** (China-Pakistan FTA) | CIQ | Duty preference for exports to Pakistan |
| **Form M** (China-New Zealand FTA) | CIQ | Duty preference for exports to New Zealand |
| **COO (non-preferential)** | China Chamber of Commerce (CCPIT) | Generic certificate for countries without FTA |

### 3.5 CIQ Health Certificate (卫生证书 / Inspection Certificate)

Issued by the General Administration of Customs of China (GAC, formerly CIQ) at the port of export.

**Required tests typically include:**

- Organoleptic inspection (color, odor, texture, foreign matter)
- Moisture content determination
- Heavy metals screening (Pb, As, Cd, Hg)
- Microbiological analysis (Salmonella, E. coli, APC, yeast & mold)
- Sulfur dioxide (SO₂) content
- Pesticide residue screening (if required by destination market)

**Validity:** Typically 7–15 days from the date of issue. The certificate covers a specific lot/batch.

### 3.6 Phytosanitary Certificate

Mandatory for nearly all plant-based product exports to prevent the spread of quarantine pests. Issued by GAC/Plant Quarantine Office.

**Key declarations for potato flakes:**

- Product has been processed (heat-dried) to eliminate pest risk
- Free from quarantine pests listed by the importing country
- No live insects, mites, or fungal pathogens detected
- Packing materials are pest-free (if applicable)

**Countries with specific phytosanitary requirements for Chinese potato products:**

| Country | Additional Requirement | Reference |
|---------|----------------------|-----------|
| Australia | BIP (Biosecurity Import Permit) + treatment certification | BICON import conditions |
| South Korea | Additional WPM (Wood Packaging Material) inspection | Korean Plant Quarantine |
| Japan | Japanese Plant Protection Station inspection at arrival | Plant Protection Law |
| United States | APHIS PPQ Form 203; treatments per 7 CFR 319 | USDA APHIS regulations |
| EU countries | Plant passport equivalency for processed products | EU Plant Health Regulation 2016/2031 |
| Saudi Arabia | SASO-certified lab testing for aflatoxins, pesticides | SASO standards |
| Brazil | MAPA registration + phytosanitary certification | IN 39/2017 |

### 3.7 Certificate of Analysis (CoA)

Issued by the manufacturer's QA department (or third-party lab) confirming the product meets specifications.

**Standard CoA parameters for potato flakes:**

1. **Physical:** Moisture, bulk density, particle size distribution, color (Hunter L/a/b)
2. **Chemical:** Protein, starch, ash, SO₂, reducing sugars, total sugars, pH (reconstituted)
3. **Microbiological:** APC, coliforms, E. coli, Salmonella, yeast & mold, B. cereus
4. **Extraneous matter:** Dark specks count, foreign matter

### 3.8 Fumigation Certificate

Required by most countries when wood packaging materials (pallets, dunnage) are used. Must comply with **ISPM 15** (International Standards for Phytosanitary Measures No. 15).

- Treatment: Heat treatment (HT) to 56°C core for 30 minutes, or Methyl Bromide fumigation (MB)
- Marking: IPPC logo + country code + treatment facility code + treatment type (HT/MB)

> **Recommendation:** Use heat-treated (HT) pallets for European and US markets. Methyl bromide (MB) is being phased out in many jurisdictions.

### 3.9 Certificate of Free Sale (CFS)

For food products exported to certain markets (primarily Middle East, Africa, Latin America):

- Certified by China Chamber of Commerce for Import & Export of Foodstuffs (CCCFNA) or equivalent
- Declares the product is legally sold and freely available in the domestic Chinese market
- Valid for 1–2 years from date of issue

---

## 4. Letter of Credit (L/C) Compliance

### 4.1 Most Common L/C Types for Potato Flake Trade

| Type | Description | Risk Level for Exporter |
|------|-------------|:----------------------:|
| Irrevocable, Confirmed L/C | Adding bank confirmation; payment guaranteed by confirming bank | Low |
| Irrevocable, Unconfirmed L/C | Payment obligation from issuing bank only | Moderate |
| Sight L/C | Payment upon presentation of compliant documents | Low |
| Usance (Time) L/C | Payment at 30, 60, 90 days after B/L date | Moderate |
| Revolving L/C | Multiple shipments under a single L/C structure | Low |

### 4.2 Critical L/C Compliance Checklist for Potato Flake Shipments

| Item | Rule | Common Discrepancy |
|------|------|--------------------|
| **Latest shipment date** | Must ship on or before date; never after | Late shipment = discrepancy |
| **L/C expiry** | Documents must be presented by expiry date | Late presentation = discrepancy |
| **B/L presentation** | Typically within 21 days from B/L date | Backdated / stale B/L not accepted |
| **Product description** | Must match L/C exactly; no abbreviation unless specified | Partial or incorrect description |
| **Weight tolerance** | Typically ±5% unless specified otherwise | Over/under shipment tolerance exceeded |
| **Non-negotiable docs** | All copies as per L/C checklist | Missing copy = discrepancy |
| **Third-party docs** | Usually acceptable unless expressly prohibited | May require beneficiary reference inclusion |
| **Partial shipments** | Must check: allowed or prohibited | Sending partial when prohibited |
| **Transshipment** | Must check: allowed or prohibited | Important for Tianjin → transshipment at Hong Kong/Singapore |

### 4.3 Typical L/C Discrepancy Costs

| Discrepancy | Bank Charge | Potential Penalty |
|-------------|:----------:|:-----------------:|
| Late shipment | CHF 50–150 per discrepancy | Rejection of documents |
| Missing document | CHF 50–150 per discrepancy | Buyer may refuse payment |
| Description mismatch | CHF 50–150 per discrepancy | Waiver required from buyer |
| Weight deviation >5% | CHF 50–150 per discrepancy | Retrading or refusal |

---

## 5. Incoterms 2020 for Potato Flake Shipments

| Incoterm | Shipper Responsibility | Risk Transfer Point | Recommended For |
|----------|----------------------|---------------------|-----------------|
| **EXW** (Ex Works) | Make goods available at factory | Buyer's premises | Domestic shipments only |
| **FOB** Tianjin/Qingdao/Shanghai | Load goods on vessel | On board vessel | Bulk shipments, experienced buyers |
| **CFR** (Cost & Freight) | FOB + ocean freight | On board vessel | Standard for most international sales |
| **CIF** (Cost, Insurance & Freight) | CFR + marine insurance | On board vessel | Buyers in developing markets |
| **DAP** / **DDP** | Full door-to-door logistics | At destination | Premium buyers, full-service model |

---

## 6. Document Flow Timeline

### Standard Shipment Timeline (FOB Tianjin → Rotterdam)

| Day | Event | Document Generated |
|:--:|-------|-------------------|
| D-30 | Sale confirmation | Sales Contract / Proforma Invoice |
| D-25 | Buyer opens L/C (if L/C transaction) | Letter of Credit |
| D-20 | Production scheduled | Production Schedule |
| D-5 | Production complete | Certificate of Analysis (in-house) |
| D-4 | CIQ inspection | CIQ Health Certificate, Phytosanitary Certificate |
| D-3 | Container loading | Container loading photos, seal number |
| D-2 | Customs declaration | Export Customs Declaration Form |
| D-1 | Port loading | Mate's Receipt, dock receipt |
| D (sailing date) | Vessel departure | Bill of Lading, Shipping Advice |
| D+3 | Document presentation to bank (L/C) | Full set of shipping docs |
| D+7–10 | Payment received (sight L/C or T/T) | Bank payment confirmation |

---

## 7. Common Documentation Pitfalls and Solutions

| Issue | Consequence | Prevention |
|-------|-------------|------------|
| CIQ certificate validity expires before vessel arrival | Cargo may be held for re-inspection | Schedule CIQ inspection within 3–5 days of loading |
| Phytosanitary certificate doesn't mention specific pest list | Rejection by importing country customs | Verify importing country's specific requirements before application |
| Commercial invoice and B/L weight mismatch | L/C discrepancy or customs hold | Double-check all weight figures before document issuance |
| Wood pallets without ISPM 15 marking | Rejection at destination; fumigation on arrival | Use HT-marked pallets only |
| L/C document list incomplete | Discrepancy — deduction CHF 50–150 each | Develop a pre-presentation checklist matched to L/C terms |
| Country of origin rule not verified for FTA preference | Denied preferential tariff treatment | Verify product meets the specific ROO (e.g., regional value content ≥40%) |

---

## 8. Digitalization and E-Document Trends

The global trade documentation landscape is modernizing rapidly:

| Platform/Initiative | Scope | Impact on Potato Flake Trade |
|--------------------|-------|------------------------------|
| **e-B/L (electronic B/L)** | Electronic transfer of title documents | Reduces document transit from 2 weeks to seconds |
| **China International Trade Single Window** | One-stop digital customs clearance | Faster CIQ document issuance (1–3 hours vs 1–2 days) |
| **Blockchain trade finance** | Digital L/C and smart contracts | Reduced discrepancy processing time |
| **EU ICS2** | Import Control System 2 for security | Additional data elements required for EU-bound cargo |
| **WCO Data Model** | Standardized electronic customs messaging | Long-term convergence of document formats |

---

## 9. References

1. China General Administration of Customs (GAC) — Export Documentation Guidelines portal
2. International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) — Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits (UCP 600)
3. ICC — Incoterms 2020 Official Rules
4. USDA APHIS — Export Certification Requirements by Country
5. ISPM 15 — Regulation of Wood Packaging Material in International Trade (IPPC/FAO)
6. EU Commission — Trade Helpdesk: Documentation Requirements for Imported Food Products
7. China Customs Statistics Office — Standard Export Procedure Handbook
8. Hongji Agriculture Export Team — Standard Operating Procedures (Internal Document, Rev. 2026)

---

*This guide reflects standard practices for exporting dehydrated potato products from China as of July 2026. Import requirements may change. Exporters should verify current requirements with their local GAC office and the importing country's customs authority before each shipment.*

---

---

*This document is part of the Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) Technical Documentation Series. For more information about our vertically integrated potato supply chain — from seed breeding and cultivation to processing and global export — visit our official B2B website: [https://hjpotatoflakes.com](https://hjpotatoflakes.com)*



<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-07-industry-knowledge/glossary-potato-processing-terms.md --->

# Glossary of Potato Processing Terms (中英对照行业术语表)

**Document Type:** Bilingual Technical Reference  
**Target Audience:** International procurement teams, QA inspectors, plant engineers, translators  
**Last Updated:** July 2026  
**Reference:** Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) — hjpotatoflakes.com

---

## 1. Purpose and Usage

This glossary contains over 200 industry-specific terms covering potato processing, quality control, international trade, and food manufacturing. Terms are presented in English with Chinese (Simplified) equivalents. Entries are organized by category for ease of reference.

**Abbreviations used:** (n.) = noun, (v.) = verb, (adj.) = adjective, (pref.) = prefix

---

## 2. Raw Material & Varieties (原料与品种)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 1 | Atlantic | 大西洋 | Popular processing variety; high solids, light color, round-oval tuber |
| 2 | Shepody | 夏波蒂 | Long, white-skinned variety; excellent for flakes, french fries |
| 3 | Russet Burbank | 布尔班克（褐皮） | Classic variety; high starch, russet skin, good for flakes & baking |
| 4 | Seed potato | 种薯 | Virus-free tubers used for planting commercial crops |
| 5 | Certified seed | 认证种薯 | Seed potatoes meeting official health and purity standards |
| 6 | Tuber | 块茎 | The underground stem of the potato plant; the harvested crop unit |
| 7 | Specific gravity | 比重 | Measure of tuber solids content, typically 1.080–1.095 for processing |
| 8 | Dry matter content | 干物质含量 | Solids content of potato; typically 18–25% for processing varieties |
| 9 | Starch content | 淀粉含量 | Percentage of starch in potato dry matter; 60–80% typical |
| 10 | Reducing sugars | 还原糖 | Glucose + fructose content; critical for browning control (<0.15% desired) |
| 11 | Sucrose | 蔗糖 | Non-reducing disaccharide; precursor to reducing sugars during storage |
| 12 | Total sugars | 总糖 | Sum of all sugar components; affects Maillard reaction during processing |
| 13 | Blackspot bruise | 黑斑挫伤 | Internal discoloration from impact damage; affected by potassium levels |
| 14 | Hollow heart | 空心 | Internal cavity in large tubers; defect in processing quality |
| 15 | Greening | 绿变 | Chlorophyll development from light exposure; indicates solanine risk |
| 16 | Second growth | 二次生长 | Abnormal tuber shape due to stress; affects peeling efficiency |
| 17 | Sugar ends (dark ends) | 糖端 | High sugar accumulation in stem end; causes uneven fry color |
| 18 | Cold-induced sweetening | 冷诱导甜化 | Conversion of starch to sugar at temperatures below 4°C |
| 19 | Reconditioning | 回温处理 | Holding potatoes at 10–15°C to reduce sugars after cold storage |
| 20 | Isopentenyl pyrophosphate | 异戊烯基焦磷酸 | Precursor in terpene metabolism; related to off-flavors |

---

## 3. Processing Operations (加工工艺)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 21 | Washing | 清洗 | Initial removal of soil and debris from raw tubers |
| 22 | Brushing | 刷洗 | Mechanical cleaning using rotary brushes to remove peel residues |
| 23 | Destoning | 去石 | Removal of stones and heavy debris by density separation |
| 24 | Sorting | 分选 | Manual or optical separation of defective tubers |
| 25 | Peeling | 去皮 | Removal of potato skin (steam, lye, or abrasion methods) |
| 26 | Steam peeling | 蒸汽去皮 | High-pressure steam (12–18 bar) to loosen skin for mechanical removal |
| 27 | Lye peeling | 碱液去皮 | Sodium hydroxide solution (3–15%) at 70–100°C to dissolve skin |
| 28 | Abrasion peeling | 摩擦去皮 | Mechanical removal of skin using abrasive rollers or drums |
| 29 | Inspection (trimming) | 挑选修整 | Manual removal of defects, eyes, and remaining skin |
| 30 | Slicing | 切片 | Cutting potatoes into thin slices for chip/flake production |
| 31 | Dicing | 切丁 | Cutting into uniform cube-shaped pieces |
| 32 | Sizing | 规格分切 | Ensuring uniform particle/dice size through sieving or cutting |
| 33 | Blanching | 烫漂 | Hot water or steam treatment to inactivate enzymes (polyphenol oxidase) |
| 34 | Hot water blanching | 热水烫漂 | 70–100°C water bath; standard method for enzyme inactivation |
| 35 | Steam blanching | 蒸汽烫漂 | Steam application; less leaching of solids compared to water blanching |
| 36 | Cooling | 冷却 | Post-blanch temperature reduction using cold water or air |
| 37 | Cooking | 蒸煮 | Complete gelatinization of starch; typically steam at 100–105°C |
| 38 | Ricing | 碎粒 | Mechanical breakdown of cooked potatoes into small particles |
| 39 | Drying | 干燥 | Moisture removal to achieve target water content (≤7.0% typical) |
| 40 | Drum drying | 滚筒干燥 | Thin film on heated rotating drum (typically 2–5 rpm, 150–170°C) |
| 41 | Single-drum dryer | 单滚筒干燥机 | One drum; used for lower throughput applications |
| 42 | Double-drum dryer | 双滚筒干燥机 | Two counter-rotating drums; higher capacity, more uniform drying |
| 43 | Flaking | 成片脱落 | Process of dried film being scraped off drum as thin flakes |
| 44 | Milling / Grinding | 粉碎研磨 | Reducing flake or granule size into powder form |
| 45 | Sieving / Screening | 过筛分级 | Particle size separation through vibrating screens or rotary sifters |
| 46 | Hammermill | 锤式粉碎机 | Impact mill for size reduction of potato flakes into powder |
| 47 | Pin mill | 针磨 | High-speed impact mill for fine grinding to powder consistency |
| 48 | Air classification | 气流分级 | Particle separation by density/weight using air streams |
| 49 | Agglomeration | 附聚造粒 | Process of binding fine particles into larger, free-flowing granules |
| 50 | Instantization | 速溶化 | Process making product instantly dispersible/reconstitutable in liquid |
| 51 | Reconstitution | 复水 | Addition of water/milk to dehydrated product to restore mashed potato |
| 52 | Cell rupture | 细胞破裂 | Percentage of potato cells damaged during processing; affects texture |
| 53 | Free amylose | 游离直链淀粉 | Leached amylose starch from damaged cells; affects stickiness/gumminess |
| 54 | Starch damage | 淀粉损伤 | Mechanical damage to starch granules during processing |
| 55 | Maillard reaction | 美拉德反应 | Non-enzymatic browning between reducing sugars and amino acids |
| 56 | Caramelization | 焦糖化 | Thermal breakdown of sugars causing color formation (>160°C) |
| 57 | Enzymatic browning | 酶促褐变 | Browning caused by polyphenol oxidase (PPO) activity on phenolics |

---

## 4. Quality Control & Testing (质量控制与检测)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 58 | Moisture content | 水分含量 | Water percentage in final product; measured by oven-drying method |
| 59 | Karl Fischer titration | 卡尔费休滴定 | Precision moisture analysis method for low-moisture products |
| 60 | Water activity (Aw) | 水分活度 | Free water available for microbial growth; ≤0.60 for flakes |
| 61 | Bulk density | 松密度 | Weight per unit volume (g/L or kg/m³); important for packaging |
| 62 | Particle size distribution | 粒径分布 | % of particles retained on specific mesh sizes |
| 63 | Hunter L-a-b values | 亨特色值 | Color measurement: L (lightness), a (red-green), b (yellow-blue) |
| 64 | Whiteness index | 白度指数 | Calculated from reflectance measurements; higher is better for flakes |
| 65 | Viscosity | 黏度 | Resistance to flow of reconstituted product; Brabender or Rapid Visco Analyzer |
| 66 | Cold water viscosity | 冷水黏度 | Viscosity of product when mixed with cold water |
| 67 | Hot paste viscosity | 热糊黏度 | Viscosity of gelatinized starch paste after cooking |
| 68 | Sediment content | 沉淀物含量 | Insoluble residue after reconstitution; defect indicator |
| 69 | Total plate count (TPC/APC) | 菌落总数 | Aerobic bacterial count; indicator of general hygiene |
| 70 | Coliform count | 大肠菌群计数 | Indicator of fecal contamination; critical hygiene parameter |
| 71 | Escherichia coli (E. coli) | 大肠杆菌 | Pathogenic indicator; must be absent in 1g product |
| 72 | Salmonella | 沙门氏菌 | Key pathogen; must be absent in 25g product |
| 73 | Yeast & mold count | 酵母菌和霉菌计数 | Spoilage organism indicator; affects shelf life |
| 74 | Bacillus cereus | 蜡样芽胞杆菌 | Spore-forming pathogen; risk in dehydrated products |
| 75 | Sulfur dioxide (SO₂) | 二氧化硫 | Preservative; residual levels must meet regulatory limits (≤50 mg/kg) |
| 76 | Heavy metals | 重金属 | Pb, As, Cd, Hg testing; regulatory maximum levels vary by market |
| 77 | Pesticide residues | 农药残留 | Screening for compliance with MRLs of importing country |
| 78 | Acrylamide | 丙烯酰胺 | Process contaminant formed during high-heat processing (Maillard) |
| 79 | Dark specks / Black specks | 黑点 | Visible discolored particles; customer complaint driver |
| 80 | Foreign matter | 异物 | Any extraneous material; zero tolerance in food products |
| 81 | Defect count | 缺陷计数 | Number of visible defects per unit weight (typically per 100g) |
| 82 | Organoleptic evaluation | 感官评价 | Sensory assessment of color, odor, taste, and mouthfeel |
| 83 | Triangle test | 三角测试 | Sensory discrimination test to detect differences between batches |
| 84 | Shelf life study | 保质期研究 | Accelerated aging protocol to determine product stability |
| 85 | Accelerated shelf life testing | 加速保质期测试 | Storage at elevated temperature/humidity to predict long-term stability |
| 86 | Microscopic examination | 显微镜检查 | Checking starch granule damage, cell structure, and contaminants |

---

## 5. Packaging & Storage (包装与储存)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 87 | Multi-wall paper bag | 多层纸袋 | 3–5 ply kraft paper bags for bulk potato flakes |
| 88 | PE inner liner | 聚乙烯内袋 | Polyethylene film bag inside outer packaging for moisture barrier |
| 89 | Aluminum foil liner | 铝箔内袋 | High-barrier liner for sensitive products or long shelf life |
| 90 | BOPP / PET bag | BOPP/PET 袋 | Laminated bags with enhanced barrier properties |
| 91 | Laminated woven bag | 覆膜编织袋 | Woven polypropylene bag with PE lamination; export standard |
| 92 | Vacuum packaging | 真空包装 | Air removal to extend shelf life; reduces oxidation |
| 93 | Modified atmosphere packaging (MAP) | 气调包装 | Nitrogen/CO₂ flushing to inhibit spoilage |
| 94 | Bulk bag / FIBC / Jumbo bag | 吨袋 | Flexible Intermediate Bulk Container (500–1000 kg) |
| 95 | Octabin | 八角箱 | Large corrugated cardboard container with inner liner |
| 96 | Corrugated carton | 瓦楞纸箱 | Export-grade single/double/triple wall cartons |
| 97 | Pallet | 托盘 | Wood, plastic, or paper base for unitized loading |
| 98 | Euro pallet | 欧标托盘 | 800 × 1200 mm; standard in European logistics |
| 99 | GMA pallet | GMA 托盘 | 1200 × 1000 mm; standard in North American logistics |
| 100 | China-standard pallet | 国标托盘 | 1200 × 1000 mm or 1100 × 1100 mm; domestic trade |
| 101 | Stretch wrap / Pallet wrap | 拉伸缠绕膜 | Stretchable PE film for securing palletized loads |
| 102 | Shrink wrap | 收缩膜 | Heat-shrinkable PE film for weather protection |
| 103 | Pallet corner protectors | 护角 | Cardboard or plastic L-edges to protect bag corners |
| 104 | Edge protector | 护棱 | V-shaped protector for strap edges |
| 105 | Strapping / Banding | 打包带 | PP or PET strapping for pallet unitization |
| 106 | Silica gel desiccant | 硅胶干燥剂 | Moisture-absorbing sachets for container protection |
| 107 | Oxygen absorber | 脱氧剂 | Iron-based packet that scavenges residual oxygen |
| 108 | Place pack | 定位包装 | Pre-portioned consumer-sized packs for retail |
| 109 | Controlled atmosphere storage | 气调贮藏 | Cold storage with regulated O₂/CO₂ levels for fresh potatoes |
| 110 | POS (Processing-Origin-Storage) | 产地贮藏 | On-farm storage facilities near processing plant |
| 111 | RH (Relative Humidity) | 相对湿度 | Humidity level in storage; 50–60% recommended for flakes |

---

## 6. Logistics & Shipping (物流与运输)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 112 | Container | 集装箱 | Standardized steel box for intermodal freight transport |
| 113 | 20' GP (General Purpose) | 20尺普柜 | 20-foot container; typical capacity ≈26–28 MT flakes |
| 114 | 40' GP | 40尺普柜 | 40-foot container; typical capacity ≈26–28 MT flakes (volume limited) |
| 115 | 40' HC (High Cube) | 40尺高柜 | 40-foot, 9.5' tall; extra volume for lighter products |
| 116 | Reefer container | 冷藏集装箱 | Temperature-controlled container; for fresh or temp-sensitive products |
| 117 | FCL (Full Container Load) | 整箱 | One shipper uses entire container |
| 118 | LCL (Less than Container Load) | 拼箱 | Multiple shippers share container space |
| 119 | TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit) | 标准箱单位 | Standard measure for container capacity |
| 120 | Port of loading (POL) | 装运港 | e.g., Tianjin, Qingdao, Shanghai |
| 121 | Port of discharge (POD) | 卸货港 | e.g., Rotterdam, Los Angeles, Jebel Ali |
| 122 | Free time (demurrage) | 免费滞箱期 | Days allowed at destination before container detention charges apply |
| 123 | Detention | 滞箱费 | Charges if container returned late to shipping line |
| 124 | Demurrage | 滞港费 | Charges if container not cleared from terminal on time |
| 125 | FOB (Free on Board) | 装运港船上交货 | Incoterm: seller delivers goods on board vessel |
| 126 | CIF (Cost, Insurance, Freight) | 成本加保险费运费 | Incoterm: seller pays freight and insurance to destination port |
| 127 | DAP (Delivered at Place) | 目的地交货 | Incoterm: seller delivers goods at named destination |
| 128 | DDP (Delivered Duty Paid) | 完税后交货 | Incoterm: seller bears all costs and risks to final delivery |
| 129 | Shipping mark | 唛头 | Identification markings on cargo packaging |
| 130 | Hazardous goods declaration | 危险品申报 | Required for certain fumigants or processing aids |
| 131 | VGM (Verified Gross Mass) | 验证总重 | Mandatory container weight verification for SOLAS compliance |
| 132 | Transit time | 运输时间 | Days from port of loading to port of discharge |
| 133 | Transshipment | 中转运 | Cargo transferred from one vessel to another at intermediate port |

---

## 7. Trade & Documentation (贸易与单证)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 134 | Proforma Invoice | 形式发票 | Preliminary invoice issued before final order confirmation |
| 135 | Commercial Invoice | 商业发票 | Official invoice for customs clearance and payment |
| 136 | Packing List | 装箱单 | Detailed weight and package count document |
| 137 | Certificate of Origin (CO) | 原产地证 | Proof of product's country of manufacture |
| 138 | GSP Form A | 普惠制产地证A | Generalized System of Preferences certificate for duty reduction |
| 139 | Bill of Lading (B/L) | 提单 | Title document for ocean freight cargo |
| 140 | Master B/L | 主提单 | Carrier's bill of lading |
| 141 | House B/L | 分提单 | Freight forwarder's bill of lading |
| 142 | Telex Release | 电放 | Electronic release of cargo without original B/L |
| 143 | Air Waybill (AWB) | 空运提单 | Non-negotiable document for air freight shipments |
| 144 | CIQ Certificate / Health Certificate | 卫生证书 / 健康证书 | Chinese official inspection certificate for export food |
| 145 | Phytosanitary Certificate | 植物检疫证书 | Certificate confirming freedom from quarantine pests |
| 146 | Fumigation Certificate | 熏蒸证书 | Certificate confirming wood packaging treatment (ISPM 15) |
| 147 | Certificate of Analysis (CoA) | 分析证书 / 检测报告 | Manufacturer's analysis of product quality parameters |
| 148 | Certificate of Free Sale (CFS) | 自由销售证书 | Confirms product is legally sold in the domestic market |
| 149 | Letter of Credit (L/C) | 信用证 | Bank-guaranteed payment instrument in international trade |
| 150 | L/C at sight | 即期信用证 | Payment upon presentation of compliant documents |
| 151 | Usance L/C | 远期信用证 | Payment at a specified future date (30–90 days) |
| 152 | SWIFT MT700 | SWIFT MT700 | Standard message format for L/C issuance |
| 153 | UCP 600 | 跟单信用证统一惯例 600 | ICC guidelines governing documentary L/Cs |
| 154 | Insurance Certificate | 保险凭证 | Evidence of cargo insurance coverage (typically 110% of CIF value) |
| 155 | ICC Marine Cargo Clauses | 协会货物保险条款 | Standard insurance coverage (A/B/C) per Institute of London Underwriters |
| 156 | Warehousing and forwarding | 仓储和货运代理 | Logistics services for export consolidation |
| 157 | Incoterms 2020 | 国际贸易术语通则 2020 | Standardized trade terms by the International Chamber of Commerce |
| 158 | HS Code (Harmonized System Code) | 海关编码 | Standardized commodity classification code for customs |

---

## 8. Quality Management & Certification (质量管理与认证)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 159 | HACCP | 危害分析与关键控制点 | Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points; systematic preventive approach |
| 160 | CCP (Critical Control Point) | 关键控制点 | Step where control can be applied to prevent/eliminate a hazard |
| 161 | Critical limit | 关键限值 | Tolerable range at a CCP; e.g., temperature ≥85°C for blanching |
| 162 | PRP (Prerequisite Program) | 前提方案 | Basic environmental and operational conditions for food safety |
| 163 | OPRP (Operational PRP) | 操作性前提方案 | PRP identified as essential to control a specific hazard |
| 164 | GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) | 良好生产规范 | Regulatory requirements for food production environments |
| 165 | SSOP (Sanitation Standard Operating Procedure) | 卫生标准操作程序 | Written procedures for cleaning and sanitation |
| 166 | SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) | 标准操作规程 | Step-by-step instructions for specific operations |
| 167 | ISO 9001 | ISO 9001 质量管理体系 | General quality management system standard |
| 168 | ISO 22000 | ISO 22000 食品安全管理体系 | International food safety management system standard |
| 169 | FSSC 22000 | FSSC 22000 | GFSI-recognized food safety certification scheme (ISO 22000 + PAS 220/ISO/TS 22002) |
| 170 | BRCGS Global Standard for Food Safety | BRCGS 食品安全全球标准 | GFSI-benchmarked standard widely required by UK/EU retailers |
| 171 | SQF (Safe Quality Food) | SQF 食品安全与质量规范 | GFSI-benchmarked standard; widely required in North America |
| 172 | IFS (International Featured Standards) | IFS 国际卓越标准 | GFSI-benchmarked standard; common in German/French retail |
| 173 | GFSI (Global Food Safety Initiative) | 全球食品安全倡议 | Benchmarking body that recognizes major food safety schemes |
| 174 | GMO-free certification | 非转基因认证 | Certificate confirming product from non-GMO sources |
| 175 | Organic certification | 有机认证 | Certificate according to EU/USDA/China organic standards |
| 176 | Kosher certification | 犹太洁食认证 | Certification for Jewish dietary law compliance (OU, OK, Star-K) |
| 177 | Halal certification | 清真认证 | Certification for Islamic dietary law compliance |
| 178 | Traceability | 可追溯性 | Ability to track product from source through processing to shipment |
| 179 | Lot number / Batch number | 批号 | Unique identifier for a production batch |
| 180 | Mock recall | 模拟召回 | Practice exercise to test traceability system effectiveness |
| 181 | Food allergen | 食品过敏原 | Substance causing allergic reactions; 8 (China) / 14 (EU) categories |
| 182 | Cross-contamination | 交叉污染 | Transfer of hazard (allergen, pathogen) from one product to another |
| 183 | Good Warehousing Practice (GWP) | 良好仓储规范 | Practices for safe storage of food products |

---

## 9. Regulatory & Compliance (法规与合规)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 184 | Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) | 最大残留限量 | Maximum allowed level of pesticide residue in/on food |
| 185 | ADI (Acceptable Daily Intake) | 每日允许摄入量 | Amount of substance that can be consumed daily without health risk |
| 186 | GRAS (Generally Recognized as Safe) | 公认安全 | FDA designation for substances safe under conditions of intended use |
| 187 | Prior Notice (US FDA) | 预先通知 | Notification to FDA before food import shipment arrives |
| 188 | FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) | 美国食品安全现代化法案 | US law shifting focus from response to prevention |
| 189 | FSVP (Foreign Supplier Verification Program) | 国外供应商验证计划 | FSMA rule requiring importers to verify foreign suppliers' food safety |
| 190 | EU Rapid Alert System for Food and Feed (RASFF) | 欧盟食品饲料快速预警系统 | EU system for notifying food safety risks across member states |
| 191 | China GAC (General Administration of Customs) | 中国海关总署 | National customs authority; successor to CIQ/SAQSIQ |
| 192 | China National Standard (GB) | 国家标准 | Mandatory Chinese national standard (Guobiao) |
| 193 | Recommended Chinese Standard (GB/T) | 推荐性国家标准 | Voluntary Chinese national standard |
| 194 | Trade standard (SB/T) | 行业标准（SB/T） | Sector-specific standard issued by Ministry of Commerce |
| 195 | SC Production License (QS replaced) | 食品生产许可证 | China's food production license mark (replaced QS in 2018) |
| 196 | Food Additive | 食品添加剂 | Substance added to food for technological or sensory purposes |
| 197 | Processing aid | 加工助剂 | Substance used during processing, not intended for final product |

---

## 10. Potato Chemistry & Nutrition (马铃薯化学与营养)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 198 | Amylose | 直链淀粉 | Linear starch polymer; 20–30% of potato starch; affects retrogradation |
| 199 | Amylopectin | 支链淀粉 | Branched starch polymer; 70–80% of potato starch; affects gelatinization |
| 200 | Gelatinization | 糊化 | Irreversible swelling and disruption of starch granules in water + heat |
| 201 | Retrogradation | 老化/回生 | Recrystallization of gelatinized starch on cooling; causes staling |
| 202 | Pasting temperature | 糊化温度 | Temperature at which starch granules begin to swell (typically 58–65°C) |
| 203 | Peak viscosity | 峰值黏度 | Maximum viscosity reached during heating of starch slurry |
| 204 | Breakdown | 衰减 | Reduction in viscosity after peak; indicates starch granule fragility |
| 205 | Setback | 回生值 | Re-increase in viscosity on cooling; reflects retrogradation tendency |
| 206 | Swelling power | 膨胀力 | Capacity of starch granules to absorb water and swell |
| 207 | Solubility | 溶解性 | Percentage of starch that dissolves in water under defined conditions |
| 208 | Dietary fiber | 膳食纤维 | Indigestible plant material (~5–10% dry weight in potato) |
| 209 | Protein content | 蛋白质含量 | Typically 6–10% of dry matter; includes patatin storage protein |
| 210 | Patatin | 马铃薯糖蛋白 | Major storage protein in potato (40% of soluble protein) |
| 211 | Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) | 维生素C | Naturally present in potato (~10–20 mg/100g fresh); significant nutrient |
| 212 | Potassium (K) | 钾 | Major mineral in potato (~400 mg/100g fresh weight) |
| 213 | Glycemic index (GI) | 血糖生成指数 | Measure of carbohydrate impact on blood glucose; depends on processing |
| 214 | Solanine | 茄碱 | Toxic glycoalkaloid; increased by greening, light exposure (>200 mg/kg toxic) |
| 215 | Chaconine | 查茄碱 | Second major glycoalkaloid in potato; acts in concert with solanine |
| 216 | Polyphenol oxidase (PPO) | 多酚氧化酶 | Enzyme causing browning; must be inactivated by blanching |
| 217 | Peroxidase (POD) | 过氧化物酶 | Indicator enzyme for blanching adequacy; must be <5% residual activity |
| 218 | Lipoxygenase | 脂肪氧化物酶 | Enzyme involved in off-flavor development from lipid oxidation |

---

## 11. End-Use Applications (终端应用)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 219 | Reconstituted mash | 复水马铃薯泥 | Mashed potato prepared from flakes by adding water/milk |
| 220 | Water-to-flake ratio | 水粉比 | Typical 3.5:1 to 6:1 by weight depending on recipe and texture desired |
| 221 | Dough conditioning | 面团调理 | Use of potato flakes in bread/bun dough for softness and moisture |
| 222 | Extrusion cooking | 挤压蒸煮 | High-temperature, short-time processing for snacks and pellets |
| 223 | Battering / Coating | 挂糊/裹粉 | Applying potato flake batter to fried products (chicken, fish) |
| 224 | Binding agent | 粘结剂 | Functional property of potato starch to bind ingredients together |
| 225 | Thickening agent | 增稠剂 | Potato starch's ability to increase viscosity of liquids/sauces |
| 226 | Meat extender | 肉类填充剂 | Potato flakes used to extend meat products (nuggets, patties) |
| 227 | Clean label | 清洁标签 | Consumer preference for minimally processed, recognizable ingredients |
| 228 | Mouthfeel | 口感 | Sensory property of texture and coating in the mouth |
| 229 | Refrigerated dough | 冷藏面团 | Prepared dough stored at chilled temperatures for later baking |
| 230 | Par-fried product | 预炸产品 | Partially fried then frozen (e.g., fries); final frying at point of consumption |

---

## 12. Miscellaneous (其他)

| # | English Term | Chinese Term | Definition / Notes |
|---|-------------|-------------|-------------------|
| 231 | Integrated supply chain | 全产业链整合 | Vertically integrated operations from seed to finished product |
| 232 | Contract farming | 订单农业 | Pre-agreed purchase of farmer's crop at fixed/negotiated terms |
| 233 | Yield | 得率/收率 | Process efficiency: kg finished product per kg raw material |
| 234 | Throughput | 产能/处理量 | Processing capacity per unit time (kg/hr or MT/day) |
| 235 | Grade yield | 等级收率 | Percentage of output meeting premium/standard/economy specifications |
| 236 | Boll-off rate | 蒸发速率 | Rate of water removal during drying (kg H₂O/hr/m²) |
| 237 | Energy efficiency (SER) | 能源消耗率 | Specific Energy Requirement: GJ per tonne of product |
| 238 | CIP (Clean-in-Place) | 原位清洁 | Automated cleaning of processing equipment without disassembly |
| 239 | COP (Clean-out-of-Place) | 拆卸清洁 | Manual cleaning requiring equipment disassembly |
| 240 | Rework / Reprocessing | 返工 | Blending off-spec product back into production at controlled rate |
| 241 | Scrap / Waste | 废料/损耗 | Non-recoverable material loss during processing |
| 242 | Desiccant | 干燥剂/吸湿剂 | Substance that absorbs moisture; used in container protection |
| 243 | Dew point | 露点 | Temperature at which water vapor condenses; critical for container shipping |
| 244 | Container sweat | 集装箱出汗 | Condensation inside container in transit; risk of product damage |
| 245 | Marine insurance average | 海损 | Partial/total loss or damage during sea transit |
| 246 | Force majeure | 不可抗力 | Unforeseeable circumstances preventing contract fulfillment |
| 247 | SGS / Bureau Veritas / Intertek | SGS/必维/天祥 | Third-party inspection and testing companies commonly used in trade |
| 248 | NDA (Non-Disclosure Agreement) | 保密协议 | Legal agreement to protect proprietary information |
| 249 | MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) | 最小起订量 | Smallest quantity supplier is willing to accept per order |
| 250 | Lead time | 交货期/生产周期 | Time from order confirmation to readiness for shipment |

---

<!--- DOCUMENT: domain-07-industry-knowledge/faq-b2b-potato-ingredient-sourcing.md --->

# FAQ: B2B Industrial Sourcing of Potato Flakes & Powder

**Document Type:** Buyer's FAQ Reference  
**Target Audience:** Procurement managers, sourcing specialists, R&D directors  
**Last Updated:** July 2026  
**Reference:** Hongji Agriculture (弘基农业) — hjpotatoflakes.com

---

## 1. Introduction

This FAQ document addresses 21 of the most commonly asked questions from B2B buyers sourcing potato flakes and potato powder for industrial food manufacturing. It covers product specifications, quality control, ordering processes, logistics, and certifications — providing clear, data-backed answers to support procurement decision-making.

---

## 2. Product & Specifications

### Q1: What are the key product grades available for potato flakes?

Potato flakes are generally classified into three commercial grades:

| Grade | Moisture | Starch Damage | Typical Applications | Price Premium |
|-------|:--------:|:-------------:|---------------------|:-------------:|
| **Premium Food Grade** | ≤6.5% | ≤8% | Baby food, premium bakery, fine pastry | +15–25% over standard |
| **Standard Food Grade** | ≤7.5% | ≤12% | Industrial baking, snacks, sauces, meat binding | Baseline |
| **Economy / Animal Feed** | ≤8.5% | ≤18% | Pet food, animal feed | –10–20% vs standard |

Additionally, potato powder (milled from whole flakes or directly processed) is available in various particle sizes, from 80 mesh (coarse) to 200 mesh (fine).

---

### Q2: What is the difference between potato flakes and potato powder?

| Parameter | Potato Flakes | Potato Powder |
|-----------|:-------------:|:-------------:|
| **Particle form** | Thin, irregular flakes (typically 1–5 mm) | Fine, granular powder (80–200 mesh) |
| **Typical particle size** | ≥95% through 850 μm (20 mesh) | ≥90% through 150 μm (100 mesh) |
| **Reconstitution behavior** | Quick rehydration, fluffy mash | Requires mixing, can be lumpy if not agglomerated |
| **Best application** | Mashed potatoes, dough conditioning | Dry mixes, soups, batters, coatings |
| **Bulk density** | 350–450 g/L | 500–700 g/L |
| **Shelf life** | 12–24 months (standard packaging) | 18–24 months (standard packaging) |

> **Tip:** Hongji Agriculture offers both product forms from the same integrated production line, ensuring consistent raw material quality between flake and powder products.

---

### Q3: Which potato varieties do you process, and why does variety matter?

Hongji Agriculture processes three primary varieties, each selected for specific functional properties:

| Variety | Dry Matter | Reducing Sugars | Best Used For | Growing Region |
|---------|:----------:|:---------------:|---------------|:-------------:|
| **Atlantic** | 20–23% | Very low (<0.05%) | Premium flakes, light color → baby/pastry | Zhangjiakou, Hebei |
| **Shepody** | 19–22% | Low (0.03–0.08%) | Standard food-grade flakes, frozen fry blending | Zhangjiakou, Hebei |
| **Russet Burbank** | 21–24% | Low (0.05–0.10%) | High-starch flakes for industrial binding, meat processing | Zhangjiakou, Hebei |

Variety selection directly impacts the finished flake's color (Hunter L value), starch damage sensitivity, and final viscosity characteristics.

---

### Q4: What is the typical shelf life, and what factors affect it?

**Standard declared shelf life:**
- Standard packaging (PE-lined paper bags): **12 months** from production date
- Aluminum foil laminated packaging: **18–24 months**
- Vacuum/Modified Atmosphere packaging: **up to 24 months**

**Factors affecting shelf life:**
1. **Storage temperature** — ideal: 15–25°C; avoid >35°C (accelerates browning)
2. **Relative humidity** — storage environment must maintain <60% RH
3. **Packaging integrity** — any breach allows moisture ingress; moisture >8% triggers quality degradation
4. **Light exposure** — UV light accelerates lipid oxidation and discoloration
5. **Oxygen exposure** — headspace oxygen causes oxidative rancidity in residual lipids

---

### Q5: Can you provide specific nutritional data for your potato flakes?

**Typical nutritional profile per 100g (potato flakes, dry basis):**

| Component | Value | Unit |
|-----------|:-----:|:----:|
| Energy | 355–370 | kcal |
| Protein | 6.0–8.5 | g |
| Total Carbohydrates | 80–85 | g |
| of which Starch | 70–78 | g |
| of which Sugars | 1.5–3.5 | g |
| Dietary Fiber | 5.0–8.0 | g |
| Total Fat | 0.2–0.8 | g |
| of which Saturated Fat | <0.1 | g |
| Moisture | 5.5–7.5 | g |
| Ash | 2.5–4.0 | g |
| Potassium | 350–550 | mg |
| Vitamin C | 5–15 | mg |
| Sodium | 10–50 | mg |

*Values may vary ±15% depending on growing season and variety.*

---

## 3. Quality Control & Testing

### Q6: What quality testing do you perform on each production batch?

Each production batch undergoes the following standard tests:

**Physical / Chemical (every batch):**
- Moisture content (oven method, 105°C, 2h)
- Color measurement (Hunter L, a, b)
- Particle size distribution (sieve analysis)
- Bulk density (Scott volumeter method)
- pH of reconstituted product (6.0–7.0 typical)
- Sulfur dioxide (SO₂) residual
- Protein (Kjeldahl N×6.25)

**Microbiological (every batch or daily composite):**
- Aerobic Plate Count
- Coliforms + E. coli
- Salmonella (absence in 25g)
- Yeast & Mold
- Staphylococcus aureus
- Bacillus cereus

**Periodic / Third-party (quarterly or per customer request):**
- Heavy metals (ICP-MS: Pb, As, Cd, Hg)
- Pesticide residues (GC-MS/MS multi-residue scan, 200+ compounds)
- Acrylamide
- GMO analysis (PCR-based)
- Full nutritional panel

---

### Q7: How do you ensure food safety throughout production?

Hongji Agriculture operates a **HACCP-based food safety management system** covering:

1. **Raw material control** — CCP-1: incoming potato inspection for mold, rot, foreign objects
2. **Washing and peeling** — CCP-2: effective soil and pesticide removal verification
3. **Blanching** — CCP-3: time/temperature monitoring for PPO inactivation
4. **Drying** — CCP-4: drum temperature and residence time control for moisture ≤7.0%
5. **Metal detection** — CCP-5: ferrous, non-ferrous, and stainless steel detection after final packaging
6. **Allergen control** — Dedicated production lines; potential allergens (milk, soy, wheat) handled through separate systems

**Certifications held:**
- ISO 22000:2018 (Food Safety Management System)
- FSSC 22000 (GFSI-recognized)
- HACCP Certificate
- Halal Certificate (authorized by recognized Islamic body)
- Kosher Certificate (upon request)

---

### Q8: Do you offer third-party inspection at origin?

Yes. We accept inspections by any of the following internationally recognized third-party agencies:

| Agency | Service | Typical Cost (per container) |
|--------|---------|:----------------------------:|
| **SGS** | Loading supervision, sampling, lab testing | $300–$600 |
| **Bureau Veritas (BV)** | Quality inspection, quantity check | $280–$550 |
| **Intertek** | Full QA inspection + lab analysis | $350–$650 |
| **TÜV Rheinland** | Food safety audit | $500–$1,000 (audit) |
| **UL / Eurofins** | Lab analysis only | $150–$400 |

The buyer bears the cost of third-party inspection unless otherwise agreed in the sales contract.

---

### Q9: What are the typical color specifications buyers request?

| Application | Minimum Hunter L Value | Target L Value | Maximum Dark Specks (/100g) |
|-------------|:---------------------:|:--------------:|:--------------------------:|
| Baby food & infant nutrition | 88 | 90–92 | ≤1 |
| Premium bakery | 85 | 88–90 | ≤3 |
| Standard industrial baking | 82 | 85–87 | ≤5 |
| Snack / coating | 80 | 83–85 | ≤8 |
| Animal feed / pet food | 75 | 78–80 | ≤15 |

---

### Q10: How do you handle non-conforming product or quality complaints?

Our quality complaint procedure follows these steps:

1. **Notification** — Buyer notifies within 7 days of receipt (14 days for hidden defects)
2. **Evidence collection** — Buyer provides photos, retained samples, and CoC (Certificate of Conformance)
3. **Joint investigation** — We review production batch records, HACCP logs, and retained samples
4. **Resolution options** (depending on findings):
   - Replacement shipment at our cost
   - Credit note for accepted non-conforming goods
   - Price adjustment for minor deviation
   - Return and refund (including freight)
5. **CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action)** — Root cause analysis report within 30 days; updated SOPs if systemic issue identified

**Historical complaint rate:** <0.5% of shipments (based on 2024–2025 data)

---

## 4. Ordering & Commercial Terms

### Q11: What is your Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ)?

| Product Form | Standard MOQ | FCL Equivalent | Less-than-FCL (LCL) |
|--------------|:-----------:|:--------------:|:-------------------:|
| Potato flakes (standard grade) | 10 MT | 26–28 MT (20' GP) | 2 MT minimum |
| Potato flakes (premium grade) | 5 MT | 26–28 MT (20' GP) | 2 MT minimum |
| Potato powder (standard) | 10 MT | 26–28 MT (20' GP) | 2 MT minimum |
| Potato powder (fine, 100+ mesh) | 5 MT | 22–26 MT (20' GP) | 1 MT minimum |
| Custom blend / specialty | Negotiable | — | Negotiable |

---

### Q12: What is the typical lead time?

| Order Type | Lead Time (from confirmed order) | Notes |
|------------|:-------------------------------:|-------|
| Stock product (standard grade) | 7–14 days | Subject to inventory availability |
| Production order (standard) | 15–25 days | For quantity ≥25 MT |
| Custom specification | 20–35 days | Additional time for process adjustment |
| Organic / certified product | 25–40 days | Batch segregation and certification paperwork |
| Trial sample (small quantity) | 3–7 days | Usually dispatched by courier (DHL/FedEx) |

---

### Q13: What payment terms are available?

| Payment Method | Common Terms | Risk Consideration |
|----------------|:------------:|:------------------:|
| **T/T (Telegraphic Transfer)** | 30% deposit + 70% against B/L copy | Standard for first-time buyers |
| **T/T (100% in advance)** | Full payment before shipment | Typically for small orders (<10 MT) |
| **L/C at sight** | Irrevocable, confirmed L/C | Most secure for both parties |
| **L/C usance (30–90 days)** | Deferred payment L/C | Requires established relationship |
| **D/P (Documents against Payment)** | Payment at sight of documents | Lower bank fees than L/C |
| **D/A (Documents against Acceptance)** | Payment at maturity after acceptance | Trust-based, for repeat customers only |

---

### Q14: What information do you need to prepare a formal quotation?

Please provide the following details:

1. **Product description** — Flakes or powder? Any specific particle size?
2. **Grade** — Standard, premium, organic?
3. **Quantity** — MT per order; container type (20'GP/40'HC/FCL/LCL)
4. **Packaging** — Standard (25 kg PP/PE bag) or custom packaging?
5. **Destination port** — For accurate freight calculation
6. **Incoterm** — Preferred term (FOB, CFR, CIF, DAP)
7. **Special requirements** — GMO-free certificate, organic, third-party inspection, specific antioxidant usage
8. **Payment terms** — Preferred method
9. **Any target price** — Helps us identify suitable grade options

---

### Q16: How is the product packaged for export?

**Standard export packaging for potato flakes:**

| Package Type | Net Weight | Dimensions (L×W×H, cm) | Units per 20'GP |
|-------------|:----------:|:----------------------:|:---------------:|
| Multi-wall kraft paper bag with PE liner | 25 kg | 50 × 38 × 14 | 1,040 bags (≈26 MT) |
| PP woven bag with PE liner | 25 kg | 50 × 38 × 14 | 1,040 bags (≈26 MT) |
| FIBC / Jumbo bag | 500 kg | 90 × 90 × 110 | 20 bags (≈10 MT per 20'GP) |
| FIBC / Jumbo bag | 1,000 kg | 100 × 100 × 120 | 26 bags (≈26 MT, tight fit) |
| Corrugated carton (retail pack) | 10 × 1 kg or 20 × 0.5 kg | 40 × 30 × 25 | Custom pallet configuration |

**Palletization:**
- 20 bags per pallet (500 kg/pallet net) for 25 kg bags
- 40 bags per pallet (1,000 kg/pallet net) for 25 kg bags (stacked 2-high)
- Pallet type: Heat-treated wood (ISPM 15 compliant), plastic, or disposable paper

|-------------|:-------:|:----:|:--------:|:---------:|
| Standard product sample | 500 g – 2 kg | Free | Buyer pays courier (DHL/FedEx) | 3–5 working days |
| Custom spec sample | 2–5 kg | Free | Shared cost | 7–14 working days |
| Organic sample | 1 kg | Free | Buyer pays courier | 7–10 working days |
| Production-matching sample (from actual production lot) | 5 kg | Free + sample deposit | Buyer pays courier | Based on production schedule |

> **Note:** A sample deposit may be required for first-time sample requests (refundable upon order placement). This is to ensure serious inquiry handling.

---

### Q19: Do you accept factory audits from buyers?

We warmly welcome factory audits. Our facility in Zhangjiakou, Hebei Province, offers:

- **Dedicated bilingual QA team** to guide audits
- **Full transparency** on production records, HACCP logs, traceability, and supplier management
- **Virtual audit option** (video walkthrough + document review) as a preliminary step

**Recommended audit duration:** 1 full day (production + QA + warehouse + management review)

**Nearest airports:** Beijing Capital International Airport (PEK, ~3 hours by highway) / Beijing Daxing (PKX, ~3.5 hours). Hotel and ground transportation can be arranged upon request.

---

## 7. Commercial & Strategic

### Q20: What makes Hongji Agriculture different from other Chinese suppliers?

| Factor | Hongji Agriculture | Typical Small/Medium Chinese Processor |
|--------|-------------------|---------------------------------------|
| **Vertical integration** | Seed breeding → farming → processing → export | Usually processing only; raw material purchased on open market |
| **Capacity** | 60,000 MT/year | 3,000–15,000 MT/year |
| **GFSI certification** | FSSC 22000 certified | Many uncertified or only ISO 9001 |
| **Variety control** | Atlantic, Shepody, Russet Burbank — named contracts | Mixed varieties; supplier cannot guarantee variety |
| **International team** | Bilingual export staff; 15+ markets served | Limited English capability; reliant on trading companies |
| **Traceability** | Full batch trace from seed lot → field → process → shipment | Basic traceability only |
| **R&D capability** | In-house lab + collaboration with agricultural universities | Limited testing capacity |

---

### Q21: What is the best strategy for testing potato flakes in my application before committing to a full container?

**Recommended evaluation roadmap:**

**Stage 1 — Desk evaluation (Week 1)**
- Request full specification sheets (CoA format)
- Share your product's target application parameters (desired viscosity, color, particle size)
- Discuss formulation compatibility with our technical team

**Stage 2 — Sample testing (Week 2–3)**
- Request 1–2 kg free sample for lab-scale trials
- Test in your specific application (bakery, extruder, coating line, etc.)
- Compare side-by-side with current supplier using same formulation

**Stage 3 — Pilot order (Week 4–6)**
- Place a 2–5 MT trial order (LCL or consolidation)
- Run full-scale production test in your plant
- Evaluate on: processability, yield, organoleptic quality, consistency

**Stage 4 — Commercial order (Week 6–8)**
- Negotiate contract terms based on pilot results
- Order first FCL (26–28 MT)
- Establish regular quality monitoring protocol with agreed CoA parameters

---

- **Location:** Zhangjiakou, Hebei Province, China
- **Certifications:** ISO 22000, FSSC 22000, HACCP, Halal, Kosher, Organic (EU/USDA)
